10329:
<color=#FFC861>Details:</color>
1. Kingdom Number: Each Kingdom has a unique number, which can be used for quick identification and search.

2. Ruling Guild: The guild that seizes the Base in the War for Wonders will become the ruling guild of the Kingdom.

3. Overlord/Conqueror: If the ruling guild is from the same Kingdom, the guild leader will become the Overlord. If the ruling guild is from a different Kingdom, the guild leader will become the Conqueror.

4.<color=#FFEE9E>Protection period</color>: The Kingdom is migration restricted, and cannot participate in KvK events within 20 days of its creation.

5.<color=#FFEE9E>Restriction period</color>: The Kingdom is migration restricted, but can participate in KvK events

6.<color=#FFEE9E>Partially Restricted</color>: Some but not all players can migrate to the Kingdom.

7.<color=#FFEE9E>Migration Restricted</color>: Indicates that you cannot migrate to this Kingdom
10330:
<color=#FFC861>Migration Restrictions:</color>
1. To migrate to a Protected or Restricted Kingdom, the Kingdom you're in must be younger than the target Kingdom.

2. To migrate to a [Partially Restricted 1] Kingdom, your account creation date cannot be older than the target Kingdom by 90 or more days.

3. To migrate to a [Partially Restricted 2] Kingdom, your account creation date cannot be older than the target Kingdom by 180 or more days.

4. To migrate to a [Partially Restricted 3] Kingdom, your account creation date cannot be older than the target Kingdom by 270 or more days.

5. To migrate to a [Partially Restricted 4] Kingdom, your account creation date cannot be older than the target Kingdom by 360 or more days.

6. To migrate to a [Partially Restricted 5] Kingdom, your account creation date cannot be older than the target Kingdom by 450 or more days.

7. You cannot migrate to a Kingdom which displays the [Migration Restricted] message.
10331:
<color=#FFC861>Migration:</color>
1. Before reaching Castle Lv 6, players can use [Newbie Relocators] to migrate to any Kingdom. Migration Scrolls are required for migration after reaching Castle Lv 6.

2. The number of [Migration Scrolls] required is based on your Player Rank in the target Kingdom. The higher your Might ranking, the more Scrolls are required.

3. How to migrate: Select the target Kingdom, then select an empty tile within that Kingdom. Tap on it, and select the [Migrate] button to start the migration process.

4. Please take note of the rules and requirements for migration.

3. Underaged users that are <color=#ffff00>16</color> years old and above cannot make a single purchase of over 100 RMB, and their total monthly purchases cannot exceed 400 RMB.

Please schedule your gaming time wisely, and lead a healthy lifestyle.

11426:
Powerful lords gather from various Kingdoms to fight for the Royal Chalice!

<color=#FFC861>Basic Rules</color>
1. During the Chalice Battle, all Kingdoms (except protected Kingdoms) will engage in battle!
2. During the event, players with <color=#00FFFF>Lv 25 Castles can use a [Relocator] to enter the Royal Battleground</color>.
3. You must be in a guild to participate.
4. <color=#00FFFF>Wonders will be owned by the rally commander instead of the guild leader</color>.
5. <color=#00FFFF>The commander who occupies the Royal Chalice for the longest time will be proclaimed the victor and become the Paladin!</color>
6. You <color=#FF6278>may not be able to participate in the Chalice Battle if you migrate to another Kingdom</color>. Return to your starting Kingdom if you wish to continue with this event.
7. Earn Chalice Points and Battle Points by participating in Chalice Wonder battles.
8. Please check the Chalice Battle rules for more details on earning Chalice Points. Exchange Chalice Points for special items in the Chalice Treasury. Unused Chalice Points will not expire and can be used in the next event. <color=#00FFFF>You can only hold a maximum of 1 billion Chalice Points at any one time.</color>
9. Please check Chalice Journey in the Events Board for more details on earning Battle Points. Reach Battle Point requirements to earn checkpoint rewards. Battle Points obtained during the event will only be valid for the current event period. Unclaimed rewards will be sent to your mailbox after the end of the event.
10. Less troops will die in Wonder battles, as the ratio of Wounded troops sent to the Infirmary will be increased from 60% to 80%. However, <color=#00FFFF>troops killed in Wonder battles will not enter the Sanctuary.</color>
11. Leaders captured in the Royal Battleground during Wonder/Fort battles can only be <color=#00FFFF>imprisoned for 5 mins</color>. Items cannot be used to execute or escape. Bounty or ransom can't be set for these prisoners. All prisoners will be released once their captor leaves the Royal Battleground.
11428:
War has begun!
Only the strongest can become the [Paladin]

<color=#FFC861>Rules</color>
1. The Royal Chalice will be owned by the rally commander instead of the guild leader. 
2. The commander who occupies the Royal Chalice for the longest time will be proclaimed the victor and become the Emperor!

<color=#FFC861>About Chalice Points</color>
Earn Chalice Points by killing Tier 4 and above Troops in Chalice Wonder battles.
Kill 1 <color=#00FFFF>Tier 4</color> troop to earn 1 point.
Kill 1 <color=#00FFFF>Tier 5</color> troop to earn 2 points.
11429:
Lords together strong!
The Royal Chalice is no longer alone! Occupy Chalice Forts to earn Boosts and become the [Paladin]!

<color=#FFC861>Rules</color>
1. The Royal Chalice and Chalice Forts can <color=#FFFF00>only be occupied through rally attacks</color>; they cannot be attacked by a single army.
2. Rally times are shortened to <color=#FFFF00>2, 3, 4 or 5 minutes</color>
3. The player that initiated the rally will occupy the Royal Chalice, and whoever occupies it for the longest time will be proclaimed the [Paladin]!

<color=#FFC861>About Forts</color>
1. <color=#FFFF00>Numerous Forts will appear</color> in the Wonder's Province <color=#FFFF00>5 minutes before</color> the Chalice Battle starts.
2. Chalice Forts will be in <color=#00FF00>Protection Phase</color> when the round starts. They can only be attacked once Protection Phase ends.
3. Guilds must <color=#FFFF00>successfully defend the Fort for 1 minute</color> to occupy it.
4. The guild that occupies a Fort will <color=#FFFF00>allow all their allies in the battle to benefit from the Fort Boost</color>. The boost will disappear when the event ends or if the guild loses control of the Fort.
5. Once occupied, a Fort will enter <color=#00FFFF>Pacifist Phase for 5 minutes</color>. It can be attacked again after Pacifist Phase ends.
6. Players can start a rally to attack an occupied Fort when it is still in Pacifist Phase. However, if the Fort is still in Pacifist Phase when the rally reaches it, the rally will withdraw.

<color=#FFC861>About Chalice Points</color>
Earn Chalice Points by killing Tier 4 and above Troops in Royal Chalice or Chalice Fort battles.
Kill 1 <color=#00FFFF>Tier 4</color> troop to earn 1 point.
Kill 1 <color=#00FFFF>Tier 5</color> troop to earn 2 points.

12039:
1. You can get Dark Essences by destroying <color=#FFEE9E>Darknests</color>.
2. The Transmutation Lab can only store <color=#FFEE9E>a maximum of 3</color> Dark Essences; you will not be able to get any more if you already have 3. You can spend <color=#FFEE9E>Gold</color> to delete your Dark Essences.

12042:
1. You must <color=#FFEE9E>rally</color> to attack the Darknests, and the rally needs to have at least <color=#FFEE9E>2 members</color>.
2. Scout a Darknest to check its defenses. Each Darknest is guarded by a different array of troops.
3. Scouting and rallying against a Darknest will not deactivate your <color=#FFEE9E>Shield</color> or cause <color=#FFEE9E>Battle Fury</color> to be activated .
4. If you are victorious, the Darknest will be destroyed, and you will get <color=#FFEE9E>Dark Essences</color>. If you fail, the Darknest will immediately revert to its original state before your attack.
5. The Goddess Athena will <color=#FFEE9E>heal</color> a large portion of your troops that were injured or died during the attack on the Darknest.
6. Dark Essences can be transmuted in the <color=#FFEE9E>Transmutation Lab</color>.

12117: The Monsterhold is where you manage your Familiars. You can summon Familiars with Pacts, enhance them to the next growth stage, upgrade their Skills, awaken and level up their Army Talents. You can also shatter Runes here.
12118: Springs affect Anima production rate and storage. Production stops when storage is full. Build more or upgrade to increase efficiency and merging speed in the Mystic Spire.
12119: The Mystic Spire is where you get Pacts and Skillstones. Pacts can be used to summon Familiars (duplicate Familiars will turn into Runes). Skillstones are used to activate Familiar skills. Upgrade to increase the amount of items you can get in each batch.
12120: The Gym is where you can train your Familiars. Here, they can gain EXP and level up. Elder Familiars at max level can also gain Skill EXP. (Each Gym built will give 1 additional training slot).

<color=#FFEE9E>Inviting Friends</color>
• Invited friends that join and link their account will get the [Welcome Gift]
• You will receive an [Invitation Reward] when a friend you invited joins the game
• You get the [Comrade Reward] when your friend completes a Voyage Quest
<color=#FFEE9E>Conditions</color>
1. You can start inviting friends at Castle <color=#00ff00>Lv.7</color>
2. Your friend must be <color=#00ff00>downloading the game on their device for the first time</color> and <color=#00ff00>must register a new account</color>.
3. Your friend must use the <color=#00ff00>[Invite Link] to download the game within 24 hours and log in to the game</color>. Once they link their account, both of you will get rewards.
<color=#FFEE9E>Note: The steps in point 3 must all be done on the same device and network. We recommend that you download the game on a strong Wifi connection and not switch IP addresses. This will ensure that both you and your friend can be linked.</color>
1220: I'll decorate my shell with your bones!
12200:
<color=#FFEE9E>Rules</color>
1. You can have a maximum of <color=#00ff00>100</color> friends, but only <color=#00ff00>10</color> of them can be on your Allied Voyage at any one time.
2. You can invite friends using an invite link. Once they have linked their account and received the Welcome Gift, they can join your Allied Voyage if you have less than 10 friends on it. Friends you have invited will appear on the roadmap of your Allied Voyage, and you will receive an Invitation Reward.
3. Friends can only start Voyage Quests at Castle Lv 4, and they will only be available for <color=#00ff00>14 days</color>. You will receive a Comrade Reward after your invited friends complete a Voyage Quest and claim their reward.
4. Friends will disappear from the Allied Voyage if they are inactive. You can still collect any unclaimed Comrade Rewards.
5.Once you have reached the maximum amount of friends (10 in the Allied Quest, 100 friends invited in total), friends who use your invite link can still get the Welcome Gift. However, they cannot join your Allied Voyage, or show up on your Allied Voyage road map; you will also not get the Invitation Reward.

12242:
1. Each Lords Cup will have <color=#00FF00>4 matches</color>. Lords Balls will spawn at the start of each match, and players will have <color=#00FF00>120 minutes</color> to attack. Points accumulated from all 4 matches will go towards your final score.
2. Each troop tier has a different kicking skill, allowing them to kick the Lords Ball for different distances. You must have the required number of troops of that tier to use the skill.
3. Tap the Lords Ball on the Kingdom Map, hold down the skill icon, slide towards the direction you want, then release to send your troops marching. Once your troops reach the Lords Ball, they will use their skill to kick the ball in the specified direction.
4. When the Lords Ball lands, the 8 tiles around it (including the ball itself) will be regarded as the <color=#00FF00>active Lords Ball area (9 tiles in total)</color>. Points will be awarded if this area lands on the Base, a Fort, or the Wonder's Land. The Lords Ball will disappear after being kicked to one of these goals, and will respawn on the map. If this area lands on a Darknest, points will also be awarded, but it is the Darknest that will disappear and respawn instead.

12360:
<color=#FFC861>Gameplay</color>
1. In the Dragon Arena, guilds are paired for guild versus guild battles. To win, guilds must earn points by occupying Strongholds.
2. The Wonders in the Dragon Arena are named Strongholds, which include Outposts, Transporters, Hot Springs, Ancient Remains, Citadels, etc.. Each Stronghold grants a different amount of points when occupied, and some even grant special boosts. The Dragon Altar lies in the center, and serves as the starting point for Caravans. The Dragon Altar cannot be occupied.
3. You must <color=#FFFF00>defend a Stronghold for 3 consecutive minutes</color> to occupy it. Once occupied, you will earn Guild Points and Solo Points.
4. When a Caravan reaches a Stronghold, the side that has occupied the Stronghold will earn a large number of points.
12361:
5. You will not lose troops in this event. Wounded troops will be healed, damaged traps will be repaired, and your Wall will be restored once you leave the Dragon Arena.
6. After the round ends, the guild that has earned more points will be victorious. Both guilds will receive prizes based on the outcome.
7. Dragite won in the event can be exchanged for items in the Dragite Shop.
8. You will be issued Dragonwing Boots upon entering the Dragon Arena (the number you get depends your League). Dragonwing Boots can only be used in the Dragon Arena, and will be removed when you leave.
12362:
<color=#FFC861>Registration & Matching</color>
1. <color=#FFFF00>Guilds</color> must register to participate in this event. Only guilds with at least <color=#FFFF00>30 guild members with Castle Lv 17 and above</color> before the event starts are eligible.
2. Rank 4 and above members can register their guild and choose a preferred time slot for the event. This time slot can be changed at any time during the registration phase.
3. After registration, Rank 4 and above members can set the participant lists for Team A and Team B. Guilds will earn or lose Cups based on Team A's results.
4. Guilds may not get the time slot they selected during registration, and may be assigned to a different time slot. They will get a mail notifying them of the change.
5. Guilds will be paired according to their preferred time slot and the number of Cups they have. Successfully paired Guilds can enter the Dragon Arena during their assigned time slot. If a Guild has not set its participant lists, it will not be paired and cannot participate in the event.
6. From the start of the matching phase to the end of the event, guilds cannot change their participant list and cannot be disbanded.
12363:
<color=#FFC861>Leagues & Cups</color>
1. Guilds will be placed into Leagues in the Guild Showdown. New guilds will start from the Newbie League.
2. League progression hinges on the number of Cups a guild has.
3. Guilds will earn or lose Cups based on Team A's results in the Arena. The actual number of Cups will depend on the difference in Cups between the two battling guilds.
4. Guilds will remain in the same League (and will not earn or lose Cups) if they do not register to participate.
5. The system may pair guilds from different Leagues together. In this case, they will follow the <color=#FFFF00>lower League</color>'s rules.
6. In higher Leagues, there will be more Strongholds and Caravans, but fewer Transports.
12364:
<color=#FFC861>Participation Requirements</color>
1. Guilds must have at least <color=#FFFF00>30 guild members at Castle Lv 17 and above</color> to be eligible.
2. Once your guild has registered, Rank 4 and above members can set the participant lists for Team A and Team B. Only players on these lists can participate.
3. You can only represent the guild you are in <color=#FFEE9E>when the matching phase starts</color>. You will not be able to participate if you switch guilds during the event.
12365:
<color=#FFC861>Entry Requirements</color>
1. Must be at Castle <color=#FFFF00>Lv 17</color> and above
2. No wounded troops in the Infirmary
3. No damaged traps and at full Wall HP
4. Battle Fury must be inactive
5. No troops outside Turf
6. Each player can only enter the Dragon Arena once. You cannot re-enter if you leave midway through.
7. Guild members in the Dragon Arena cannot leave the guild or be kicked out of the guild.
12366:
<color=#FFC861>Leaving the Dragon Arena</color>
1. You will retain the points you earn in the Dragon Arena when you leave. However, you <color=#FFFF00>cannot re-enter the Dragon Arena</color>.
2. All Leaders caught in the Dragon Arena will be released when their captor leaves.
3. If your Leader is imprisoned when you leave the Dragon Arena midway, they will only be released when their imprisonment ends.
4. Troops wounded in the Dragon Arena will be healed once you leave.
5. Traps damaged in the Dragon Arena will be repaired once you leave. However, you cannot exceed your Trap Limit. If your damaged Traps exceed your Trap Limit, the higher tiered Traps will be repaired first. Remaining Traps will be placed under the Repair Traps section in your Wall, and will not be automatically repaired.
12367:
<color=#FFC861>Rewards</color>
1. After the battle ends, both sides will win rewards. The winning guild will get Victory Rewards, while the losing guild will get Consolation Rewards.
2. In the case of a tie, both guilds will get the Consolation Rewards. Cups can still be won or lost, and will be calculated the same way.
3. Rewards will be distributed after the event ends. You must stay in the <color=#FFFF00>guild you represented</color> to get your rewards.
<color=#FFFF00>Guild Rewards: All guild members will receive these rewards based on Team A's results</color>
<color=#FFFF00>Solo Rewards: Earn checkpoint rewards based on your team's results and by reaching checkpoints, which include Dragite. Dragite can be exchanged for items in the Dragite Shop</color>
4. If a guild does not get matched with an opponent, they will automatically win. Guild members that fulfill the participation requirements will get Guild Rewards.
12368: After entering the Dragon Arena, players will be assigned into the protected areas either at the top or the bottom
12369:
<color=#FFC861>Preparation Phase</color>
1. Cannot Transport or attack
2. Can view the Dragon Arena map and locate the Strongholds
3. Can set Tactics and Formation
12370:
<color=#FFC861>Battle Phase</color>
Phase 1: <color=#FFFF00>Outposts</color> and <color=#FFFF00>Transporters</color> can be occupied
Phase 2: <color=#FFFF00>Hot Springs</color>, <color=#FFFF00>Ancient Remains</color> and <color=#FFFF00>Citadels</color> can be occupied
Phase 3: <color=#FFFF00>Caravans</color> will start on a random route from the Dragon Altar.
12371:
<color=#FFC861>Leaving Phase</color>
1. All Strongholds will be put into Protection Phase. Traveling Caravans will vanish.
2. Players can check the event's battle status. The guild with the most points wins.
3. Players can leave the Dragon Arena themselves or wait to be returned.
12372:
<color=#FFC861>Stronghold Rules</color>
1. Guilds must <color=#FFFF00>defend</color> the Stronghold successfully for <color=#FFFF00>3 consecutive minutes</color> to occupy it.
2. Guilds will receive Guild Points for the first occupation of each Stronghold. Guild members who have sent their troops to the Stronghold will also get Solo Points.
3. After occupying a Stronghold, guilds will earn Guild and Solo Points per second.
4. Citadels, Hot Springs, Ancient Remains, Transporters, and Outposts are Strongholds. Arena layouts vary with each League.
5. Please refer to the [Points] tab for more details on each Stronghold and the points system.
12373:
<color=#FFC861>Caravan Rules</color>
1. Caravans will start their journey from the Dragon Altar. They will pass 2 or 3 Strongholds before reaching the Citadel.
2. When a Caravan reaches a Stronghold, the side that has occupied the Stronghold will earn points. If a Stronghold is currently being battled for, the Caravan will stop until a side emerges victorious.
3. The higher the League, the more Caravans available. Caravans will disappear once they reach the Citadel. No new Caravans will appear if the maximum number of Caravans are currently traveling.
4. Please refer to the [Points] tab for more details on Caravans and the points system.
12374:
<color=#FFC861>Dragon Arena Rules</color>
1. Troops will not be lost in the Arena, only wounded. <color=#FFFF00>There is no Infirmary capacity limit.</color>
2. Healing wounded troops and repairing damaged traps only takes time, and does not require any resources.
3. Wounded Tier 1 troops and damaged Tier 1 traps require some time to be healed/repaired.
4. Wounded troops will be healed, damaged traps will be repaired, and your Wall will be <color=#FFFF00>restored once you leave the Dragon Arena.</color>
5. Guild members cannot supply resources to each other in the Dragon Arena.
6. Players will be placed into a protected area when they enter the Dragon Arena. While in this area, they cannot be attacked, or initiate an attack. However, they can join a rally, and send reinforcements to Strongholds.
7. First Transport in the Dragon Arena is free and does not require any items.
8. Higher Leagues have a Transport Limit. This is shared within a guild. Guilds can earn more Transports by occupying Transporters, but cannot exceed the max Transports available.
12375:
9. Certain Familiar skills cannot be used in the Dragon Arena.
10. You will not earn resources, Player EXP, or Hero EXP from attacking players in the Dragon Arena.
11. Leaders can only be imprisoned for 5 minutes in the Dragon Arena. Items cannot be used to execute prisoners or escape from Prison. Bounty or ransom can't be set for these prisoners. Prisoners will be released once their captor leaves the Dragon Arena.
12. Rally times are shorter in the Dragon Arena.
13. Actions in the Dragon Arena will not be included in profile or Guild Fest statistics.
14. After entering the Dragon Arena, active Shields will be temporarily deactivated. Shields will be reactivated with their previous remaining time after leaving the Dragon Arena.

12524:
<color=#FFFF00>About Maintenance Shields</color>
1. Maintenance Shields are activated automatically, but ONLY during select maintenance periods.
2. The maintenance notice displayed when logging in will specify if a Maintenance Shield will be activated.

<color=#FFFF00>Maintenance Shield Duration</color>
1. Each Maintenance Shield may last for different durations. The exact duration will be detailed on the maintenance notice.
2. If you log in to the game while your Maintenance Shield is still active, it will remain active for 5 minutes after. If you log in when there is less than 5 minutes left on its active duration, your Maintenance Shield will expire when the duration ends.
3. If your Turf is located in the Wonder's Province, your Maintenance Shield will be deactivated IMMEDIATELY once you log in to the game.
4. Your Maintenance Shield will be lost if you deploy any troops and/or Heroes while it is still active.

<color=#FFFF00>Maintenance Shield Effects</color>
1. Captured Leaders will not be released when the Maintenance Shield is active.
2. When the Maintenance Shield is activated, armies outside your Turf (armies engaging in military action, traveling troops, gathering troops) will be returned to your Turf (does not include troops in Camps, Darknest Rallies, Hunting Party, Supply Troops, Adventure Quests, or escaping Leaders).
3. You cannot receive Reinforcements or Garrisoned Troops while the Maintenance Shield is active.
4. The Maintenance Shield will not overwrite or otherwise affect any of your active Shields.
12525: You can't attack or scout a Turf protected by a Maintenance Shield.
12526: You can't send reinforcements or Garrisoned Troops to a Turf protected by a Maintenance Shield.

<color=#FFEE9E>Type: Verge Towers</color>
Verge Towers cannot be moved, but have a long attack range. Can be used for cover, and main line of defense.
12744:
<color=#FFEE9E>Type: Verge Soldiers</color>
Verge Soldiers will attack in groups and can be summoned repeatedly. They are practical battle units.
12745:
<color=#FFEE9E>Type: Verge Skills</color>
Verge Skills can be summoned outside your current territory, but they have a long cooldown time and will take time to cast. Use Verge Skills at the right moment for amazing results!
12746:
<color=#FFEE9E>Type: Verge Officers</color>
Verge Officers are special units that can unleash powerful attack skills. There can only be 1 of each Officer at a time. Officers can be summoned again after they fall.

3. Guilds that <color=#FFFF00>complete all three phases</color> will advance to the next level in the next Guild Bash.

Dear player, your Player Name did not meet our regulations, and we were forced to change it! We have given you a [Name-Changer]; you can use this item to choose a new name.

How to change your Player Name: Tap on your Leader's portrait at the top left, select the quill & parchment icon next to your name, select Change Name, then use the [Name-Changer] to change your name.

Please refrain from using names that are offensive or inappropriate. Repeat offenders may have their accounts banned.

Get Monster Crests by earning points in Guild Bash challenges. Monster Crests can be used to summon monsters.
14508:

Monsters will disappear if they are not killed within the specified time limit.
14509:

Guilds will receive a Summon Bonus (found in Guild Gifts) when they successfully summon a monster.
14510:

14524: Guild members unite to summon Guild Monsters. Successful challenges reward the whole Guild!
14525:
<color=#FFC861>Basic Rules</color>
1. Complete event quests to earn points. When the total points of the entire Guild reach the specified score, <color=#00FF00>Monster Crests</color> will be obtained.
2. Monster Crests can only be used by <color=#FFFF00>Guild Rank 4 or higher members</color> to summon Guild Monsters that grant generous rewards
. 3. After the Guild Monsters are summoned, they will appear on the Kingdom Map, and all members of the Kingdom can go to hunt them.

<color=#FFC861>Rewards</color>
1. Summon Bonus: The Guild that summons the Guild Monster will receive a <color=#00FF00>Summon Bonus</color>.
2. Slayer Loot: The Guild that kills the Guild Monsters will receive <color=#00FF00>Slayer Loot</color>.
 Both the Summon Bonus and Slayer Loot can be opened in Guild Gifts.
14526:
<color=#FFC861>Other Special Rules</color>
1. For points obtained from "Darknest" Quests, <color=#00FF00>50%</color> will be received by the <color=#FFFF00>Rally Captain</color>, while the other <color=#00FF00>50%</color> will be distributed based on the <color=#00FF00>Kill Count ratio</color> of all <color=#FFFF00>Rally participants (including the captain)</color>.
2. You will not be able to participate in this event again in another Guild after receiving the Summon Bonus or Slayer Loot.
3. If you migrate to a Kingdom that does not have this event during the event period, you will lose your current event participation eligibility.
14528:
Rally Guildmates to attack Guild Monsters and reap bountiful rewards! 

1. <color=#FFFF00>Guild Rank 4 or higher members</color> can use Monster Crests to summon Guild Monsters.
2. After summoning, the Guild will receive a <color=#00FF00>Summon Bonus</color>, which can be opened in Guild Gifts.
3. When Guild Monsters are summoned, they will appear on the Kingdom Map, and everyone in the Kingdom can hunt the Monster. The Guild that lands the killing blow will receive <color=#00FF00>Slayer Loot</color>.
4. Consecutive bonuses do not trigger when hunting Guild Monsters, and the effects of [Familiar Skill: Hunting Ground] <color=#FF6278>will not be counted</color>.
5. Players will not gain any event points from attacking and/or killing Guild Monsters.

1. Your Adventure Level affects the quantity and grade of Adventure Quests. Higher quest grades will give better rewards.

Adventure Lv 2: Max <color=#FFEE9E>5</color> quests. Highest quest grade: <color=#5CCFF5>Rare</color>
Adventure Lv 3: Max <color=#FFEE9E>6</color> quests. Highest quest grade: <color=#5CCFF5>Rare</color>
Adventure Lv 4: Max <color=#FFEE9E>7</color> quests. Highest quest grade: <color=#C881FF>Epic</color>
Adventure Lv 5 and above: Max <color=#FFEE9E>8</color> quests. Highest quest grade: <color=#F5D94F>Legendary</color>
2. Some Adventure Quests may have time restrictions, and can only be completed in the <color=#FFEE9E>day</color> or at <color=#FFEE9E>night</color>. Check quest descriptions, Kingdom Map, and the Adventure Log page for such time restrictions.
3. Dark Army and Dark Fortress Adventure Quests will not deactivate Shields or activate Battle Fury. The Goddess will also <color=#FFEE9E>heal</color> most of the casualties from Dark Army and Dark Fortress battles.
4. Adventure Quests can only be done in your home Kingdom. If you are temporarily in another Kingdom for a special event, wait until you return to your home Kingdom to continue Adventure Quests.
5. Adventure Quest location may change if your location is changed (due to migration, relocation, event ending, etc.) or if you are too far away.

14752:
1. There will be <color=#00FF00>4 matches</color> in each event. Lords Balls will spawn at the start of each match. Players will have <color=#00FF00>5 hours and 55 minutes</color> to attack in the first 3 matches, and <color=#00FF00>5 hours and 30 minutes</color> to attack in the 4th and final match. Points accumulated from all 4 matches will go towards your final score.
2. Each troop tier has a different kicking skill, allowing them to kick the Lords Ball for different distances. You must have the required number of troops of that tier to use the skill.
3. Tap the Lords Ball on the Kingdom Map, hold down the skill icon, slide towards the direction you want, then release to send your troops marching. Once your troops reach the Lords Ball, they will use their skill to kick the ball in the specified direction.
4. When the Lords Ball lands, the 8 tiles around it (including the ball itself) will be regarded as the <color=#00FF00>active Lords Ball area (9 tiles in total)</color>. Points will be awarded if this area lands on the Base, a Fort, or the Wonder's Land. The Lords Ball will disappear after being kicked to one of these goals, and will respawn on the map. If this area lands on a Darknest in a rival Kingdom, points will also be awarded, but it is the Darknest that will disappear and respawn instead.
5. If the Lords Ball lands on an obstacle (Player Castle, Camp, Field, Rich Vein, Rocks, Woods, Ruins, Gem Lode, Monster, Lords Ball, Lake, Shore), it will be moved to the nearest empty tile (Grassland, Forest, Rock Pile, Magma Path, Lava Hill, Tundra, Wonder's Province) in the same direction.
6. Your troops will gain a <color=#00FF00>Travel Boost</color> when you kick the same ball consecutively.

1. Do not post any rumors that will upset the balance and stability of the game.
2. Do not post anything illegal or instigate others to engage in illegal activity.
3. Do not post any inappropriate or unethical content. 
4. Do not post anything related to politics or divisive ideologies.
5. Do not post advertisements of any kind.
6. Do not impersonate a GM, or lead others to believe you are a GM.
7. Do not post anything sexual, offensive, or violates the privacy of others.

<color=#FFEE9E>We reserve the right to remove any Overlord Message without prior notice, and to suspend the Overlord's ability to post Overlord Messages.</color>

14813:
1、本遊戲是一款玩法複雜的策略類游戲，適用於年滿16周歲及以上的用戶，建議未成年人在家長監護下使用遊戲產品。
2、本遊戲基於架空的故事背景和幻想世界觀，為喜愛策略類游戲的玩家提供豐富的遊戲體驗。遊戲畫面色彩鮮明、玩法自由多樣，擁有較為複雜的遊戲系統。遊戲主要玩法為多人實時在線競技，玩法基於較為複雜的思維判斷和操作，鼓勵玩家通過溝通、思考和團隊協作達成目標。遊戲中有基於文字的陌生人社交系統，但社交系統的管理遵循相關法律法規。
3、本遊戲中有用戶實名認證系統，用戶必須進行實名認證，否則將無法遊玩本遊戲，認證為成年人的用戶遊戲不受任何限制，認證為未成年人的用戶將接受以下管理：
遊戲中部分玩法和道具需要付費。未滿12周歲的用戶不能付費；12周歲以上未滿16周歲的未成年人用戶，單次充值金額不得超過50元人民幣，每月充值金額累計不得超過200元人民幣；16周歲以上的未成年人用戶，單次充值金額不得超過100元人民幣，每月充值金額累計不得超過400元人民幣。 
未成年用戶僅可在每週五、週六、週日和法定節假日每日20時至21時遊玩本遊戲，其他時間一律不能進行遊戲。
4、遊戲內玩家需要針對現有資源進行調配、以及合理規劃發展計劃、與盟友協作贏取勝利，以上玩法可以鍛煉玩家的思維能力、全局觀念以及團隊協作能力和溝通能力。

<color=#FFC861>Ranking Rules</color>
1. Players will compete against other players from different Kingdoms.
2. If two or more players have the same number of Artifact Chests opened, the one who obtained the amount first will be ranked ahead.
3. Players must open at least 10 Artifact Chests during the event for a chance to enter the rankings.

<color=#FFC861>Ranking Rewards</color>
After the event ends, the <color=#FFEE9E>top 1,000 players</color> will win Ranking Rewards. These rewards will be automatically sent to their Bag via a system mail.

14971: Wow. Death Knight sure has great "hospitality" towards his guests, huh?

14975: Man, these ruins sure are eerie. Hey! Who's there? Show yourselves!

15503:
<color=#004B97>[To regret is to live in the past.]－Joanna</color>
As a Cleric Hunter, Joanna's duty is to eliminate the beasts that plague our Kingdoms. Her white whale was a powerful ancient beast called Merald. After a long and exhausting journey, Joanna finally found its lair, but was in for a shock. The years had not been kind to Merald, and it had been reduced to a blind and feeble shadow of its former self.
Laying eyes on the once formidable beast, Joanna only felt pity. Her quest was now over. As she turned to leave, Merald called out and begged her to fight it. She decided to honor its last wish, and as her blades pierced its heart, a ray of light emerged from the wound and entered her right eye. Years of wisdom and power coursed through her, and everything... went black.
When Joanna awoke, she found that her skin had taken a greenish hue. Merald's powers had corrupted her into a half-Beast. She is now torn between her duty and her new existence...
15506:
<color=#004B97>[The bigger your cannon, the smaller your problems!] - Manfred</color>
Born on a small farm, Manfred chose to join the army at a young age to bolster his family's income. His talents in battle soon became apparent, and he was sent on an undercover mission to infiltrate a notorious pirate's crew.
Many years and numerous secret reports later, the pirates were finally wiped out. Unfortunately, the officers who sent Manfred undercover were all killed in the final battle, and Manfred was unable to prove his identity.
Tired of trying to clear his name, Manfred roams the land as a rogue soldier, fending off bounty hunters with his trusty cannon and living free.
15509:
<color=#004B97>[Every picture has a story!] - Lisa</color>
Dark heavy clouds lumbered across the horizon, as the wind strolled among the trees. The old lady dabbed her brush on the easel, carefully capturing the pregnant calm. As she painted, a small figure darted into her house, and she sighed. "You're late today. I thought you weren't coming."
"I'm here, aren't I? After all, I promised!" Lisa chirped cheerfully.
Theirs was a friendship forged through a mutual love of painting and stories. The old lady spent her last days sharing her many memories with her young friend, memories of amazing adventures that she had recorded with her brush strokes. After the old lady passed, Lisa decided to relive the stories she was told, and, as time passed, began making her own stories. She would use her paints to chronicle all that she sees, to pass on forevermore.
15512:
<color=#004B97>[Magma burns even fire!]－Vulcan</color>
The elementals live in a seperate realm, that only rarely collides with the physical realm. Items from the physical realm sometimes travel through these holes, and are treasured by the elementals, especially Vulcan. He is obsessed with the physical realm, and wishes he could join his friend, Rasmus, in residing there. During one of the collisions, he took his chance and jumped.
While Vulcan managed to pass through, he destroyed a performing circus' tent when he lands. To the ringmaster's surprise, Vulcan offers to join the circus to repay his debt. Despite his hulking size and strength, Vulcan is gentle and responsible, and has become the circus' new opening act: The Magmaroid.
15515:
<color=#004B97>[I've returned to claim my throne!]－Lionel</color>
A man of royal descent, Lionel claimed the throne was stolen from him, and managed to gather and raise a rebellion. However, he was no match for the true emperor. In the throes of defeat, Lionel implored Nox for help, but was slain before his pleas were answered.
Now, many years later, Lionel has been awoken by dark magic, and still harbors an aggressive determination to claim what he believes is rightfully his.
15518:
<color=#004B97>[Don't ever give up hope!]- Seiya</color>
A stubborn and naive youth who will charge recklessly into battle to protect his friends. He never gives up even against stronger foes, and always gives his all in battle.
He has fought his way through the 12 Gold Palaces, against Sea Emperor Poseidon, and even the Lord of the Underworld, Hades, but his courage and determination has never faltered.
Seiya has followed Athena to our Kingdoms and is ready to fight for justice, for the Goddess, and for you!
1552: Promoting Heroes raises their attributes. Collect Medals to promote Heroes!
15521:
<color=#004B97>"The sands taught me courage!" - Ilya</color>
Ilya was born with a sandstone clutched in her hand, fulfilling a prophecy of a child chosen by the Sand Spirit. As a child, she did not know how to control her own power, causing those around her to get hurt. No one would play with her, and she became withdrawn and fixated on the flowing sands. Her only friend was her twin sister, Marcia, who also had the power to control sand.
Marcia's life was put in danger when a group of sand bandits attacked the oasis. Ilya, who had always been protected by her older sister, suddenly unleashed her full power, creating a violent sandstorm that laid waste to the bandits, saving her sister and her village.
This incident gave Ilya a new purpose: to become a master of sand magic. With newfound determination, Ilya's quest for power began.
15524:
<color=#004B97>"A sand warrior never runs from a fight!" - Marcia</color>
Marcia and her younger twin sister Ilya possessed the power to control sand from a young age. Marcia was the courageous one, and was lauded as the strongest warrior in their village when she was only twelve. However, Ilya found it difficult to control this power, and became a recluse in fear of hurting others. Marcia stayed by Ilya's side, helping her master her powers, and became a pillar of strength and support to her sister.
When a group of sand bandits attacked, Marcia rushed to their village's defense, but was outnumbered and collapsed. She prayed to the Sand Spirit to give her strength, but instead bore witness to Ilya's power as her sister summoned a sandstorm, saving them all.
Though Marcia was proud of how much Ilya had grown, she also worried that her sister would lose control, so decided to accompany her on her journey.
15527:
<color=#004B97>"Even the biggest waves won't stop me!" - Austin</color>
Austin was born on a distant island, and grew up studying numerous fighting techniques. Eventually, he used his skills to defeat challengers from other tribes, thus emerging as the youngest champion at his coming of age ceremony. The elders presented him with the Guardian's Anchor and a tattoo of the waves symbolizing the God of the Sea's blessing, and acknowledged him as their new leader.
However, it wasn't all smooth sailing. With the island's resources growing scarce, many brave warriors were tempted by the legend of a promised land that lay beyond the sea, only to fall to the stormy waves. At last, Austin took it upon himself to accept this challenge. He realized that he needed to build a sturdy ship to weather the perilous journey, and so he began his quest.
After years of training rigorously and fending off pirates, Austin finally gathered enough materials to build the magnificent Wavefarer. He then set sail to lead his people to the promised land.
15530:
<color=#004B97>"How much are you willing to pay for your heart's desire?" - Kimiya</color>
Many would be surprised to learn that Kimiya was once the chief royal alchemist. With her extraordinary skills, there was no shortage of wealthy nobles offering her riches in exchange for her potions and alchemical wonders. However, a worry-free life of luxury soon turned mundane and predictable for Kimiya. She desired the freedom to create and experiment.
Thus, she made the bold decision to relinquish her position as the chief royal alchemist and embarked on a journey across the Kingdoms of Athena. During her travels, Kimiya collected local ingredients and used them to create medicines for the poor and needy. However, some of those whom she cured became greedy and begged her to grant them wealth, beauty, and eternal life. Whenever she hears these pleas, Kimiya can only smile and ask, "So, how much are you willing to pay?"
15533:
<color=#004B97>"Each flower that blossoms has a story to tell."－Rolanda</color>
Rolanda hails from an ancient tribe that lived on the Bountiful Plains. Blessed by the divine, she was bestowed with the power to control vines and plants around her. She could make grapevines bloom with beautiful flowers and command them to lower their branches, offering her the sweetest fruits.
Rolanda was held in high regard by her tribe elders, who had hopes of grooming her to become the future chieftain. But young Rolanda was mischievous and often skipped her lessons. Sometimes, she would even wrap her vines around the elders' chambers to stop them from going to teach her. Their frustrations each time were met with her sweet smile and flowers, which softened their hearts. They'd simply sigh and say, "Promise me you won't do that again."
However, the day before Rolanda was to participate in her coming-of-age ceremony, she pulled the same trick again. She sneaked out of the village to play on a swing made of flower vines. Before she knew it, the sun had set. She made her way back home, but her entire tribe seemed to have vanished, and a faint, dizzying scent filled the air. Rolanda crumpled to the ground in confusion and despaired for three days and three nights.
On the fourth morning, Rolanda finally stood up, gathered the books she had once neglected, and left. Thick vines rose up behind her, engulfing the place where her tribe had lived. "I'll bring everyone back one day. I promise." Years passed, and that sweet girl grew up to be a composed young lady who still never forgot her promise to find her people…
1554: Each Hero comes with unique Skills. Gather different Heroes to maximize your team's potential!
15542:
<color=#004B97>“By Light's path, by faith's edge.”</color>
Reyna's noble lineage carried an ancient covenant with the light elves—their blessing forged warriors worthy of the Holy Sword title. Her mother embodied this legacy: both the empire's greatest blade and her most demanding teacher.
"Talent without discipline is wasted potential," she would say, "Rules don't restrain—they refine." Through battlefield lessons and witnessing warriors consumed by their own power, Reyna learned restraint was the true measure of strength. She became the youngest Holy Sword in history, her skill matched only by her devotion.
Now, as her mother's strength wanes, Reyna sharpens her blade with renewed purpose—to uphold their family's honor, share the weight of leadership, and perhaps, one day, eclipse the legend who trained her.

16070:
<color=#FFC861>About Familiars:</color>
1. Use <color=#FFEE9E>Pacts</color> to summon familiars!
2. <color=#FFEE9E>Duplicate familiars</color> will be turned into <color=#FFEE9E>Runes</color>. You can use <color=#FFEE9E>Runes</color> to <color=#FFEE9E>enhance your Familiars</color> and <color=#FFEE9E>increase their skill levels</color>. <color=#FFEE9E>Shatter</color> extra Runes to make <color=#FFEE9E>Fragments</color>.
3. Familiars have 3 growth stages: Hatchling, Adult, and Elder. Enhance them to the next stage to increase their max level.
4. Your Familiar's level cannot exceed your (player) level.

<color=#FFC861>Familiar Skills:</color>
1. Each familiar has its unique skills and effects.
2. There are two types of skills: <color=#FFEE9E>Support skills</color> and <color=#FFEE9E>Attack skills</color>. To use support skills, tap the Familiar button on the right (in Turf or Kingdom Map). To use attack skills, tap on an enemy's Turf and select the Familiar button. <color=#FFEE9E>Familiar attack skills will not be blocked by Shields</color>.
3. Using attack skills <color=#00FF00>will not deactivate your Shield, or activate Battle Fury</color>.
4. Certain skills require Skillstones to activate.
5. Familiar effects do not require Skillstones; they are activated when you obtain the Familiar.

<color=#FFC861>Army Talents:</color>
1. Enhance a Familiar to <color=#FFEE9E>Elder stage to awaken their Army Talent</color>. Army Talents can be used when Familiars are deployed in battle.
2. <color=#FFEE9E>Research [Familiar Battle Slots] (requires Academy Lv 21)</color> to deploy awakened Familiars in battle.
3. Use <color=#00FF00>Talent Orbs</color> to awaken and level up Army Talents.

Only rally attacks can be used to battle for the District Wonder and District Forts
<color=#FF6278>※ Tap the [i] for detailed rules</color>
16444:
Powerful lords come together to vie for the prize!

<color=#FFC861>Basic Rules</color>
1. During the Feudal War, all Kingdoms (except protected Kingdoms) will engage in battle!
2. During the event, players with <color=#00FFFF>Lv 25 Castles can use a [Relocator] to enter the Royal Battleground</color>.
3. You must be in a guild to participate.
4. <color=#00FFFF>Wonders will be owned by the rally commander instead of the guild leader</color>.
5. <color=#00FFFF>The commander who occupies the District Wonder for the longest time will be proclaimed the victor and become the Baron!</color>
6. You <color=#FF6278>cannot participate in the Feudal War if you migrate to a Kingdom outside your District.</color> Return to your District if you wish to continue with this event.
7. Earn Feudal Points and Battle Points by participating in District Wonder battles.
8. Please check the Feudal War rules for more details on earning Feudal Points. Exchange Feudal Points for special items in the Feudal Treasury. Unused Feudal Points will not expire and can be used in the next event. <color=#00FFFF>You can only hold a maximum of 1 billion Feudal Points at any one time.</color>
9. Please check Feudal Journey in the Events Board for more details on earning Battle Points. Reach Battle Point requirements to earn checkpoint rewards. Battle Points obtained during the event will only be valid for the current event period. Unclaimed rewards will be sent to your mailbox after the end of the event.
10. Less troops will die in Wonder battles, as the ratio of Wounded troops sent to the Infirmary will be increased from 60% to 80%. However, <color=#00FFFF>troops killed in Wonder battles will not enter the Sanctuary.</color>
11. Leaders captured in the Royal Battleground during Wonder/Fort battles can only be <color=#00FFFF>imprisoned for 5 mins</color>. Items cannot be used to execute or escape. Bounty or ransom can't be set for these prisoners. All prisoners will be released once their captor leaves the Royal Battleground.
16446:
War has begun!
Only the strongest can become the [Baron]!

<color=#FFC861>Rules</color>
1. Wonders will be owned by the rally commander instead of the guild leader. 
2. The commander who occupies the District Wonder for the longest time will be proclaimed the victor and become the Baron!

<color=#FFC861>About Feudal Points</color>
Earn Feudal Points by killing Tier 4 and above Troops in District Wonder battles.
Kill 1 <color=#00FFFF>Tier 4</color> troop to earn 1 point.
Kill 1 <color=#00FFFF>Tier 5</color> troop to earn 2 points.
16448:
Lords together strong!
The District Wonder is no longer alone! Occupy District Forts to earn Boosts and become the Baron!

<color=#FFC861>Rules</color>
1. The District Wonder and Forts can <color=#FFFF00>only be occupied through rally attacks</color>; they cannot be attacked by a single army.
2. Rally times are shortened to <color=#FFFF00>2, 3, 4, or 5 minutes</color>
3. The player that initiated the rally will occupy the District Wonder, and whoever occupies the Wonder for the longest time will be proclaimed the [Baron]!

<color=#FFC861>About Forts</color>
1. <color=#FFFF00>Numerous Forts will appear</color> in the Wonder's Province <color=#FFFF00>5 minutes before</color> the Feudal Rally starts.
2. District Forts will be in <color=#00FF00>Protection Phase</color> when the round starts. They can only be attacked once Protection Phase ends.
3. Guilds must <color=#FFFF00>successfully defend the Fort for 1 minute</color> to occupy it.
4. The guild that occupies a Fort will <color=#FFFF00>allow all their allies in the battle to benefit from the Fort Boost</color>. The boost will disappear when the event ends or if the guild loses control of the Fort.
3. Once occupied, a Fort will enter <color=#00FFFF>Pacifist Phase for 5 minutes</color>. It can be attacked again after Pacifist Phase ends.
6. Players can start a rally to attack an occupied Fort when it is still in Pacifist Phase. However, if the Fort is still in Pacifist Phase when the rally reaches it, the rally will withdraw.

<color=#FFC861>About Feudal Points</color>
Earn Feudal Points by killing Tier 4 and above Troops in District Wonder or District Fort battles.
Kill 1 <color=#00FFFF>Tier 4</color> troop to earn 1 point.
Kill 1 <color=#00FFFF>Tier 5</color> troop to earn 2 points.

1. Guilds will be placed into Divisions in the Guild Showdown. New guilds will start from Division 1. <color=#FFFF00>Guilds in Divisions 1 - 6 will not be relegated.</color>
In Divisions 1 - 5, guilds that finish in the <color=#FFFF00>top 8</color> will advance to a higher Division
In Division 6, guilds in the <color=#FFFF00>top 4</color> will advance.
17043: 2. Guilds in Divisions 7 or higher <color=#FFFF00>will only advance to a higher Division if they are ranked in the top 4. Guilds in the bottom 4 will be relegated to a lower Division.</color>
17044: 3. The number of contestants required for each guild depends on the Division they are in. <color=#FFFF00>Guilds must have enough contestants to participate in the Showdown.</color>
17045: 4. Guilds will be <color=#FFFF00>relegated to a lower Division if they do not have enough contestants.</color>
17047: 1. You can only register if your <color=#FFFF00>Castle is Lv 5 or above</color>.
17048: 2. Upon registrations, your <color=#FFFF00>active Talents, Equipment, Army Lineup, Turf Boosts, and Might</color> will be recorded.
17049: 3. Your heroes and troops <color=#FFFF00>will not be wounded or killed</color>.
17050: 4. Once you submit your registration, you'll represent this guild. You can change your army lineup at any time during the registration period.
17052: 1. Each group will consist of 16 guilds. Guilds will be <color=#FFFF00>eliminated when they are defeated</color>. The guild that makes it through all 4 rounds will become the Showdown Champion!
17053: 2. If a guild's opponent is unavailable, they will win uncontested and advance to the next round.
17055: 1. Players will face contestants from their opponent guild. The <color=#FFFF00>weakest to strongest (Might)</color> members will do battle in turn. The winner of each battle will face their next opponent with <color=#FFFF00>their remaining army</color>. <color=#FFFF00>Each battle won = 1 event point.</color>
17056: 2. The guild that <color=#FFFF00>defeats all contestants in their opponent guild</color> will win and advance to the next round.
17057: 3. Players will start off <color=#FFFF00>each round</color> with their <color=#FFFF00>full army</color>.
17059: 1. <color=#FFFF00>Battle Fury will NOT be activated</color> by any actions related to the Guild Showdown.
17060: 2. Registered armies will not be included in the player's <color=#FFFF00>Army Limit</color>.
17061: 3. The player's Talents, Equipment, Turf Boosts, Army Lineup, and Might will be based on what is equipped / active upon <color=#FFFF00>registration</color>.
17062: 4. Boosts from Forts and Titles will be based on those active when the <color=#FFFF00>event starts</color>. They are <color=#FFFF00>NOT recorded upon registration</color>.
17064: 1. Registered participants can claim their rewards after the event ends.
17065: 2. Rankings will be based on total event points. If multiple guilds have the same points, the guild with the highest Might will win the tie.
17067: 1. You can view every match in the event Bracket.
17068: 2. <color=#FFFF00>Live Videos</color>: You can watch the battles live when matches are in progress!
17069: 3. <color=#FFFF00>Replay</color>: After the event ends, battle replays can be viewed on the Bracket.
17070: 5. If the number of registered contestants exceeds the requirements, <color=#FFFF00>players with higher Might will be selected ahead of others</color>.

17117: The Goddess Athena has bestowed a new blessing upon us: the ability to forge pacts with Monsters and make them our Familiars!
17118: The [Monsterhold] is where you manage your Familiars. Everything to do with Familiars can be found here!
17119: Build the [Spring], the [Mystic Spire], and the [Gym] to get more Familiars and make your Familiars stronger!
17120: Use the Anima produced in the [Spring] to make Pacts in the [Mystic Spire]. Take the Pacts and use them to summon your Familiar in the [Monsterhold], then send it to the [Gym] to make it stronger!
17121: You can make your Familiars stronger by helping them to level up, enhancing them, and improving their skills.
17122: All this requires the [Runes] and [Fragments] you get from your Pacts. The Gym will give your Familiars a big boost!

<color=#004B97>[O Goddess! Please lead us to victory!]－Bellena</color>
Bellena the Oracle is not your typical hero. Extremely reclusive, she spends days at a time praying and communing with the Goddess Athena. But she always appears to those in need, ready to lend a hand and guide them in the right direction.
Lately, Bellena has been unusually active, traveling from town to town with dire warnings of falling stars and creatures of pure flame. The Goddess has told her of a terrible force gathering in the shadow, and the Oracle must prepare the world for its coming.

<color=#004B97>[Need... more... WATER!] - S.A.M.</color>
Long ago, an evil warlock kidnapped a Dwarven smith and forced him to craft a robot soldier. Yielding to the torture, the smith created the Steam Activated Mechanoid, or S.A.M. for short. One day after a rather brutal streak of destruction, S.A.M. was refueled with some contaminated water. Instead of breaking down, the water miraculously caused the automaton to become self-aware. Immediately developing a conscience, S.A.M. turned on the warlock and rescued the Dwarven smith. The Dwarf brought it back to his home under the mountains, where S.A.M. replenishes itself with only the purest spring water.

Kauket is a highly respected Priestess in a cult that worships the Goddess of Darkness. However, this cunning sorceress' true loyalty is not to Nox, but to herself. Driven by the memories of her family's deaths at the hands of Nox's followers, Kauket has vowed to eliminate them all, no matter the cost to her soul. After years of training, she has risen up the ranks and grown complacent, unaware that the darkness is constantly feeding on her desire for revenge, and getting stronger....

1724:
<color=#004B97>[Don't wind me up!] - Astre</color>
Long ago, when the land was at peace, the clockwork dolls Astre and Etoile were the stars of the great North Circus, bringing joy and laughter to generations of boys and girls. But the war came, and people forgot how to laugh. Forgotten when the circus was abandoned, the scared and confused Astre came by chance into the possession of Anderson. The Sage worked to restore her, and even taught her some magic. When Astre was repaired, Anderson encouraged her to seek her own way in the world.
Travel far enough, and you may hear folk tell of a strange girl who wanders about the northern villages, constantly asking about a star.

Kassim grew up in a simple village that, year after year, was bled dry by hefty taxes. Tired of seeing his friends and neighbors suffer, he became an outlaw, robbing from the rich and giving to the poor. His notoriety increasing by the day, Kassim was soon branded as a criminal by the nobles, and a large bounty was placed on his head. 1730:
<color=#004B97>[Time for the hunters to be the hunted!] - Forrest</color>
 A dryad born in the mystical Woodlands, Forrest has always longed for adventure. However, he was forbidden to step outside his homeland due to the dangers lurking outside. One day, while playing in the groves, Forrest caught sight of a fearsome cyclops hot on the heels of an injured fawn. Desperate to save the young animal, Forrest fired a shot at the cyclops with his trusty slingshot, and hit the monster right in the eye. The grateful fawn stayed by his side, and after years of training, Forrest and Bucky - as he named the fawn - are a fearsome duo that will take out anyone who dares threaten their home.

1733:
<color=#004B97>[The Eye sees all.] - Har'Kon</color>
Deep in the Temple of the Eternal Eye, the little girl climbed the altar. She knelt before the smooth orb, took a breath, and began to chant, the familiar words rolling off her tongue. As the chant reached a crescendo, she reached out to touch the orb, just as she had seen her father do so many times. Too late she heard the warning, her fingers pressing on the orb as Har'Kon the high priest grabbed her arms. A swirl of color and noise engulfed the temple. When he came to, Har'Kon stumbled out of the ruins. The Eye has been awakened. He must find his daughter before it is too late.

17352: The Kingdom Gazette brings you the hottest news from your kingdom and the world!
1736:
<color=#004B97>[It's RAWWWWWW!!!] - Ramsay</color>
Of all the Mustelidae, Ramsay is the only one who prefers the kitchen to the battlefield. He spends his time cooking and experimenting at the stove, and his delightful creations have earned him much renown across the land. His own people, however, shunned him as an oddity and an outcast. But Ramsay showed his worth when the Muste Forests were invaded, his knowledge of heat and fire allowing him to use flamethrowers and explosives to devastating effect. His incendiary tactics turned the tide of the battle, and Ramsay the Mastercook was hailed as a hero.

17399:
Use Luck Tokens to play Kingdom Tycoon!

<color=#FFC861>Rules:</color>
1. You use <color=#FFEE9E>Luck Tokens</color> to advance on the Map.
2. Map tiles may reward you with <color=#FFEE9E>items or special effects</color>. You may even encounter a Gemming Gremlin!
3. The goal is to reach the <color=#FFEE9E>end of the map, where you will encounter a Gemming Gremlin</color>.
4. Encounter a Gemming Gremlin to nab Gems from its stash!
5. The map will be reset once you reach the end.
6. When the time runs out (top left corner), the map will reset with <color=#FFEE9E>new treasures</color> and you will be sent back to the beginning.
7. You'll get one free roll per day.

<color=#FFC861>Auto Roll</color>
1. You can tap <color=#FFEE9E>Auto Start</color> to automatically roll the dice after using your free roll. This will allow you to choose how many Luck Tokens you want to use to automatically continue rolling.
2. If you encounter the daily reset while auto rolling, your free roll will automatically be used.
3. If you reset the map while auto rolling, you will continue on the new map. Please note that the prizes on each map may vary.

1764:
<color=#004B97>[Everyone deserves a happily ever after.] - Thalia</color>
Born in the Depths, Thalia the faun knew only a life of slavery and toil. Despite the grim circumstances, she remained optimistic and yearned to be free. And freedom came calling when Thalia chanced upon a mysterious book of tales, a powerful relic that granted her the ability to bring stories to life. Armed with this magic, Thalia and the other prisoners fought their way to the surface. The liberated Thalia now travels the world, listening to new stories and writing her own tale. Will she have a happily ever after? Follow Thalia on her journey to find out!

17669:
Battle it out with your fellow Lords to prove yourself worthy of being the Kingdoms' Paragon!

<color=#FFC861>Rules</color>
1. Complete the designated tasks during the event to earn points. Accumulate Points to earn Point Rewards and get on the ranking boards.
2. There are 2 ranking boards - the <color=#FFEE9E>Kingdoms' Paragon Rankings</color> and the <color=#FFEE9E>Point Rankings</color>.
3. Point Rankings will feature a random set of <color=#FFEE9E>5</color> different tasks per event.
4. Each group will consist of <color=#FFEE9E>50</color> players at most. The top 3 players of each ranking board will win Crowns.
5. If two or more players have the same points, the one who obtained the points first will be ranked ahead.
6. After the event ends, players will receive Ranking Rewards based on their rankings. Rewards will be automatically sent via mail to the winners' accounts.

<color=#FFC861>Kingdoms' Paragon Rankings</color>
1. Win any number of Crowns on the Point Rankings to get on the Kingdoms' Paragon Rankings.
2. Players will be ranked by the number of Crowns they've won (<color=#00FF00>Gold＞Silver＞Bronze</color>).
3. If two or more players have the same number of Crowns, the player with the most ranking points will be ranked ahead.
1767:
<color=#004B97>[I'll make your blood run cold!] - Ursula</color>
For millenia, a primitive tribe of warriors has jealously guarded the Frozen Peaks, suffering none to pass. In the blistering cold and sharp crags, there is no room for weakness and charity, and the sickly Ursula was not expected to survive past childhood. But Ursula stubbornly kept going, year after year putting all of her time and effort into strengthening her body and sharpening her skills. At her coming of age ceremony, she proved herself by being the first in two centuries to claim a bear for her kill. The name of the Berserker is now feared in all the lands that fall under the shadow of the peaks.

17698: Revisit the past to collect precious memories.
1770:
<color=#004B97>[A bite... just one morsel...] - Fenrir</color>
Fenrir was once the leader of a dark cult, a group of men and women who worshipped the Goddess of Darkness. For their service, Nox transformed them into lycans, wolf-like creatures many times faster and stronger than humans. However, dark gifts come with a dark price: the bestial instincts of the lycans overpowered their humanity, and many of them turned feral. A horrified Fenrir was determined to lift the curse and save his brethren, but many years of effort turned out to be in vain. His humanity hanging by a thread, Fenrir is now driven both by his quest and an insatiable hunger, and will rip apart anyone who dares get in his way.

<color=#004B97>[I'll drown you in despair!] - Mizuki</color>
Mizuki is the sole survivor of a peaceful kitsune village that was destroyed in the war. The young kitsune was forced to fend for herself, though she almost met her end at the hands of a hunting party. Mizuki was ready to join her clan in death, but was rescued by a kind maiden who tended to her injuries. Fate was cruel, however, for the hunters had managed to track them down to the maiden's village. In their rage, they set the entire village ablaze and cut her savior down before her very eyes. Overcome with grief, Mizuki was finally able to display her true powers, summoning a thunderstorm to extinguish the fire and strike down her enemies. Mizuki has now taken on her savior's form, and uses her power to help those in need.

1776:
<color=#004B97>[Nothing will stand in my way!]－Gravios</color>
Gravios can still recall the splendor of the Dragon Kingdom, ruled by the majestic Draxido the Black. He can also recall the day the humans attacked, the great war that waged for a hundred years, and the final fall of Draxido.
The terrible days of hiding and wandering through the wilds with his sister Eloise, how they were separated by a band of knights, the long years of training and searching... these are all etched in his mind. When the fully grown Chaos Dragon made his way back to his homeland, it was nothing like he remembered. The great kingdom of the dragons was buried beneath the dirt, and the great Black Spear of Draxido sat in a museum, a trophy of the humans... 
With spear in hand, Gravios now seeks to reclaim the land of the dragons, and rebuild all that was lost.

1779:
<color=#004B97>[Now that was funny!]－Lilith</color>
Another letter arrived, spilling confetti on his floor and notifying him of Lake's demise by drowning. Not her best joke. She had enjoyed taunting him from the beginning, saving him for the very end. He remembered the young woman she used to be, the young woman he and his colleagues had experimented on. She was frightened then, pleading with him to end her suffering. But he ignored her, obsessed with thoughts of fame and fortune should the experiment succeed. The last time he saw her, her powers had manifested, and the terrified look in her eyes was replaced by maniacal glee and hatred as she tore through laboratory screaming knock knock jokes. 
“She's coming for me.” He was now the only one left. He wondered how she would do it, and hoped his death would at least make for a good punchline.

<color=#004B97>[I joust love you, milady!] - Alfonso</color>
Born of a noble family, Alfonso received the very best education aristocracy could demand. Loyalty, discipline, and class were drilled into him from birth, and he learned the sword and pistol from the kingdom's masters.
With age, however, came freedom from a stifling household and having to constantly mind his manners, and in Alfonso this freedom awoke a lascivious romantic. 
Kassim, the prince of thieves, sums him up succinctly "He looks like he is 40, thinks he is 30, f... flirts around like he is 20, and talks like he is 10. But I'll have him at my back any day."

1785:
<color=#004B97>[Let me light the way!]－Elora</color>
There was once a man, handsome and brave, who won the heart of a daughter of the forest. Their love was strong, and Man and Elf soon celebrated their union with a child. But the man soon left on an expedition. He never returned.
Elora had heard many tales of her father, how proud he would be of her, and how much she looked like him. She longed to meet him, to do more than see him through the eyes of others.
As she grew older, longing grew into hope, and Elora became convinced that her father was still alive, lost somewhere in the wide world. She searches for him, taking her light into the darkest corners of the world.

Shelley is a gnome who marches to her own beat. Instead of tinkering with machines, she spends her time on music, charming her audience wherever she goes.
Her magic flute helps bring her melodies to life, rejuvenating anyone who hears them.
Enamored by her tunes, Coral the snail offered to escort her on a quest to spread her music to every corner of the world. The two friends have never been seen apart since, earning Shelley the moniker of the Snail Princess.

<color=#FFEE9E>Devotion:</color>
Devotion will accumulate over time when you have troops in the Sanctuary. Use Devotion to resurrect your troops! You can also earn more Devotion by completing Divine Tasks.
18597:
<color=#FFEE9E>You can increase Devotion production by:</color>
<color=#FFEE9E>Healing all troops in the Infirmary.</color>
18598:

The amount of troops the Sanctuary can hold. Upgrade your Castle to hold more troops in your Sanctuary.

18617:
The Sanctuary takes in wandering souls, those who have fallen in battle but cannot enter the Infirmary. The Goddess blesses those with Devotion with the power to resurrect these poor souls.

<color=#FFC861>Rules:</color>
1. Some of your fallen troops that cannot enter the Infirmary when it is full will be directed to the Sanctuary.
2. Sanctuary effects (Sanctuary capacity, Devotion production speed, Devotion granted from Divine Tasks) will grow stronger as you increase your <color=#00FFFF>Castle level</color>.
3. <color=#00FFFF>Troops killed in Wonder battles will not be able to enter the Sanctuary</color>, e.g. <color=#00FFFF>Royal Wonder, Feudal Wonders, Royal Chalices, Bases, Forts, etc.</color>
4. When the Sanctuary is full, newly Wounded troops at a higher tier than those currently in the Sanctuary will be given priority.
18618:
<color=#FFC861>Divine Resurrection:</color>
1. Devotion will be produced when there are troops that require Divine Resurrection in the Sanctuary. Use Devotion to resurrect your troops.
2. More Devotion is required to resurrect higher tier troops.
3. <color=#00FFFF>Devotion production speed will slow down as the number of wounded troops in the Infirmary increases.</color>
18619:
<color=#FFC861>Divine Tasks:</color>
1. Divine Tasks will be available when there are troops that require Divine Resurrection in the Sanctuary. Complete Divine Tasks to earn Devotion.
2. Divine Tasks you have completed will reset daily, and can be completed again.
3. <color=#00FFFF>Gain more Devotion from Divine Tasks by increasing your Castle level.</color>
18620:
<color=#FFC861>Divine Providence:</color>
1. Troops in Divine Providence are blessed by the goddess and can be rescued with her power. However, this takes time. Claim troops in Divine Providence to add them to your army.
2. The goddess will rescue more troops when your Turf is engaged in a defensive battle.
3. The Sanctuary can only hold a limited number of Divine Providence Troops. If Divine Providence Troops are at full capacity, new troops will stop coming in. Remember to claim your troops ASAP!

2. Investments have a minimum and maximum limit. You can make investments in batches, <color=#FF6278>but if interest is available, you must claim the interest before investing more</color>.
3. After investing capital, you can start claiming interest once per day (after the day resets).
Day reset time: {0}
4. The number of times interest can be claimed varies according to each Stratagem. After all interest is collected, you will <color=#00FF00>get your capital back</color>.
5. Each Stratagem can only be completed once.
6. You will get your capital and interest back in Linked Gems.

18840:
This equipment is currently being tempered. Astralite will not be returned if dismantled.
Dismantle this equipment anyway?
18841:
1. <color=#ffff00>Astralite</color> can be used to temper Lv 50 and above [<color=#FF8328>Mythic</color>] equipment, upgrading its effects.
2. The number of Astralite required for tempering depends on the equipment and its tier level.
3. Some Equipment Sets have <color=#ffff00>specialized Astralite</color>, which can only be used to temper their matching Equipment Sets. Tempering will require more specialized Astralite than normal Astralite.
4. Tempering <color=#FFEE9E>Emperor Equipment and Exalted Equipment</color> requires <color=#ffff00>Monarch Astralite</color>.
5. The number of Astralite used will determine the success rate (max 100% success).
6. If tempering succeeds, the equipment's tier level will increase, increasing the equipment's effects. If tempering fails, the equipment's tier level will not change.
7. Astralite will be consumed regardless of success or failure.
8. Hunt <color=#ffff00>Astra Monsters</color> on the Kingdom Map to get specialized Astralite for their respective Equipment Set. Astra Monsters are more powerful than their normal counterparts, and are indicated by a blue glow.
9. Emperor equipment and time-limited equipment cannot currently be tempered.

18876:
Collect Lucky Cards to form an optimal set of cards, and exchange them for the amount listed as Gems!

1. Find <color=#FFEE9E>Lucky Card Chests</color> on the Kingdom Map and Scout them to Draw a Lucky Card.
2. Only 1 card can be drawn from each Lucky Card Chest.
3. Your Lucky Cards will automatically be sorted into the number slots in descending order.
4. When you are satisfied with the number combination, you can exchange them for the specified number of Gems. If you do not do so by the end of the event, you will automatically receive the Gems that would have been given by the combination you had at the end of the event.
<color=#FF6278>※Note: You can only exchange your cards once during the event. You cannot get any new Lucky Cards after making an exchange.</color>
5. Before you make your first exchange, you can unlock higher number slots by fulfilling certain conditions (tap on the locked slots to see the unlock condition)
6. After you've unlocked more number slots and exchanged your Cards for Gems, you will be able to purchase <color=#FFEE9E>one more exchange</color>
7. Gems acquired in this event will not count for the [Limited-Time: Full Charge Gift].

19865:
Prove yourself on the battlefield! Endure Trial by Fire stages to win amazing prizes!

<color=#FFC861>Rules:</color>
1. Trial By Fire stages are split based on the enemy's configuration. Once you clear a stage, you will win the stage's reward and move on to the next stage.
2. The daily clear limit is <color=#00FFFF>50</color> stages. Auto Battle will stop once you reach this limit or if you fail a stage.
3. You can unlock <color=#00FFFF>Quick Sweep</color> if your Might is strong enough to clear all available stages that day after clearing Stage <color=#00FFFF>10</color> of that same stage type.
4. Clear Infantry/Cavalry/Ranged Stage <color=#00FFFF>150</color> to unlock <color=#00FFFF>Mixed stages</color>.
5. Configurations set in Trial By Fire can <color=#00FFFF>only be used in Trial By Fire stages</color>.
6. You can use <color=#00FFFF>the highest troop tiers you've unlocked in Trial By Fire stages</color>.
7. When a Battalion is used in Trial by Fire stages, it will be formed based on the ratio of the Battalion's Soldier Type ratio, with troops of the highest tiers unlocked.
8. Each stage will have a Max Army size limit. <color=#00FFFF>You also cannot exceed your Max Army Size</color>.
9. In Trial By Fire defensive stages, <color=#00FFFF>the Wall and traps are automatically configured and cannot be adjusted</color>. Your Max Wall HP will be used, while the traps used will be of the highest tier you've unlocked.
10. Your Trial By Fire configurations will not be affected if your Leader is captured.
11. The following boosts are unavailable in Trial By Fire stages: Turf Boosts (except Battle Fury), Title Boosts, Wonder Stat Boosts, Prison Stat Boost when a Leader is imprisoned, Altar Stat Boosts, and the Familiar skill: Poison Sting.
12. Battle Fury boosts will become active in Trial By Fire stages when you reach Castle Lv 9. However, Battle Fury will not be activated in your Turf.

20580:
The <color=#FFEE9E>Artifact Hall</color> was established to preserve artifacts lost in the throes of war. Collect them to gain Artifact boosts and Might.

<color=#FFC861>Artifacts</color>
1. Artifacts can be obtained from the <color=#FFEE9E>Artifact Fair</color>. Collect enough <color=#FFEE9E>Artifact Shards</color> to <color=#FFEE9E>appraise</color> and obtain the Artifact.
2. Every Artifact has a set Grade of <color=#FFFF00>Legendary</color>, <color=#CE99FF>Epic</color>, or <color=#5CCFF5>Rare</color>. Higher Graded Artifacts will have stronger bonuses.
3. You can <color=#FFEE9E>upgrade or enhance Artifacts to increase their boosts and Might</color>.

<color=#FFC861>Upgrading Artifacts</color>
1. <color=#FFEE9E>Records</color> are used to increase an Artifact's level.
2. The max level an Artifact can reach is <color=#FFEE9E>Lv 12</color>.

<color=#FFC861>Enhancing Artifacts</color>
1. <color=#FFEE9E>Artifact Brushes</color> are used to enhance and increase an Artifact's <color=#FFEE9E>Stars</color>.
2. Enhancing an Artifact to <color=#FFEE9E>3-Star and above</color> requires <color=#FFEE9E>certain Artifact Shards</color> as well.
3. The max Star level an Artifact can reach is <color=#FFEE9E>5</color>. 5-Star Artifacts can also be <color=#FFEE9E>Blessed</color> to further improve their boosts.
4. Once an Artifact is enhanced to 5-Star, the Artifact's Shards can be exchanged for Generic Artifact Shards of the same Grade.
5. Duplicate 5-Star Artifact Shards obtained from the Artifact Fair will automatically be converted into Generic Artifact Shards of the same Grade.
6. You can also convert 5-Star Artifacts to Generic Artifact Shards of the same Grade while enhancing an Artifact.

<color=#FFC861>Artifact Sets</color>
Some Artifacts are part of a <color=#FFEE9E>set</color>. Collect the full set and reach the requirements to <color=#FFEE9E>get additional set bonuses</color>.

Dome Vault Challenge: Unlock a Treasure Trove of Rewards! 

<color=#FFEE9E>Event Rules:</color>
1. Insert a <color=#FFEE9E>Fate Coin</color> to spin the wheel, then tap <color=#FFEE9E>Stop</color> to halt the spin. The wheel will automatically come to a stop after a set time if you don't manually hit the stop button. 
2. You receive rewards based on the symbol combinations you land on (check the Rewards page for details), and your <color=#FFEE9E>Daily Collection</color> progress will be recorded! 
3. Tap <color=#FFEE9E>Multiplier</color> to switch your bet amount and reward multiplier. 
4. You receive a specific amount of points each time you insert a coin. each the point requirements to claim <color=#FFEE9E>checkpoint rewards</color>. 
5. Hold to activate <color=#FFEE9E>Auto Use</color>, which lets you spin and stop the wheel automatically. 

<color=#FFC861>Daily Collection</color>
1. Collect the specified symbols to unlock Daily Collection rewards. 
2. The Daily Collection features multiple quests. Complete and claim the reward for your current quest to unlock the next quest. 
3. Daily Collection quests reset each day according to the event schedule. Unclaimed rewards will be automatically sent to your Bag via a system mail. 

<color=#FFC861>Treasury Vault</color>
1. <color=#FFEE9E>Vault Card</color> can be exchanged for rewards in the [<color=#FFEE9E>Treasury Vault</color>]. 
2. Unused Vault Cards will be converted into Speed Up (3 m) items after the Treasury Vault closes.
