20929:
Are you ready to embark on a magical journey? Serendipitous Spins brings endless surprises, with plenty of prizes waiting for you to discover!

<color=#FFC861>Event Rules:</color>
1. Insert a <color=#FFEE9E>Fate Coin</color> to spin the wheel, then tap <color=#FFEE9E>Stop</color> to halt the spin. The wheel will automatically come to a stop after a set time if you don't manually hit the stop button.
2. You receive rewards based on the symbol combinations you land on (check the Rewards page for details), and your <color=#FFEE9E>Daily Collection</color> progress will be recorded!
3. Tap <color=#FFEE9E>Multiplier</color> to switch your bet amount and reward multiplier.
4. You receive a specific amount of points each time you insert a coin. each the point requirements to claim <color=#FFEE9E>checkpoint rewards</color>.
5. Hold to activate <color=#FFEE9E>Auto Use</color>, which lets you spin and stop the wheel automatically.

<color=#FFC861>Daily Collection</color>
1. Collect the required amount of symbols to unlock Daily Collection rewards.
2. The Daily Collection features multiple phases. Complete and claim the reward for your current phase to unlock the next phase.
3. Daily Collection quests reset each day according to the event schedule. Unclaimed rewards will be automatically sent to your Bag via a system mail.

<color=#FFC861>Serendipitous Shop</color>
1. <color=#FFEE9E>Vault Cards</color> can be exchanged for rewards in the [<color=#FFEE9E>Serendipitous Shop</color>].
2. Unused Vault Cards will be converted into Speed Up (3 m) items after the Serendipitous Shop closes.

21493:
The Dragon Arena thirsts for battle, urging powerful contenders to prove their might. Only the most resilient teams can overcome its brutal challenges and secure a spot in the championship match.
<color=#FF6278>※Notes:
1. Only 256 guilds can participate in the trial. The trial will end after the first round of the regionals.
2. Rules may be adjusted based on feedback during the trial. Any unexpected errors will be fixed in future updates. Thank you for your understanding!</color>
21494:
<color=#FFC861>Schedule</color>
1. After the registration phase ends, the system will divide guilds into 8 divisions based on their active time periods. Each division will consist of 16 groups, with 16 guilds in each group.
2. Each group will compete in preliminary matches, with guilds facing off in pairs. The winners will advance to the regionals to produce one division champion. The 8 division champions will then advance to the finals to compete for the championship title.
3. All matches will use a single-elimination format. (When guilds lose a match, they will be immediately eliminated and their event ranking will be finalized.)
21495:
<color=#FFC861>Registration & Matching</color>
1. <color=#FFFF00>Guilds</color> must register to participate in this event. Only guilds with at least <color=#FFFF00>4,000 Cups</color> in the Dragon Arena and <color=#FFFF00>30 guild members with Castle Lv 17 and above</color> before registration ends are eligible.
2. Rank 4 and above members can register their guild and choose up to 3 preferred time slots for the event. These time slots can be changed at any time during the registration phase.
3. After registration, Rank 4 and above members can set the participant list.
4. If more than 2,048 guilds register, only the top 2,048 guilds, based on their Dragon Arena Cup rankings, will qualify to compete.
21496:
<color=#FFC861>Participant Guidelines</color>
1. The participant list will be locked on the day of the event. Guilds may edit the list at all other times.
2. Guild members cannot be added to the participant list if they have already participated in the Dragon Showdown with another guild.
3. The system will match guilds based on their preferred time slots. Once the official match time is determined, guilds will be notified via mail.
4. The winning guild of each match may change its preferred time slot before they are matched with another guild for the next round. Guilds may also edit the participant list before the next round begins.
5. While participating in the Dragon Showdown, guilds cannot also participate in the Dragon Arena until they are eliminated or all matches have ended.
6. Guild members who are not on the participant list may change Guilds to participate in the Dragon Arena.
7. The Dragon Showdown uses the same rules as the Dragon Arena's Master League.
8. Wins and losses in the Dragon Showdown will not affect your guild's Cup count in the Dragon Arena.

9. If the two guilds are tied, the guild with the higher kill count will be declared the winner. If they have the same kill count, the system will randomly select a winner.
10. If a match is interrupted by system maintenance, it will be replayed during the final time slot of that round's schedule.
21498:
<color=#FFC861>Rewards</color>
1. After the battle ends, both sides will win rewards. The winning guild will get Victory Rewards, while the losing guild will get Consolation Rewards.
2. In the case of a tie, the guild with the most kills will be declared the winner.
3. Rewards will be distributed after the event ends. You must stay in the <color=#FFFF00>guild you represented</color> to get your rewards.
Guild Rewards: <color=#FFFF00>All guild members</color> will receive rewards based on whether their guild wins or loses
Solo Rewards: <color=#FFFF00>Earn checkpoint rewards based on the team's results and by reaching checkpoints</color>. These include Dracolium, which can be exchanged for items in the Dracolium Shop.
4. Guild Ranking Rewards: Once the battle results determine the final guild rankings, all members who participated in that battle will receive the corresponding Guild Ranking Rewards.
5. If a guild does not get matched with an opponent, they will automatically win. Guild members that fulfill the participation requirements will get the corresponding rewards.

22003:
<color=#FFC861>About Dragon Arena Trials</color>
Dragon Arena Trials will only be open to new Kingdoms. It has the same rules as the Dragon Arena, but with a few minor differences:
1. Registration requirements for Dragon Arena Trials are less strict. Guilds only need at least <color=#FFFF00>11 guild members with Castle Lv 7 and above</color> to register.
2. When the Dragon Arena Trial starts, guilds participating in the current round will obtain a fixed amount of <color=#FFFF00>T2 troops (Arena Troops)</color>. These Arena Troops will be <color=#FFFF00>removed</color> once players leave the Dragon Arena. When dismissing troops in the Dragon Arena, the player's troops will be dismissed before the Arena Troops.
3. When in the Dragon Arena, troops <color=#FFFF00>will not consume Upkeep</color>. Upkeep will resume after players leave the Dragon Arena.
4. In Trials, Guilds will still earn or lose Cups based on their results, but the Cup gain/loss will be lesser compared to the actual Dragon Arena. Once your guild graduates from Dragon Arena Trials, the <color=#FFFF00>Cups your guild has obtained will determine the League you are placed in your first Dragon Arena battle</color>.
5. If your Kingdom's protection period is below 30 days (may have 1 day difference based on Kingdom opening time), your Kingdom will advance to the normal Dragon Arena.

7. Dragite won in the event can be exchanged for items in the Dragite Shop.
8. You will be issued Dragonwing Boots upon entering the Dragon Arena (the number you get depends your League). Dragonwing Boots can only be used in the Dragon Arena, and will be removed when you leave.
9. When the Dragon Arena Trial starts, guilds participating in the current round will obtain a fixed amount of Arena Troops. These Arena Troops can <color=#FFFF00>participate in Dragon Arena battles, and will be removed once you leave the Dragon Arena</color>. When dismissing troops in the Dragon Arena, your troops will be dismissed before the Arena Troops.
22007:
<color=#FFC861>Registration & Matching</color>
1. <color=#FFFF00>Guilds</color> must register to participate in this event. Only guilds with at least <color=#FFFF00>11 guild members with Castle Lv 7 and above</color> before the event starts are eligible.
2. Rank 4 and above members can register their guild and choose a preferred time slot for the event. This time slot can be changed at any time during the registration phase.
3. After registration, Rank 4 and above members can set the participant lists for Team A and Team B. Guilds will earn or lose Cups based on Team A's results.
4. Guilds may not get the time slot they selected during registration, and may be assigned to a different time slot. They will get a mail notifying them of the change.
5. Guilds will be paired according to their preferred time slot and the number of Cups they have. Successfully paired Guilds can enter the Dragon Arena during their assigned time slot. If a Guild has not set its participant lists, it will not be paired and cannot participate in the event.
6. From the start of the matching phase to the end of the event, guilds cannot change their participant list and cannot be disbanded.
22008:
<color=#FFC861>Participation Requirements</color>
1. Guilds must have at least <color=#FFFF00>11 guild members at Castle Lv 7 and above</color> to be eligible.
2. Once your guild has registered, Rank 4 and above members can set the participant lists for Team A and Team B. Only players on these lists can participate.
3. You can only represent the guild you are in <color=#FFEE9E>when the matching phase starts</color>. You will not be able to participate if you switch guilds during the event.
22009:
<color=#FFC861>Entry Requirements</color>
1. Must be at Castle <color=#FFFF00>Lv 7</color> and above
2. No wounded troops in the Infirmary
3. No damaged traps and at full Wall HP
4. Battle Fury must be inactive
5. No troops outside Turf
6. Each player can only enter the Dragon Arena once. You cannot re-enter if you leave midway through.
7. Guild members in the Dragon Arena cannot leave the guild or be kicked out of the guild.
2201:
Deals <color=#ffff00>%x Physical DMG</color>.
Also reduces enemy's <color=#ff6278>ATK by %y</color>.
%b
22010:
<color=#FFC861>Leaving the Dragon Arena</color>
1. You will retain the points you earn in the Dragon Arena when you leave. However, you <color=#FFFF00>cannot re-enter the Dragon Arena</color>.
2. All Leaders caught in the Dragon Arena will be released when their captor leaves.
3. If your Leader is imprisoned when you leave the Dragon Arena midway, they will only be released when their imprisonment ends.
4. Troops wounded in the Dragon Arena will be healed once you leave.
5. Traps damaged in the Dragon Arena will be repaired once you leave. However, you cannot exceed your Trap Limit. If your damaged Traps exceed your Trap Limit, the higher tiered Traps will be repaired first. Remaining Traps will be placed under the Repair Traps section in your Wall, and will not be automatically repaired.
6. In Dragon Arena Trials, Arena Troops provided by the system will be removed once you leave the Dragon Arena.
7. Upkeep will resume after leaving the Dragon Arena.
22011:
<color=#FFC861>Leaving Phase</color>
1. All Strongholds will be put into Protection Phase. Traveling Caravans will vanish.
2. Players can check the event's battle status. The guild with the most points wins.
3. Players can leave the Dragon Arena themselves or wait to be returned.
4. In Dragon Arena Trials, Arena Troops provided by the system will be removed once you leave the Dragon Arena.
5. Upkeep will resume after leaving the Dragon Arena.
22012:
9. Certain Familiar skills cannot be used in the Dragon Arena.
10. You will not earn resources, Player EXP, or Hero EXP from attacking players in the Dragon Arena.
11. Leaders can only be imprisoned for 5 minutes in the Dragon Arena. Items cannot be used to execute prisoners or escape from Prison. Bounty or ransom can't be set for these prisoners. Prisoners will be released once their captor leaves the Dragon Arena.
12. Rally times are shorter in the Dragon Arena.
13. Actions in the Dragon Arena will not be included in profile or Guild Fest statistics.
14. When the Dragon Arena Trial starts, guilds participating in the current round will obtain a fixed amount of Arena Troops. These Arena Troops can <color=#FFFF00>participate in Dragon Arena battles, and will be removed once you leave the Dragon Arena</color>. When dismissing troops in the Dragon Arena, your troops will be dismissed before the Arena Troops.
15. After entering the Dragon Arena, active Shields will be temporarily deactivated. Shields will be reactivated with the remaining time after leaving the Dragon Arena.

Auto-join is a feature that allows you to automatically join your guildmates' Darknest rallies when it is activated.
1. Auto-Join will only stay active for up to <color=#FFEE9E>8 hours</color> at a time, and can be manually deactivated at any time.
2. Your deployed army will only consist of one troop type (based on the majority troop type of the Rally Captain). No troops will be deployed if you do not have that troop type, and higher tier troops will be given priority.
3. You cannot auto-join rallies where the rally time is set for 8 hours.
4. This feature cannot be used and will be automatically deactivated if you do not have enough Infirmary Capacity (if Infirmary is full or exceeds its capacity from an event battleground).
5. You can only auto-join Darknest rallies <color=#FFEE9E>1 minute</color> after the rally is initiated.

22096:
Evil is invading the Kingdoms of Athena. Rally your guildmates and help us rid the darkness!

<color=#FFEE9E>Event Rules</color>
1. Only <color=#FFEE9E>Rank 4 and above members</color> can use Dark Crests to summon special Darknests for the guild.
2. The summoner will have to choose a difficulty level. When summoned, 5 Vanguard Darknests and 1 Commander Darknest will appear on the Kingdom Map around the summoner.
3. After a successful summon, the challenge must be completed within <color=#FFEE9E>3 hours</color>. Complete the challenge to unlock the next difficulty level.
4. Dark Crests will be returned if the guild fails the challenge or if a Rank 4 and above member abandons the challenge.
5.You can only join the event through <color=#FFEE9E>1</color> Guild; you may not switch Guilds.
6. Unlocked difficulty levels will not be reset at the end of the event. Your guild will be able to continue from the highest unlocked difficulty level in the next event.
22097:
<color=#FFEE9E>Battle Rules</color>
1. The basic rules for special Darknests summoned in this event are similar to normal Darknest battles. However, please note that this event's Darknests will not drop Dark Essences. Players can still use Auto-Join for this event.
2. Each guild member will only get to start <color=#FFEE9E>1</color> rally per challenge. This rally quota will be deducted when <color=#FFEE9E>a Vanguard Darknest is defeated or a Commander Darknest is attacked</color>.
3. Once a Vanguard Nest is defeated, it will receive reinforcements. Vanguard Nests must be defeated several times before they can be destroyed.
4. All Vanguard Nests must be destroyed before the Commander Darknest can be attacked.
5. The Commander Darknest will not receive reinforcements after being attacked. Destroying it will require multiple rally attacks. Once the guild destroys the Commander Darknest (when the troop count reaches <color=#FFEE9E>0</color>), the challenge will be complete.

22247: (Oath Keeper tries to stop them, but Demon Slayer blocks the way.)

2225: Crumple Zone absorbs up to <color=#ffff00>%y incoming Physical DMG</color>.

22254: Damn it! Well, if you don't wish to live, then you'll die by my hand!
22255: Is this... fate? Will the dragons ever reclaim what we've lost?

23022: Strike down Dark Monsters with Dazzling Stars to win treasures!
23025:
Something sinister is happening in the Labyrinth! Rise up to the challenge and keep the dark beasts at bay with the power of Dazzling Stars!

<color=#FFC861>Event Rules</color>
1. Only the light of a <color=#FFEE9E>Dazzling Star</color> can hurt the dark creatures empowered by the Labyrinth Challenge.
2. The Labyrinth Challenge has two modes: Normal and 10x.
<color=#FFEE9E>Normal Mode:</color> Each attack requires 100 Dazzling Stars. You’ll get a reward after every attack. You’ll also get one free hit per day!
<color=#FFEE9E>10x Mode:</color> Each attack requires 1,000 Dazzling Stars. You’ll get 10x the rewards after every attack.
3. You may get <color=#FFEE9E>Labyrinth Badges</color> as a drop after every attack, which can be exchanged for special items in the <color=#FFEE9E>Labyrinth Challenge Shop</color>.
4. Unused Labyrinth Badges will be converted into Speed Up items after the Labyrinth Challenge Shop closes.
5. Reach tier goals when you spend enough Dazzling Stars to claim the corresponding rewards.
6. Duke it out with players from different Kingdoms in the <color=#FFEE9E>Labyrinth Challenge Rankings</color> based on the amount of Dazzling Stars spent! Get ranking rewards based on your final ranking after the event ends.

<color=#FFC861>Special Events</color>
<color=#FFEE9E>Divine Blessing:</color>The Goddess Athena might bless your Heroes with her power! When the blessing is active, you won’t need Dazzling Stars to attack the creatures of the Labyrinth.
<color=#FFEE9E>The Guardians:</color> In your journey through the Labyrinth, you might chance upon one of the Guardians. Don't let it get away! Kill it and take its treasure!

<color=#FFC861>Auto Attack</color>
You can tap <color=#FFEE9E>Auto Use</color> to auto attack after using your free hit. This will allow you to choose how many Dazzling Stars you want to use to automatically continue attacking.
2. If you encounter the daily reset while auto attacking, your free hit will automatically be used.

23031: Embark on a treasure hunt with Marvelous Tokens!
23034:
Are you feeling lucky? Use Marvelous Tokens to explore the Tycoon Challenge map and discover amazing treasures!

<color=#FFC861>Event Rules</color>
1. Only <color=#FFEE9E>Marvelous Tokens</color> can be used to advance on the Tycoon Challenge map.
2. The Tycoon Challenge has two modes: Normal and 10x.
<color=#FFEE9E>Normal Mode:</color> Each roll requires 1 Marvelous Token. You'll get one free roll per day.
<color=#FFEE9E>10x Mode:</color> Each roll requires 10 Marvelous Tokens. You’ll get 10x the item rewards after every roll.
3. Map tiles may reward you with <color=#FFEE9E>items or special effects</color>. You may even encounter a Gemming Gremlin!
4. The goal is to reach the <color=#FFEE9E>end of the map, where you will encounter a Gemming Gremlin</color>.
5. Encounter a Gemming Gremlin to nab Gems from its stash!
6. The map will be reset once you reach the end.
7. You may get <color=#FFEE9E>Tycoon Badges</color>, which can be exchanged for special items in the <color=#FFEE9E>Tycoon Challenge Shop</color>.
8. Unused Tycoon Badges will be converted into Speed Up items after the Tycoon Challenge Shop closes.

<color=#FFC861>Auto Roll</color>
1. You can tap <color=#FFEE9E>Auto Start</color> to automatically roll the dice after using your free roll. This will allow you to choose how many Marvelous Tokens you want to use to automatically continue rolling.
2. If you encounter the daily reset while auto rolling, your free roll will automatically be used.

23201: Why did you ask me to move these treasure chests from the Labyrinth here?
23202: We're going to use them to buy land! Our troops need more food, and we could use the [Bountiful Plains].
23203: The Bountiful Plains can certainly feed as many hungry mouths as you have, and it's also rich in wood and ore! As they say, "the one who owns the Bountiful Plains will rule the world"...
23204: Alright, we've heard enough. Do you think that the treasure we brought is enough to buy the Bountiful Plains?
23205: *touches the treasure in the chests* Oh, this is certainly enough...
23206: *suddenly smirks* Well, what are you waiting for, fellas? It's time to fight!
23207: Oof. Well if you can't beat em... you gotta run!
23208: Sir! The barrels ahead look quite suspicious. I think the enemy's hiding in them!
23209: Follow me! We need to find and capture them!
23210: Trying to fool me again? Blow those barrels up!
23211: Calm down now, sir! How about this? I'll return your treasures. Now we can all be friends!
23212: Please spare my life. Say, I hear you're investigating those [Dragon Bones]. I've got some valuable information about that I'm sure you'd love to hear!
23213: Is this the ruins Tattler was talking about?
23214: *voice from the depths of the ruins* Is the Dragon King's skull here?
23215: Cathiss?! Why are you leading your troops against me?
23216: You want to stop the Dragon King's resurrection and save the world, don't you? So do I. But I don't want to just be a sidekick!
23218: We've gathered quite a few resources from exploring the Bountiful Plains. I think that it's time we built a granary.
23220: Mmm, I'd love to go fishing at night and catch some fresh fish. Could you help me gather the tools I need before nightfall? Oh, please hurry! It'll get dark soon!
23221: Wow, look at all these sandbags! They're in great condition too. We could use them as military supplies.
23222: Everyone, this is Anderson. I think he can tell us more about the Dragon King.
23223: This old man knows too much only because he's lived for so long...
23224: Do you know why Mount Gloria was named as such? It's because it's where the Dragon King was finally slain...
23225: If you want to stop the Dragon King from being resurrected, you must go to Mount Gloria.
23226: It's so messy here... it makes it seem like I'm not a good leader!
23227: I didn't expect to find a chest after I tidied everything up... I hope there's treasure inside!
23228: Chaos Dragon and Death Knight were right! You have designs on Mount Gloria!
23230: Alas, has my silver tongue finally met its match?
23231: I never thought I'd get to buy land! Let's go meet the owner of the Bountiful Plains!
23232: You're not going to trick me again!
23234: *glances at Death Archer* Death Knight is in cahoots with the person collecting the dragon bones! We can't let them get the skull too!
23236: Produce tools that will help transport the treasure
23237: Clear the vines around the <color=#0000FF>Dragon Skull</color> to obtain it before the mysterious person can!
23238: Please collect the items required within the <color=#BD0000>time limit</color>
23239: Get a <color=#0000FF>Pulley</color> and <color=#0000FF>Lamp</color> for Rose Knight <color=#BD0000>before night falls</color>
23248: The troops need more food. Oath Keeper decides to use the treasures we obtained from the Labyrinth to purchase the [Bountiful Plains].
23249: Trickster, the owner of the Bountiful Plains, seems friendly at first, but turns hostile once he's paid.
23250: It is revealed that Trickster isn't the owner of the Bountiful Plains, and Oath Keeper is incensed!
23251: Trickster can't fool nor defeat Oath Keeper, and has no choice but to offer information about the Dragon Bones in order to survive.
23252: We follow Trickster's information to ruins, and discover that the mysterious person is working with Death Knight, and the [Dragon Skull] they're seeking is here...
23253:
After obtaining the Bountiful Plains, the party chases after Chaos Dragon, only to be stopped by Death Archer, who challenges Oath Keeper to a showdown.
After this confrontation, they agree to join forces to stop Death Knight and Chaos Dragon's scheme.
23254: To prevent the resurrection of the Dragon King, Oracle summons the Sage of Storms, who has vast knowledge of the past.
23255: The Sage of Storms points us towards Mount Gloria, but someone stands in our way.
23261: Who else dares to seek the Dragon King's bones?
23262: And what are you collecting them for? Are you after that unlimited power? You're not going to resurrect the Dragon King, are you?
23263: Ah, it seems like you know a fair bit. But do you really think that you can stop me, a mighty dragon?
23264: (The dragon brandishes his spear. A sinister dark aura engulfs everything as the earth trembles, and the Dragon Skull is swallowed by the black aura.)
23265: Where did the Dragon Skull go? After them!
23266: So, between us, I must find out who's the hunter and who's the prey!
23267: How interesting. I'll challenge you again after you stop Chaos Dragon and Death Knight!
23268: He's escaped into Mount Gloria! Let's follow him!
23269: Hold up. We need to expand the Castle Wall again before we can march towards Mount Gloria.

4. You can re-upload your layout. It can only be shared or viewed once the re-upload is completed.
5. Players from different Kingdoms will compete in the Turf Layout Ranking. Only the top 1,000 players in this ranking can share their layouts.

23772: The Guild Battleground is an ancient kingdom built on sacred ground. It is home to numerous special Wonders called Keeps, with the Pioneer's Monument at its center. Guilds must work together to stake their claim on the Keeps and make their way towards the Pioneer's Monument to obtain Pioneer Rocks. This journey has come to be known as the Guild Expedition.
23773:
<color=#FFC861>Gameplay</color>
1. Guilds are divided into groups of 8 for guild vs guild battles. In the Guild Expedition, their objective is to occupy Keeps and expand their territory towards the center. Rankings will be determined by the amount of Pioneer Points each guild has at the end of the event (based on the Keeps occupied), and will determine whether guilds advance to a higher Division or drop to a lower Division.
2. The battleground is divided into different regions, which will be unlocked as time progresses. The Pioneer's Monument, located in the center of the battleground, is the Keep that holds the highest number of Pioneer Points.
3. Guilds will be scattered around the outskirts of the battleground at the start of the event. They will begin from their Guild Headquarters and must occupy Relay Towers adjacent to their territory to expand it.
4. Guilds will encounter various Keeps in the battleground. Occupying them will provide the guild with various boosts or features.
5. Guilds must <color=#FFFF00>successfully defend Keeps for 5 minutes</color> to occupy them.
6. Troops will not be lost during this event. <color=#FFFF00>There is no Infirmary capacity limit while in the Guild Battleground. Troops will not be automatically healed when you leave the Guild Battleground</color>. If your Infirmary exceeds its capacity when you leave the battleground, it will stop taking in Wounded troops and will not give higher tier troops priority to enter the Infirmary.
7. Infirmary Healing Speed will be increased by 100% and Healing Cost will be reduced by 50% while in the Guild Battleground. These boosts will be lost once you leave the Guild Battleground, but can be regained once you reenter the Guild Battleground.
8. Complete Journey Quests to get Solo Points for [Solo Journey] in the Events Board to unlock and claim Checkpoint Rewards.
9. Guild Points are the total number of Solo Points earned by all participating guild members. Accumulate Guild Points to unlock and claim Checkpoint Rewards for [Guild Journey] in the Events Board.
10. Pioneer Rocks can be exchanged for items in the Pioneer Rock Shop.
23774:
<color=#FFC861>Registration & Matching</color>
1. <color=#FFFF00>Guilds</color> must register to participate in this event. Only guilds with at least <color=#FFFF00>50 guild members with Castle Lv 17 and above</color> before the event starts are eligible.
2. Rank 4 and above members can register their guild for the event.
3. Guilds will be grouped together with guilds of similar Divisions. Each group will consist of 8 guilds max.
4. Guilds cannot be disbanded during the battling period.
23775:
<color=#FFC861>Registration & Matching</color>
1. <color=#FFFF00>Guilds</color> must register to participate in this event. Only guilds with at least <color=#FFFF00>5 guild members with Castle Lv 5 and above</color> before the event starts are eligible.
2. Rank 4 and above members can register their guild for the event.
3. Guilds will be grouped together with guilds of similar Divisions. Each group will consist of up to 8 guilds.
4. Guilds cannot be disbanded during the battling period.
23776:
<color=#FFC861>Divisions</color>
1. Guilds will be placed into Divisions in the Guild Expedition. New guilds will start from Division 1.
2. The top 3 guilds will advance to a higher Division, while guilds in Rank 6~8 will be dropped to a lower Division.
3. Guilds will remain in the same Division if they do not register to participate.
23778:
<color=#FFC861>Participation Requirements</color>
1. Guilds must have at least <color=#FFFF00>50 guild members at Castle Lv 17 and above</color> to be eligible.
2. Once your guild has registered, all guild members who meet the requirements can participate.
3. You can only represent the guild you are in <color=#FFEE9E>when the registration phase starts</color>. You will not be able to participate if you switch guilds during the event.
23779:
<color=#FFC861>Participation Requirements</color>
1. Guilds must have at least <color=#FFFF00>5 guild members at Castle Lv 5 and above</color> to be eligible.
2. Once your guild has registered, all guild members who meet the requirements can participate.
3. You can only represent the guild you are in <color=#FFEE9E>when the registration phase starts</color>. You will not be able to participate if you switch guilds during the event.
23780:
<color=#FFC861>Entry Requirements</color>
1. Must be at Castle <color=#FFFF00>Lv 17</color> and above
2. Battle Fury must be inactive
3. No troops outside Turf
4. You can re-enter the battleground during the event, but there will be a <color=#FFFF00>10m cooldown</color> after entering. You cannot re-enter during this cooldown.
5. Guild members in the Guild Battleground cannot leave the guild or be kicked out of the guild.
23781:
<color=#FFC861>Entry Requirements</color>
1. Must be at Castle <color=#FFFF00>Lv 5</color> and above
2. Battle Fury must be inactive
3. No troops outside Turf
4. You can re-enter the battleground during the event, but there will be a <color=#FFFF00>10m cooldown</color> after entering. You cannot re-enter during this cooldown.
5. Guild members in the Guild Battleground cannot leave the guild or be kicked out of the guild.
23782:
<color=#FFC861>Leaving the Battleground</color>
1. You will retain the points for Journey Quests earned when you leave the Guild Battleground. You can only continue to earn points by re-entering the battleground.
2. If your Leader is imprisoned when you leave the Guild Battleground midway, they will only be released when their imprisonment ends.
3. All Leaders caught in the Guild Battleground will be released when the event ends and players are returned to their Kingdoms.
4. Wounded troops that exceed your Infirmary Capacity will not die when you leave the battleground. However, <color=#FFFF00>the Infirmary will stop taking in Wounded troops and will not give higher tier troops priority to enter the Infirmary until the Infirmary is no longer full</color>.
5. You cannot leave the Guild Battleground while Battle Fury is active.
23783:
<color=#FFC861>Journey Rewards</color>
1. Head to the Events Board to check your [Solo Journey] and [Guild Journey]'s progress on checkpoint rewards.
2. Solo Journey: Complete Journey Quests to get Solo Points to unlock and claim Solo Checkpoint Rewards.
3. Guild Journey: Guild Points are the total number of Solo Points earned by all participating guild members. Reach Guild Point and Solo Point requirements to unlock and claim Guild Checkpoint Rewards.
4. Unclaimed checkpoint rewards will be automatically sent when the event ends.
23784:
<color=#FFC861>Ranking Rewards</color>
1. Rewards will be distributed after the event ends.
2. You must be in the <color=#FFFF00>guild you represented</color> during this time and <color=#FFFF00>have entered the battleground during the event</color> to get your rewards.
23785: Guilds will start out at their Guild Headquarters. Players will be randomly transported into their guild's territory upon entering the battleground. They will not be able to enter if there is no available space in the guild's territory. Guilds must expand their territory in order to allow more guild members to enter the battleground.
23786:
<color=#FFC861>Battle Phase</color>
There are 5 Phases, which will gradually unlock over time.
Phase I: <color=#FFFF00>Towers of Swiftness</color> can be occupied. Work hard to expand your guild's territory during this phase!
Phase II: <color=#FFFF00>Basic Thresholds</color>, <color=#FFFF00>Fountains of Blessings</color>, and <color=#FFFF00>Ruins of Valor</color> can be occupied. Guilds must occupy Basic Thresholds to move to the next region. Basic Thresholds can only be occupied by guilds whose home territory is in the Threshold's region.
Phase III: <color=#FFFF00>Intermediate Thresholds</color> and <color=#FFFF00>Heroic Citadels</color> can be occupied. Guilds must occupy Intermediate Thresholds to move to the next region, and compete with other guilds for territory.
Phase IV: <color=#FFFF00>Advanced Thresholds</color>, <color=#FFFF00>Arcane Towers</color>, and the <color=#FFFF00>Pioneer's Monument</color> can be occupied. Guilds must occupy Advanced Thresholds to reach the central region and compete with other guilds for the Pioneer's Monument.
Phase V: <color=#FFFF00>Basic Thresholds</color> can be occupied by any guild. Guilds have to defend their Keeps until Battle Phase ends.
23787:
<color=#FFC861>Leaving Phase</color>
1. All Keeps will be put into Protection Phase after Pioneer Points are calculated based on the Keeps occupied.
2. Players can check the final event rankings.
3. Players can leave the Guild Battleground themselves or wait to be removed.
23788:
<color=#FFC861>Keep Rules</color>
1. All buildings in the Guild Battleground are special Wonders called Keeps. Please refer to Keep Info for more details on the various Keeps. <color=#FFFF00>[Max Coalition Army Size (Wonder)] related boosts will not take effect against Keeps</color>.
2. Each Keep has its own territory. Occupying a Keep will expand your guild's territory and allow your guild to attack adjacent Keeps. Keeps not occupied by a Guild will be protected by Keep Protectors. Guilds must defeat the Keep Protectors to fight for the Keep.
3. Guilds must <color=#FFFF00>successfully defend Keeps for 5 minutes</color> to occupy them.
4. Keeps with special effects will grant boosts to all guild members belonging to the occupying guild (such boosts are only active in the Guild Battleground).
5. Players can check the [Expedition Rankings] at any time. Rankings will be determined by the amount of Pioneer Points each guild has at the end of the event (based on the Keeps occupied), and will determine whether guilds advance to a higher Division or drop to a lower Division.
23789:
<color=#FFC861>Territory Rules</color>
1. Guild members can only relocate within their guild's territory. Each guild starts off with their Guild Headquarters as territory, and must occupy Relay Towers adjacent to their territory to expand it.
2. Guilds can only attack Keeps, Castles, resource tiles, Darknests, Monsters, etc. that are adjacent to their territory.
23790:
<color=#FFC861>Guild Battleground Rules</color>
1. Troops will not be lost in this event, only wounded. <color=#FFFF00>There is no Infirmary capacity limit. Troops will not be automatically healed when you leave the Guild Battleground</color>.
2. Certain Familiar skills cannot be used in the Guild Battleground.
3. Leaders can only be imprisoned for 5 minutes in the Guild Battleground. Items cannot be used to execute prisoners or escape from Prison. Bounty or ransom can't be set for these prisoners. Prisoners will be released when the event ends and players are returned to their Kingdoms.
4. Rally times are shorter in the Guild Battleground.
5. Actions in the Guild Battleground will be included in event statistics, but not in Guild Fest statistics.
6. You will not lose resources when your Castle is attacked in the Guild Battleground.
7. Participants will be granted an 8-hour Maintenance Shield after leaving the Guild Battleground when the event ends.
23791: The various Keeps in the Guild Battleground serve different purposes. See below for details:
23792:
The Guild Headquarters serves as the starting point for Guild Expeditions. Here, guilds begin their adventure and head towards the Pioneer's Monument in the center.
<color=#FFEE9E>Territory:</color> Can relocate and Camp in this area
23793:
The Pioneer's Monument lies at the center of the battleground. It's the building every warrior desires to conquer.
<color=#FFEE9E>Opens in:</color> Phase IV
<color=#FFEE9E>Pioneer Points:</color> 10,000
23794:
Relay Towers are scattered throughout the Guild Battleground and hold strategic importance, as guilds must occupy them in order to advance towards the Pioneer's Monument located at the center.
<color=#FFEE9E>Territory:</color> Can relocate and Camp in this area once occupied
23795:
Basic Thresholds link different regions, all of which are protected by a mysterious power.
<color=#FFEE9E>Opens in:</color> Phase II (Can only be occupied by guilds whose home territory is in the Threshold's region), Phase V (Can be occupied by any guild)
23796:
Intermediate Thresholds stand in the intersection on the way to Heroic Citadels. Occupy them to pass through.
<color=#FFEE9E>Opens in:</color> Phase III
23797:
Advanced Thresholds stand in the intersection on the way to the Pioneer's Monument. Occupy them to pass through.
<color=#FFEE9E>Opens in:</color> Phase IV
23798:
Towers of Swiftness bless their occupiers with the power of speed, which can help guilds rapidly expand their territory during the early phases.
<color=#FFEE9E>Opens in:</color> Phase I
<color=#FFEE9E>Occupation Boost:</color> Travel Speed +50%
<color=#FFEE9E>Pioneer Points:</color> 1,000
23799:
Fountains of Blessings bless their occupiers with the power of vitality, which can help guilds survive on the battleground.
<color=#FFEE9E>Opens in:</color> Phase II
<color=#FFEE9E>Occupation Boost:</color> Army Max HP +20%
<color=#FFEE9E>Pioneer Points:</color> 1,000
23800: The EXP Set Helper automatically puts on the equipment with the highest Player EXP boosts (cannot be manually edited).
24501: The gnomes love treasure, and have hidden their loot in the location marked on this map. While it may look simple, no one has yet to discover the location of their treasures.
24502: This compass was a gift from Athena to the true Emperor when he was just a boy. Blessed by the goddess, it guided him to the throne. Many believe those who obtain this artifact can also follow the needle and become the Emperor.
24503: Crafted by top dwarven craftsmen, this object can predict the orbit of the stars, eclipses, and even unpredictable powers of nature such as the weather and the tides. This valuable item is highly sought after.
24504: Nebulite is formed from the energy of celestial bodies, and holds a miniature nebula within. Nobles love playing with it, watching the stars and feeling the power within swirling at their fingertips.
24505: Even the most experienced sailors worry about getting lost in the vast oceans. Sailors prayed to the goddess Athena for a blessing, and she granted them this helm which always points the way home.
24506: Many believe this to be a fragment of the sun that landed on the Kingdoms of Athena. It still emanates some light and warmth, and provides a calm, healing effect. It is a sacred relic of the Lapins who worship the light.
24507: One of the Elementalist's inventions. The Elementalist believes that his people come from the stars, and have spent many years using this telescope to search for their birth planet. It is the Elementalist's belief that their origins hold the key to the mysteries of themselves: how they were born and how they will die.
24508: Sandals crafted by top dwarven craftsmen. Made from magic hair and Raven feathers, a sorcerer's spell is the final touch to allow the wearer to fly freely in the sky.
24509: An ancient calendar that records the past and future of Athena's Kingdoms. Many seek to comprehend its writings so that they can rewrite their destiny, quell the chaos, and ascend to the throne.
24510: Many, many years ago, the goddess Athena sent this tablet to guide the tribes in the area, teaching them how to cultivate the land. That land became fertile and is the Bountiful Plains we know today. Many still seek this tablet in hopes of asking the goddess for her blessings.
24511: A gnome invention. This bottle can store solar energy, which can be used when needed. This can be used to replace manual power, and is used in military, admin, construction, and other machinery.
24512: Even the Sirens, who were born and raised in the seas, can lose their way in the vast oceans. Every Siren has a Star of the Sea, a gift from the Siren King, which guides them home when they are lost.
24513: A conch belonging to the Sirens. It contains an endless stream of gold and silver treasures salvaged from shipwrecks, as evidenced by the waterlogged coins rimmed with moss.
24514: Ding ding ding! Congratulations, you're getting closer to treasure! This special machine was made by the greedy Goblin to detect gold mines. It's every treasure hunter's dream.
24515: A mask made by the gnomes from the remains of the Dragon Arena. It is resistant to high temperatures, fire, and abrasions, and was made for mining gemstone. Legend says that as long as you wear this mask, you can explore the underground entirely unharmed.
24516: Made by gnome craftsmen and dragon magic, this treasure once resided in the castle of the true Emperor. Whatever you wish for will appear in the box.
24517: Hundreds of years ago, a meteor shower fell in the mountains of the Encampment. The energy of the fallen stars eventually crystallized into a special salt, and this top ingredient is highly sought after by many chefs.
24518: A rare plant that grows on the mountains in Sacred Soil. It bears delicious fruit, and is fragrant and nutritious. It can be used in cooking, medicine, or can be eaten as it is. Those who eat it often smell of lilies.
24519: Caviar made from eggs harvested from 100-year-old sturgeons. They are plump, round, and bright, and must be eaten with a mother-of-pearl spoon for ultimate freshness. You can taste the sea when the roe bursts in your mouth, but it's still wonderfully sweet.
24520: Only gnomes can sniff out the fragrance of this rare truffle. It only grows in the forest where the Snow Elves live. It varies in size and has a marble-like texture. Some say it tastes like honey, others cheese, and some even claim it tastes like garlic, but what they all agree on is its deliciousness.
24521: A rare microscopic plant that grows in the very depths of the sea. It is dried and ground into powder, and sprinkled on food to give it the fresh taste of the sea. However, it is difficult to find on land as the Sirens will only gift it to their closest friends due to its scarcity.
24522: A rare purple flower with a crimson stigma blooms only on the Lava Plateau. Its stigma is dried to make this saffron, which can be used as a spice or medicine. Rumor has it that ingesting a large amount of this saffron can grant immortality.
24523: Ethereal Guide uses this oil daily to care for her skin and maintain her sultry appearance. It is said to be extracted from a rare blue rose that blooms on the way to the other side, and is fragrant and sweet, yet seductive. But ignorance is bliss, for this scent indicates Ethereal Guide is on her way...
24524: Made from the bark of the golden cinnamon tree tended by the royal gardeners, it has a mild and sweet aroma. It can be used in medicine or in cooking, and is highly versatile. As the tree requires a lot of care, it is currently only available to royals.
24525: Because of how large they are, Giants have a duller sense of taste and are thus especially fond of sweets. After hibernating for a hundred years, their honey water naturally fermented into honey wine, which has been described as a fine vintage wine.
24526: In the Drows' gardens, there is a flower that only blooms at midnight. The fragrant turquoise flowers form nectar crystals at the top of their stamens when they bloom, and must be gathered before they dissolve with the morning mist. This makes them extremely rare, and some Drows use it to trade with other races.
24527: Grown by Petite Devil, they look like normal vanilla pods, but the seeds are pink instead of black, and they taste spicy instead of sweet. Petite Devil loves pranking people with them, giggling as they tear up after cutting into the pods.
24528: Made from tears shed by Flower Elves for the deceased. It carries a floral fragrance but tastes slightly sour. Drinking it straight will cause you to feel their deep sorrow and weep. If you must, drink it diluted instead.
24529: Lapin Nutmegs only grow in the forest where the Lapins live. They have an exciting, pleasant, slightly bitter, yet scorching aroma. After the earthquake that ravaged the Lapins' land, many nutmeg trees died, making Lapin Nutmeg a rare spice.
24530: Lethe rosemary has a strong, intoxicating fragrance, and is used in rituals in sacred places as it has a calming aroma. However, using too much will make one dizzy or even lose consciousness.
24531: This rare wine is made from every flower in the Kingdoms of Athena. It tastes smooth and sweet, and those who drink it will emanate a floral scent. It was loved by the true Emperor and Empress, and is now sought after by ladies everywhere.
24532: Many years ago, the Dragon King met an Elf by the lake at night. He plucked a star from the sky for her, but she rejected him and threw it into the bushes. Perhaps the star-shaped peppers that grew by that lake were fueled by his anger, as they are extremely spicy!
24533: During Chronicler's travels, she came across a colorful dessert, crispy on the outside, sweet on the inside. The taste lingered in her mind even after she left, and so she used her brush to draw it from memory.
24534: A snack prepared by royal chefs with a creamy base atop a crispy cracker, a layer of light sour cream, and topped with a spoonful of caviar. The salt and oil is neutralized by the light sourness, and it is a delicacy that is hard to describe.
24535: The Lava Plateau is so blisteringly hot that locals use it to bake pies with steam, adding a crispy texture and a smoky flavor. Just one bite of this pie can ignite your fighting spirit, and is a must-buy souvenir.
24536: When Sage of Storms granted life to the Witch Doll, he used a walnut from a withered tree nearby to give her a heart. The tree was frozen by this magic, unable to perish yet stuck in its current state. In gratitude, the Witch Doll gathered its remaining walnuts and kept them safe.
24537: Trickster once mentioned this onion soup in his tales, claiming he stole a pot of the soup from a witch's house after he had defeated her. But he has no idea what the soup is made of, so no one dares try it.
24538: Songstress of the Sea's love for seafood has only grown since she lost her ability to live underwater. Her family prepared this delicious dish made of the freshest octopus, crabs, and fish to satisfy her appetite.
24539: Mastercook's specialty. The beef is first roasted over high heat, then simmered, and finally drizzled with his special sauce. The meat melts in your mouth, and is fatty but not greasy. He intends on using this dish to conquer the culinary world.
24540: "Here's your final supper!" Rumor has it this meal is what Ethereal Guide prepares to welcome the souls of the dead. Green bubbles gurgle in the dark pot, and though reluctant, those it is offered to have no choice but to gulp it down.
24541: Mastercook's signature cheese. Apparently, he heats up each cheese wheel personally and ferments it for a year before it gains its unique rich and creamy flavor. It is smooth and dense, and is the secret to his flavors!
24542: The sand in this hourglass comes from the Sand Spirit's magic. When the hourglass is overturned, the sand glows and rustles as it trickles down. It is extremely calming, and those who use it will fall asleep before the last grain of sand falls into the chamber below.
24543: This music box was crafted by dwarven artisans. Colorful birds are engraved on the lid, and it does not require winding to produce the gentle sound of birds chirping.
24544: This magnificent mask was made by royal craftsmen. It is coated with rare tree sap that emits an aurora-like glow at night. Those who wear this mask will certainly become the belle of the ball. It was extremely popular among nobles.
24545: The scepter held by the true Emperor when he ascended to the throne. It is a symbol of his power: its head is a lion carved out of gold and its body is made of Dark Dragon bone. Pull open the lion's head to find a dagger used for self-defense.
24546: A valuable item from the East, and a symbol of friendship. The lines are exquisite and the painting style is beautifully unique. Their kiln firing techniques are beyond any craftsmen in the Kingdoms of Athena .
24547: A pocket watch designed by Soul Forger. The clear body showcases the fast-spinning gears that power the watch. The dots of light flying around it look like shooting stars dashing through the night, and is one of his proudest works.
24548: With a puff of this pipe, euphoria envelops the body in an instant as the bitter and slightly spicy smoke is breathed in. White butterflies flutter from the end of the pipe and disappear into the air, providing a pleasure for all senses.
24549: During his travels, the Sage of Storms met an ailing child who asked him, "Grandpa, could you tell me what the world is like?" The Sage of Storms had lived for many long years, but he felt pity for the young child and his few remaining years of life. He built this telescope for the child, letting him see even the farthest reaches of Athena's Kingdoms.
24550: Legend has it that Arctic Flippers are fond of eating seals, but have difficulty digesting them and form ambergris as a result. That ambergris is used to make this candle, which produces a natural fruity and woody fragrance that proves to be quite calming. However, due to the rarity of ambergris, the price of these candles has skyrocketed.
24551: An item used for rituals, this egg is seated atop an altar. During the day, the outer shell sparkles with diamonds and gold, but when bathed in the light of the full moon, it emits an ivory glow, a sacred light that cleanses the sins of those who touch it.
24552: The only company Shape Shifter had while being experimented on was a small spider lurking in a dark corner. After years of pain, Shape Shifter finally managed to escape, but the spider's life came to an end. She took the spider and crafted it into a magic ring which she could then use to control spiders.
24553: After the Soul Forger crafted the Lava Heart, he turned the remaining material into a bracelet. This gold bangle is studded with crimson diamonds, and when worn, they sparkle like dancing stars.
24554: A ruby prized by Femme Fatale. She loves admiring it at dinner time as she drinks. Even flickering candle flames seem to shine brighter as they reflect off this brilliant gem.
24555: An ancient treasure belonging to the Sand people. It is slightly rough due to the endless sand it has witnessed over centuries. Those who look closely can see an icy blue glow in the center of the stone. Hold it and you could be blessed with the power of sand... if the Sand Spirit approves of you.
24556: A team of adventurers discovered an ancient ruin in a wild jungle. Within these ruins, they found a altar covered in vines, and a strange, dark gemstone seated atop the altar. The leopard-shaped carving that protects the gemstone seems to hint to its forgotten history, and those who touch it seem to hear the voices of departed souls.
24557: Legend tells of a large hunk of ore unearthed a thousand years ago that had the power to lift an entire castle into the air. However, the greed of many tore it apart into small fragments. The remaining pieces only make its owner feel lighter.
24558: A treasure owned by the Snow Elves that has witnessed endless ice and snow for a thousand years. It emits an immense chill and ordinary mortals will immediately freeze if they wear it. Only those who wield the power of snow, like the Snow Queen, can withstand its power.
24559: Made by Soul Forger using Dragite found within the Dragon Altar. The yellow ore resembles a dragon eye, and the entire necklace blazes hot with resentment and anger, rendering it impossible to wear.
24560: An emerald green pearl owned by the cult that worships Nox, the Goddess of Darkness. It is enshrined on a pedestal made of demon bones. The pearl emits a sickly green glow in the dark, and is surrounded by the soft chanting of cultists, drawing unsuspecting victims into their dark clutches.
24561: A trinket Death Archer found during one of her hunting trips. This cute piece of jade doesn't seem to fit her style, so why did she keep it? You can ask all you want, but she'll never admit that it's because it reminds her of family.
24562: While on one of his hunts, Sea Squire chanced upon a bright green glow at the bottom of the sea, and discovered a strange ore in the darkness. He refined and polished it into a pendant, wearing it so his companions can spot him from afar.
24563: After the Eye vanished from the Temple of the Eternal Eye, the remaining fragments formed the Eternal Gaze. It's worn by the high priests of the temple. Don't stare at it too long or you'll get lost in its depths...
24564: Formed by magic when Sirens sing of heartbreak and loss under moonlight. Those who wear this pendant can hear the Sirens singing, no matter how far they may be.
24565: Ethereal Guide loves collecting souls, especially the strongest ones. This crown is used to bewitch her victims, and only those chosen can wear it. It allows the wearer to control the army of the dead, but the price for such a power is your soul.
24566: This is the most romantic love letter ever written, penned by the true Emperor for his beloved. The moment you break its wax seal, the surrounding petals pop and burst into a dazzling miniature rain of roses, accompanied by cheerful harp music, creating a sweet surprise that sets your heart fluttering.
24567: A pendant once carried by an ancient emperor. Drawing strength from the portrait of his true love held within, he led his army into countless battles.
24568: Because marches often take a long time, warriors must carry water pouches to stay hydrated, making them one of the most indispensable pieces of equipment besides their swords. The wear and tear on a water pouch can indicate a soldier’s level of experience.
24569: These are said to the be the socks the true Emperor wore in his final battle. The wear and tear, along with the sweat and blood, are proof to how intense the battle was, and how hard the Emperor fought.
24570: Knights are willing to defend their badge with their lives as it is a symbol of their honor. It is polished often and always pinned prominently to their chest, so that any bystander can recognize them with a single glance.
24571: Once upon a time, a young soldier took a poisoned arrow to his right calf. He persisted on, even as the poison spread through his leg, only collapsing when he defeated the last enemy. Though he lost his right leg forever, he was given a prosthetic leg. His bravery impressed the true Emperor, and he was made the Royal Knight Captain.
24572: 這面由隨手可得的枯樹枝和沾有可疑黃垢的白布拼湊出來的旗幟，是在真王的最後戰役中由黑暗軍團長獻上的休戰代表信物。誰能相信這面破爛白旗竟然帶給了艾娜希大陸和平呢？
24573: The first generation of knights in Wesley's family owned this shield. Despite getting spilt in half after weathering countless battles, the shield's scratches and dents bear witness to the rise of Wesley's family. It is no longer wielded in battle, but now serves as a symbol of the family's legacy.
24574: In the past, knights would throw their gloves in front of their opponent to start a duel. As Oath Knight proudly recounted this, noting that a knight must always act with poise and grace, six-year-old Rose Knight couldn't help but ask, "But Father, who will pick up the gloves?"
24575: This rib is from the first dragon defeated by the true Emperor. Despite its battered state, it is clear that the colossal bone once belonged to an immense beast and is a testament to the true Emperor's bravery and skill.
24576: The first magic staff ever made. It has lost its incredible power, and is believed to be slumbering, awaiting the right mage to awaken it.
24577: A pendant once worn by the goddess Athena that allows the wearer to stop time. However, it has lost all its magic, and is now an ordinary pendant displayed on a cushion, a mere witness to the changes of time.
24578: A mirror once used in Nox's bedroom. It reflects the ugliest desires of those who gaze into it. Many avoid it for fear of seeing their worst selves.
24579: Sage of Storms is so old that he often forgets if he has eaten his meals or taken a shower. He created this comb to help organize his memories, helping him remember what is important.
24580: The apple tree in Athena's courtyard bears golden fruit from her daily care. Eating this fruit will grant youth and beauty.
24581: A perfect combination of dwarf artisanry and elven magic! The elven figure carved into the body not only looka lifelike, but it dances and sings as the harp's strings are plucked. A beautiful performance paired with a lovely voice... it truly is a marvel!
24582: Scared that your wayward sheep won't find their way home? Blow on this magic flute and no matter how far they wander, they will always come home.
24583: The crystal ball used by Dream Witch. When she arrived in the land of Men, she was confused by their constant schemes. She thought to look into her crystal ball to better understand them, but even it could not understand the complexities of Men's hearts.
24584: Plagued by nightmares and insomnia? Still rely on Prima Donna's voice to fall asleep? Why not try hanging this dreamcatcher by your bed? It'll catch all your nightmares, leaving only the sweetest dreams behind!
24585: The Songstress of the Sea missed her friends and family greatly after she lost the ability to live underwater. Upon hearing her prayer, the goddess took pity on her and granted her a magic conch, which she could hold to her ear to hear her loved ones.
24586: In the early days rife with chaos, distrust was common and alliances were shattered easily. The first Oracle wore this monocle blessed by the Goddess to see through lies and discern the truth.
24587: A spell scroll used by Nox's followers. Those who wish to join these sinister devotees must discern these strange words and use them to cast dark spells.
24588: The library within the Royal Wonder holds a vast collection. The most valuable book is the Imperial Decree, which records every Imperial Title assigned by the Emperor. It is written on paper made from calfskin and tied with a gold thread.
24589: A writing instrument passed down through generations of scholars. The feather is said to be taken from the left wing of a Griffin that belonged to the true Emperor. It perfectly fits into one's grip, and makes clean and smooth strokes.
24590: When the stars move across the sky, they bring about fluctuations in both light and darkness. Those who understand these movements can grasp the mysteries behind these changes. This book details the orbits of the stars, and is said to be penned by a lord who never lost a battle in the Arena.
24591: This book was made with water magic and feels cool to the touch. It contains powerful water spells, but only those deemed worthy can discern its mysteries.
24592: This bestiary details various monsters in the Kingdoms of Athena: their appearances, special features, and weaknesses. Those who memorize this book can conquer any monster they come across.
24593: This large tome is said to contain an apocalyptic spell invented by a scholar researching a way to eliminate the Darkness. When a scholar retires, they add a chain to its binds, ensuring that no foolish mortal ever opens this forbidden book.
24594: The cover of this hefty book is emblazoned with the bright gold emblem of the true Emperor, and it details his many experiences and achievements on the battlefield. This book is now regarded as a classic and a must read for every warrior.
24595: This book details the forbidden spell cast by the Sage of Storms that led to his cursed immortality. It is filled with notes about the spell and the many ways to improve it.
24596: A set of heavy books with gilded edges that record what's happened in the Kingdoms since the true Emperor's time. It also details the heroic deeds of past Emperors, Barons, and Paladins, and has inspired many bards.
24597: This poem originated from a wandering bard's impromptu lyrics, and describes poignant scenes of war and the fragility of human nature. Its catchy melody resonated with war veterans and spread throughout the city's taverns. It was eventually penned down in this scroll to commemorate history's scars and the fallen heroes of war.
24598: Due to the spinning gears on the cover, this book seems like a puzzle at first glance. It was written by distinguished dwarven architects, and details various castle designs and how they were constructed.
24599: Spies of the Dark Army chant an incantation while writing reports with their blood, which turns the paper blank once the ritual is complete. This potion is often used to reveal their secret messages.
24600: Hand-picked flowers by maidens in line for priesthood are used to make this balm, which exudes a warm and reverent aura. It is often used in opening ceremonies for Springs.
24601: This perfume was made by pixies, and contains rare fruits and fragrant flowers. The sweet floral notes in its fresh fruity fragrance gives all who smell it an indulgent dream.
24602: Villagers used to burn herbs to rid the streets of the foul stench of battle. Many called this calming fragrance the scent of hope, and it was later made into a popular line of candles.
24603: This gorgeous bottle holds a charming and dominant fragrance, with a hint of spice and sensuality. Be the magnificent empress you were meant to be with this strong and sexy scent.
24604: This fragrance uses multiple spices and fruit wine, a luxurious reminder of the age of prosperity. Many say that breathing in this scent can increase productivity. As such, it is incredibly expensive, and each drop worth a Gem.
24605: A fragrance made by Sirens. Its ingredients all hail from the ocean, and the scent changes based on the mood of the perfumer. It feels like an ocean's warm embrace, making people feel relaxed and energized.
24606: Put on a mask and be your true self. This is an elegant and mysterious fragrance, restrained yet with a hint of seduction and playfulness. This refined perfume is, surprisingly, made by the Masked Goblin.
24607: Fresh morning dew gathered by the elves can heal all ailments. The elves only bequeathed this to their closest friends, and though its magic has long since faded away, it is considered a collector's item due to its rarity.
24608: Hundreds of years ago, an alchemist produced this frigid elixir in his attempts to discover a way to keep food fresh. However, it was too much powerful, as a single drop brought several long months of winter to an entire town, and was thus sealed.
24609: Made from Harpy saliva, this potion boosts courage and lowers inhibition, compelling the user to commit unspeakable acts. Due to these dangerous effects, it has been banned and the formula erased. The few bottles that remain have become many a collector's white whale.
24610: Feeling awkward? Getting cold feet? Just put a few drops of this oil in the fire to bring out the passion within you. Don't hesitate, just party!
24611: Made from a variety of herbs, this essence can revive withered plants, but should be used with care to prevent weed growth. Some have used it to treat baldness, but it granted excessive hairiness, and a cure has yet to be found...
24612: A mysterious chill runs down the spine of anyone who touches this eerie bottle. Once opened, a delicate mist of silver and blue emanates from it. This was a gift from the Ethereal Guide, and it is said that when applied to one's eyelids, it grants the ability to see what is hidden to the naked eye...
24613: 玻璃瓶身中裝著幽暗的墨色液體，輕嗅時讓人全身酥麻、欲罷不能。誰能想到這瓶藥水就是黑暗精靈族中代代相傳的劇毒呢？只要一滴，輕則致盲，重則失去生命，墜入永恆的黑夜。
24614: A small vial hangs from a delicate silver chain wrapped around her slender waist. Within the vial is a swirling purple-red liquid that captures everyone's interest. This exquisite pendant - crafted by dwarves, and commissioned by the Kingdom's first socialite - is no simple accessory, for it releases a crimson mist that would incapacitate even the strongest knight.
24615: Said to have been used at the first dinner party ever, this elegant and practical tableware holder accompanied many impassioned conversations about battle.
24616: What appears to be an ordinary picnic basket is said to have once been carried by the true Emperor's lover for a picnic with Dark Elf nobles. This event led to a crucial alliance with the Dark Elves, making an immense contribution to peace.
24617: 在士兵們長年征戰、艱苦貧乏的日子哩，能帶給他們慰藉的只有一杯杯烈酒了吧！雖然由邊境簡陋設備釀造出來的酒毫無香氣、入口辛辣，但聽見士兵們高舉酒杯時豪邁激昂的笑語後，不禁讓人好奇酒杯裡的味道。來！一起乾杯吧！
24618: This stunning goblet crafted out of tough crystal is used exclusively at royal dinner parties. When examined under candlelight, it reflects a myriad of pretty colors, and when clinked, lets out a crisp and pleasant sound.
24619: Lore Weaver uses this teapot to brew tea before telling a story from her book of tales. As the tea boils, steam from the spout takes on shapes that match the story, making listeners feel as if they are witnessing the tale unfold. Once the story ends, Lore Weaver pours herself a cup of tea, savoring it as the perfect conclusion to the tale.
24620: 木制的擀麵棍上寫滿密密麻麻的精靈咒文，據說用它做出來的麵團特別香甜好吃，讓人心情愉悅，食慾大開！不過也有人擔心咒文的墨水會不會在經年累月的滾壓之中被消磨，造成咒文錯誤、烘焙出黑暗麵包。
24621: 每個矮人出生時都有自己專屬的一把銀湯匙，族中的長老會讓小矮人將湯匙含在嘴中，如同人類的奶嘴。因為他們的祖先認為「銀」為上天所賜予的禮物，可以祛除黑暗力量，因此用「含著銀湯匙出生」祈求新生兒平安光明。
24622: Glasses clink and the sounds of laughter fill the dining hall, concealing the plots and schemes that accompany diplomacy. The royal officer meticulously selects the utensils for every important diplomatic banquet, and this napkin ring never fails to leave an impression. Made from pure gold, it features an exquisite bird carving and was crafted by the true Emperor to welcome his dwarven guests.
24623: A beautiful candlestick made out of gold. It looks like an angel spreading its wings with light cradled in its hands when lit. It is believed that those who pray with this will have their wishes fulfilled by the angels.
24624: It isn't always easy for knights to be able to indulge in a good drink during times of war. Therefore, anyone who possesses a flask instantly becomes the center of attention, and especially if it's one as exquisite as this, with its amber liquid glistening within the transparent glass. There's a saying that every knight on the move must own a decent flask!
24625: When the Watcher first awoke from his slumber, his immediate concern was the state of the spring water, which had been contaminated by alchemical experiments. He earnestly prayed to the Goddess for help, and she aided him by channeling his power into this magical jug. It purifies contaminated water and gives it a sweet and pleasant taste.
24626: Brewed in legendary oak, this fine wine is exceptional in quality. It is crystal clear, rich in color, and perfectly balanced in aroma and taste. Each sip carries a subtle hint of smoky oak. It's Don Guapo's favorite drink, and he often boasts that you can tell a person's taste simply by how they drink. A person without taste, he says, is no better off than one without clothes.
24627: A small palm-sized figure made out of gold to commemorate a hero who defeated a dark monster. It is intricately carved, detailing even the hero's scars. It is sought after by many nobles.
24628: An artisan spent 50 years carving this statue of Athena after they were inspired by the Goddess in a dream. It is so lifelike that many believed the Goddess had descended once again.
24629: This statue depicts a woman who took in homeless refugees during the war. She was a kind woman who grew crops to feed them, but eventually died from exhaustion. It was built by artisans she had rescued, honoring their savior for future generations to come.
24630: Only one arch remains out of the hundred and eight once built by ancient craftsmen. Every arch symbolized the high esteem mortals held for the gods, but most of them were destroyed by wars and the ravages of time.
24631: This giant stone is inscribed with totems and writings from an unknown ancient civilization. The elegant drawings reveal an artistic style that has never been seen before. Rumor has it the monolith contains prophecies about the various races, adding another layer of mystery.
24632: A vase crafted from Gems and decorated with an exquisite four-leaf clover pattern made from jade. It was made by the Gemming Gremlins as a tribute to their beloved queen.
24633: Once upon a time, a duke found himself suffering from the same nightmare every night. In it, someone tried to steal his beloved scepter. After he commissioned the top craftsmen to make a terrifying statue to place atop his castle, his nightmares mysteriously vanished.
24634: Once upon a time, a rich noble chanced upon the Siren Queen. He was so charmed by her beauty that he hired the best craftsmen to carve a statue of her from his memories. He spent his days so entranced by the statue that he forgot to eat or drink. He died alone and no one noticed.
24635: This gorgeous gown is made out of a thousand flowers that only bloom once a year, and took 500 years to complete. The flowers are in eternal bloom, and still exude a strong fragrance.
24636: This carpet was woven by an entire Sand people village. It is so large that it can seat them all. Pleased by its depiction, the Sand Spirit blessed the carpet, allowing it to whisk them away in times of danger.
24637: This exquisite crown was commissioned and loved by the Elven Queen. Even butterflies are charmed by the delicate petals made from silver. This crown has been passed down through many generations of Elven royalty.
24638: Once there was a mage so entranced by the stars that he hid his favorite nebula in a magic bottle. It looks exactly like the night sky. The stars within shimmer brightly in the dark.
24639: When the goddess Athena left our lands many years ago, she left behind a pair of wings. It is said that these wings will protect the people in times of great disaster. Many believe in this legend, and they are currently enshrined in the royal altar for worship.
24640: Unicorn horns contain powerful magic energy, and are able to heal fatal wounds. But it is extremely rare to come by as misfortune comes to those who dare hunt a unicorn, and is thus priced extravagantly on the black market.
24641: After Athena defeated Nox, she broke off a pair of the latter's horns and made her vow to never appear again. These horns were once buried deep beneath the Altar. Many believe that they still carry much darkness, and can only be safe in the hands of someone or something powerful.
24642: Pixie wings are imbued with powerful magic. The gold dust on these wings can open the door to the world of dreams, while an intact wing possesses the power to heal an ailing child. Some pixies are willing to endure the pain of losing their wings to save children whom they hold dear.
24643: The Snow Elves, known for their impassive and arrogant demeanor, rarely shed tears. Even in moments of heartbreak, they will only shed a single tear and are said to never cry again. Legend has it that those who drink the tear of a Snow Elf will never feel sadness again, a power coveted by few.
24644: This forbidden scale once rested on the throat of an evil dragon. A single touch can provoke a wave of destructive fury. Yet in his final battle, the true Emperor managed to cleave it from the beast. Hard yet impossibly light, it is utterly resistant to the scorching flames of any dragon. Gazing upon this flawless trophy, countless heroes of later generations cannot help but wonder: what price did the true Emperor pay to achieve this impossible victory?
24645: Dwarves are natural blacksmiths, and one can go through more than three hundred hammers in a lifetime. This hammer forged by a legendary dwarf, however, can face the hottest flames and toughest steel without wear or tear, and is desired by all dwarves.
24646: 血族曾是黑暗女神忠心的部下，當黑暗女神戰敗而將他們棄於人間不聞不問後，血族的祖先拔下了令人恐懼的吸血之牙，誓言自此之後，血族為只為自己而活。
24647: This brilliant feather feels a blazing flame that never dies. It can provide warmth and comfort to those battling in the harshest winters, inspiring many to soldier on despite the cold.
24648: 狼族鋒利的爪子是令人恐懼的武器，但為何這個狼爪特別收藏於文物廳中，據說是個只有歷任廳主才能知道的秘密。
24649: 雪怪經常於神聖之地的雪原地帶出沒，當地居民已經見怪不怪。但這拓印上的雪怪腳印是一般雪怪的五倍大！從來沒有人見過活生生的、這麼巨大的雪怪！
24650: Crafted for the first person to ever tame Blackwing. Worried the two heads might quarrel over who wore it, the artisan forged twin collars rather than a single one.
24651: These damaged pages excavated from Blithia are precious historical artifacts. The ancient text and mysterious patterns recorded on them have piqued the curiosity of historians, and hold immense historical and research value.
24652: This elixir is a hero's last resort in a desperate hour. Drink it, and a dragon's life-fire ignites within your body, granting impenetrable scales and monstrous strength enough to shatter castle walls. But the purple smoke swirling around the bottle betrays its sinister curse, for when the effect fades, your heart will turn to charcoal. Only those prepared to die would dare drink this forbidden elixir.
24653: This magical silk scroll is believed to be indestructible and is said to contain words from the Goddess herself. Although the language within is now undecipherable, it is still highly valued for its precious contents.
24654: Many alchemists from all over the Kingdoms worked together to compile this book of transmutation recipes. It records their analysis of Dark Essences, and how best to transform them into items useful for war.
24655: This tea is made with water from the Twilight Spring, which is also called the Fountain of Youth. It can cure all poisons and restore vitality.
24656: Many flowers grow on the Bountiful Plains, and it is filled with bees harvesting nectar. The honey produced here is especially fragrant, and tastes like a sea of blooming flowers when mixed with fresh spring water.
24657: This unique beverage is produced by the royal brewery. Only a hundred bottles are produced every year due to the rarity of the ingredients. It is carefully encased in finely engraved glass bottles, and buried in the Sacred Soil after brewing to ensure that it turns out cool and smooth.
24658: Each family owns a bottle of aged wine passed down from their ancestors. They are cautioned not to drink it as the more it ages, the more fragrant it will be. As such, so no one knows how it was made, how it tastes like, or if it is any good at all...
24659: This clear wine is made by the royal brewery to commemorate each Overlord's ascension. No detail is overlooked, from the royal winemakers hand-picking the best grapes to use, to its gorgeous regal packaging.
24660: A limited edition wine that commemorates knighthood. The road towards becoming a knight is long, for one must learn many things, such as etiquette, serving nobility, taming monsters, but nothing beats that single moment of glory when knighted.
24661: Wine made from century-old snow from the Sacred Soil, it is encased in a special bottle that locks in the flavor. Just a single whiff is intoxicating. It is a classic wine that is a staple in every noble's collection.
24662: Freshly brewed wine from the Eastern Kingdoms starts its journey in the spring, and is perfectly ready after a six-month voyage. It is rich and mellow, and pairs perfectly with crab dishes in autumn.
24663: These blossoms may seem delicate and vibrant, but they're actually indigenous to the desert, where they grow sparsely, with only one plant emerging every few dozen miles. It takes many years for them to bloom, but once they do, they can remain in bloom for up to a decade. Each plant bears a pair of conjoined flowers, each with a distinct color and differing in size, resembling the twins blessed by the Sand Spirit. As such, they have been named after them.
24664: 榮耀山丘的特殊花種，蒼白的葉片中藏著紫色花瓣，如同幽影一般，令人分不清是葉還是花
24665: 螢松在夜晚時會發出瑰麗的螢光色，因此而得名
24666: 長得像蛇，有劇毒，誤食會令人喪命
24667: This flower's light blue petals are translucent and shimmer with specks of refracted light, as if delicately carved from the purest ice. It only grows in the moist caves of the Sacred Soil, and serves as the symbol of the Snow Elves. When it blooms, the ice-blue petals gracefully unfurl, just like when the usually reserved Snow Queen shows her rare smile.
24668: 中央是點點小花，周圍一圈大花，如同花冠一般，十分優雅，常在婚禮中使用
24669: 鑿切山脈的矮人最愛的花，加在酒中可增添風味，也是小孩子的零嘴
24670: The delicate petals of this flower resemble the wings of a butterfly, fluttering ever so slightly when touched by a gentle breeze, as if ready to take flight. This precious flower is said to have been specially cultivated by a gardener for their butterfly-loving partner. When this flower blooms, a gentle breeze carries its fragrance throughout the garden, as if butterflies are gracefully dancing in the air. It is difficult to distinguish between these flowers and real butterflies, as they blend seamlessly together.
24671: Nourished by the magma of the Lava Plateau, the exquisite petals of this flower pulsate with flickering flames, exuding a magical allure. However, one must remain cautious and resist the temptation to touch it, as its scorching heat can cause burns! Nobles adorn their abodes with this flower during winter, not only for its beauty, but also for the warmth it brings.
24672: This rare flower appears to embody Pyris, the fire elemental itself. Its petals mimic flickering flames, blooming tenaciously across the barren Lava Plateau. Legends claim that at noon on the summer solstice, the Incinerator plucks these very petals to brew a floral bath which rekindles her fire magic.
24673: A miracle created by Athena in the battle for the throne, this small potted plant serves as both a refuge for life and a symbol of hope. Amid the raging flames of war and widespread suffering, the pot gathers exiled forest animals into its care, as if it were a miniature, self-contained world quietly awaiting the return of peace.
24674: According to legend, a pair of eloping lovers so moved the Goddess Athena that she transformed their tears into this sacred tree. Its twin fruits cling closely together, symbolizing souls that will never be parted. Should newlyweds share this sweet and juicy peach, they will receive the Goddess's blessing, ensuring their love remains as passionate as the day they first fell for each other.
24675: 春天開花，一株只有一朵花，花形巨大，深紅色，層層疊疊的花瓣十分鮮豔美麗引人注目
24676: A favorite treat among dwarven children, this candy begins as a hard, sour fruit plucked from rock crevices, then gets a glossy coat of special rock honey. Each piece gleams like a freshly mined ruby, a treasure that many show off proudly.
