Every bite delivers a loud, satisfying crunch, strengthening teeth and providing boundless energy for digging tunnels.
24677: Pioneers who brave arduous journeys and weather the elements take the branches of the special trees that only grow around the Pioneer's Monument as a mark of their achievements. Due to the energy from the Pioneer's Monument, the leaves and branches of the Pioneer's Tree are as hard as crystal, which makes snapping a branch or a leaf off a Pioneer's Tree a remarkable feat.
24678: This sash, woven with tough thread made from dragon scales, symbolizes supreme glory and affirmation of the bearer's skills. The blue hue represents loyalty and faith, while the gold shield pattern signifies unwavering will. It was only granted by the true Emperor to knights who had accomplished incredible feats on the battlefield, like Wesley's ancestor.
24679: 是王國內非常高級的絲綢布料，內含鑿切山脈所挖掘出的金礦離子，將此種布料直接貼著於皮膚上，將可以活性肌膚、促進血液循環、幫助有效吸收有益成分，常用來縫製成貴族居家的貼身衣物
24680: This unique fabric is woven from the leaves surrounding a dryad's flower buds at birth and exudes the very essence of nature. Light and breathable, it keeps one warm in winter and cool in summer, making it an ideal fabric for travel shawls. It is extremely rare and valuable, as only travelers who gain a dryad's trust will be gifted this precious fabric.
24681: Crafting a Grove Badge is an intricate process, for it needs dye made from greenest oak leaves, yarn spun from soft wool, and silk threads woven from afternoon sunlight. Every dryad dreams of getting one of these, for they are only awarded to those who have distinguished themselves by contributing to the Woodlands. This badge belongs to the Grove Guardian, and is his treasured possession, as it is the first badge he received after partnering up with Bucky.
24682: This mysterious carpet in Chronicler's home may look ordinary at first glance, but in fact, it is a masterfully crafted secret map. Concealed within the intricate shadows of its weave lie cryptic clues, waiting to be deciphered. It is said that only a true puzzle master can unravel the astonishing treasure hidden within and solve this mystery that has been passed down through generations.
24683: Once upon a time, there was an old lady who suffered from ill health and lived in poverty. One chilly winter's night, she chanced upon an unusual carpet, woven with intricate and exquisite patterns. Without hesitation, she covered herself with it, grateful for any warmth. The next day, the old lady was restored to her prime. Her beauty and grace won her the admiration of many nobles. Soon, she became rich in both wealth and status, but it was not meant to last. Years later, she found herself on the streets again. She hunted desperately for that carpet, but to no avail...
24684: Made from silk flowers that grow on the Lava Plateau, this fabric burns easily without being consumed. When ignited, the lace-like flowers on it seem to come alive, blooming brightly amidst a dazzling display of flames. While many hesitate to wear such fabric, it is especially adored by Incinerator.
24685: 用月石粉塵加工後所製造出來的絲綢，在夜晚飄動時會讓看到的人有如置身星空的感覺，只有王國頂尖的舞孃才有資格穿著這種材質的舞裳
24686: 據說每一百隻大長鯨只有一隻會脫皮，而脫下來的皮經過加工後，可以用來包覆食物或蔬果使其長保新鮮，是非常珍貴的物品，只有高級食材才能使用它
24687: Before heading into battle, the warriors of the Frozen Peaks would wear embroidered tokens woven from the hair of their comrades. While this might sound a bit unsettling, the warriors themselves saw it differently. To them, it embodied the blessings of their many comrades, giving them the courage to fight fearlessly and relentlessly.
24688: Priceless masterpieces created by the Snow Elves, Eternal Iceblooms must be crafted and preserved in extremely cold temperatures. They are made from magical ice that never melts, harvested from the frosty depths of the Sacred Soil. The ice is carefully shaved into thin sheets, and a delicate white flower is embroidered onto its icy surface using gentle spring rain.
24689: 據說是森林之子用來治療傷口用的奇特之布，非常薄透，太過用力撕扯即會破碎
24690: 雖然看起來像鱗片，但它卻是黑暗精靈生活在地底時，用魔法編織而成的奇特布料，使用它縫製的衣服將會有迷彩的效果，用於躲避地底危險的生物
24691: This suit of armor may be deceptively light, but it is also incredibly dangerous. It's made out of hundreds of feather-light blades sewn together. The razor-sharp edges make quick work of any foe who approaches, and the armor is deadly in close combat.
24692: 據說是黑暗女神興趣使然用魔法製作的布料，表面上看起來像片老舊的布，但是不小心觸摸到它的話，它將會幻化成你心中最漂亮的衣裳，誘使你穿著它並開始無盡的跳舞，直至你死亡為止
24693: At first glance, it looks like nothing more than a simple childhood toy. In truth, however, it contains the untamed power of the wind. When a traveler encounters danger, all they need to do is pull the crimson cord and throw it. The top will spin, whipping up a violent miniature tornado. These fierce winds can sweep away the suffocating miasma of the Black Forest and, in dire moments, serve as an ingenious weapon to repel enemies.
24694: A legendary artifact of the royal chief jester. Crafted from century‑old enchanted oak harvested from Mount Gloria, these stilts possess the strength to support even the jester's wildly dancing, comedic form. At royal banquets, he straps them on and turns intangible moonlight into solid steps, gliding across the night sky above the capital. Leaping among the stars, he delivers a dazzling, gravity‑defying performance that blends laughter with wonder.
24695: A huge, fearsome creature once roamed the southern lands, striking terror in the hearts of many with its formidable fangs. One day, an experienced hunter who was guided by the Goddess' Oracle finally managed to defeat the monster. He claimed the creature's fangs as a trophy and passed them down to his descendants. They were eventually carved into a set of chess pieces. But even in their current form, these chess pieces still exude the beast's lingering fury...
24696: Shape Shifter extracted the soul of a wandering noble girl and sealed it within this doll. The cluster of blue flowers in its hands embodies her last bond to her homeland. On moonless nights, the doll releases faint sobs. Legend says that if the flowers are watered with the tears of a pure-hearted person, the cursed ceramic shell will shatter, letting the sorrowful soul, trapped for centuries, dissolve into starlight and finally regain her freedom.
24697: 歡慶之地中棲息著一種瀕臨絕種的黑斑白蛾，蛾的翅膀顏色會因為接觸到毒素而變色，於是醫者會將蛾翅固定在針尾，用以測試病患身上或者環境水源中的毒素。因為黑斑白蛾的數量逐漸減少，此種驗毒針的價格也開始水漲船高。
24698: This mortar was crafted from withered pieces of wood found deep within the Encampment, and has been passed down through generations of shamans. A deep, mournful sound, not unlike a spirit's wail, resonates from the mortar when herbs are crushed in it, sending shivers down one's spine. Surprisingly, the resulting concoction possesses a refreshing aroma and miraculous healing properties, capable of curing even the most severe ailments. However, it remains a mystery why every shaman who uses it tends to have a relatively short lifespan, and why their hands turn shriveled and black in their final moments...
24699: 熔岩高原特產，一種燃脂效果極強的蜂蜜，可以服用，也可以塗抹，塗抹在身上會讓人覺得皮膚有燃燒般的灼熱感。生產此種蜂蜜的蜜蜂攻擊性極強，人們至今無法畜養，所以能夠採集的到蜂蜜量極少，手掌般大小的一瓶燃脂蜂蜜要價不菲。
24700: This crucible is made from the heart of a dragon. It is highly durable and can withstand high temperatures and extreme pressure. The herbs boiled in this crucible possess excellent healing powers and can treat many incurable diseases while promoting energy circulation.
24701: 「雖然會流血，但我的傷被治好了！」這是所有信徒接受黃金千針錘叩打之後，都會產生的想法。放血治療的原理其實很簡單，活血化瘀、舒筋活絡，但在知識普遍不足的王國邊陲實在少有人能接受。一名醫生為了讓傷患接受這種治療，以黃金打造這把針錘，並以宗教信仰形式推廣，成功讓當地居民願意接受這種新的療法。
24702: One day, a traveling healer lost his footing on a mountain path and tumbled deep into a valley. Beneath him lay a massive serpent that was wheezing heavily, on the verge of death. The healer understood immediately that the serpent had cushioned his fall, and so he decided to nurse it back to health. Upon its recovery, the serpent thanked the healer and gave it some of its blood, saying, "My blood is the antidote to all poisons." With this gift, the healer crafted a vial of serum that saved many dying patients, winning him fame throughout the land.
24703: Dragonroot only grows in the icy peaks of the Sacred Soil. It is an extremely potent herb but is difficult to obtain because of the perilous environment in which it grows. Thus, it is rarely seen on the market. Many doctors have asked adventurers to help them gather Dragonroot, but it is often used on those same adventurers who risked their lives to find it.
24704: 燃燒這種紅色的微小果實，產生的煙具有強烈的催眠效果，聞到的人很快就會進入深層睡眠，因此有睡美人之稱。醫者將點燃的小果實放在病患房間之中，待病患熟睡時再進行手術，以此代替麻藥使用。也有聽說當病患傷勢過重，等不及藥煙生效，有些醫者會直接拿斷木敲暈患者。
24705: A noble ceremonial accessory crafted from peacock feathers, symbolizing divine insight and foresight. Legend has it that only descendants chosen by the Covenant of Light can truly understand the revelations contained within its feather patterns.
24706: Dwarven records tell of a giant eagle whose spiraling flight was made possible by its naturally curled feathers. Arrows fletched with these rare plumes spun as they flew, greatly increasing their destructive power. Few could master these arrows, and among them was Black Crow.
24707: Long ago, there was a beautiful bird, renowned for its crystalline, vibrant melodies and radiant, multicolored feathers. Wherever it flew, flocks bowed in reverence and flowers lost their brilliance. As aristocrats among birds, their flamboyant beauty drew many predators. Facing extinction, the bird adapted: it grew silent, and its plumage turned a deep, mysterious black. Only under sunlight does the black reveal a hidden spectrum of color—a secret testament to its magnificent origins.
24708: The Goddess Athena once gifted twelve warriors with a gold feather each to thank them for their heroic efforts against the Dark Army. She promised their descendants a wish if they brought the feather to her temple. Upon hearing this story, unscrupulous merchants made replicas to turn a profit, making it impossible to distinguish the real feathers from the fake ones, unless you are the Goddess herself.
24709: In the days of old, when war raged ceaselessly, a Raven's fiancé went missing while on a mission to the Sacred Soil. She set out to find him, her wings turning to ice in the freezing temperatures, but remained steadfast in her search. Unfortunately, she eventually succumbed to the cold, and these frozen feathers serve as a symbol of her undying devotion.
24710: 艾娜希大陸深處有個古老的翼人種族，少年翼人必須通過殘酷的考驗才會被認可為成年。他們必須親手拔下生長在自己柔軟腹部上的最堅硬的羽毛，並將它製成武器狩獵，若成功狩獵了一百隻魔物後，羽毛仍未斷裂，就證明了自己能夠獨當一面。
24711: 初羽－是翼人出生之後，第一根長出的羽毛，也是純潔無瑕的象徵。所以儘管它不是顏色最美、形狀最漂亮的羽毛，但每位翼人都很珍惜初羽。成年的翼人會將它做成指環，送給心儀的對象作為定情信物。但因翼人數量稀少，很多初羽指環一生都只能戴在主人的手上。
24712: A sacred accessory from the Flower Maiden's tribe, said to come from the legendary Stormbird, a creature that dwells where storms never cease. Only the tribe's bravest nomadic warriors can earn this feather. When worn, the bearer moves as one with the wind, gliding like smoke across vast fields—a living embodiment of the storm's spirit: swift, untamed, and free.
24713: 即便身在和平年代，仍然可以看到各國使節之間的無聲競技，能將歌舞昇平的舞會變成另類的戰場。鞋面繁複的刺繡與華麗的蝴蝶結張揚卻不失高雅，鞋跟的高度是不經意流露的小心機…「唉唷，踩到啦！真不好意思呀～」當然，實木製的鞋跟可絕不是裝飾而已。
24714: A pair of soft leather shoes cherished by the Petite Devil. According to her, these shoes are dyed with blood and stitched from the tenderest skin. If you ask her why the toes keep getting longer, she just giggles, "Play a game with me to find out!"
24715: 由東方使節作為禮物呈上的華麗軟鞋。鞋面上的燙金圖騰莊重又高貴，鞋底柔軟，走起路來悄然無聲、步伐輕盈優雅，如同一隻慵懶的貓兒漫步在王宮的花園中。
24716: 這雙高得嚇人的鞋子來自一名有重度潔癖的貴族，為了防止走路時揚起的沙塵沾到衣服下擺，他寧願費力地踩著厚重的鞋底走路。在一次的宮廷宴會中，這名貴族慢吞吞的步伐引起了眾人注目，大家一致認為他走路的姿態十分高雅，厚底鞋因而流行起來。
24717: The freedom-loving and amorous Don Guapo is always ready for a beautiful encounter. He is always dressed impeccably in his flamboyant yet elusive red cape, with a perfectly curled mustache and leather boots that accentuate the curves of his strong calf muscles. But did you know these boots were made by Don Guapo’s first love? Since the unfortunate demise of that first love in battle, Don Guapo’s amorous steps have never lingered for anyone again.
24718: 這雙紅色舞鞋柔軟又透氣，最讓人意外的是，輕薄的皮料竟然意外地耐磨。製作這雙舞鞋的工匠還曾經邀請過靈狐舞姬穿上它跳舞，當靈狐舞姬踮著腳尖連續轉了一百多圈後，舞鞋仍舊閃亮如新！所以放心地穿上它參加舞會吧，不用擔心鞋子磨損，你可以放肆舞動到太陽升起那一刻！
24719: In the rain-soaked lowlands of the Kingdoms of Athena, residents once viewed the persistent dampness with a frown. When Chronicler journeyed there, she adorned their gloomy, moisture-resistant wooden clogs with flowers painted in waterproof pigments. Now, these shoes not only keep out the mud, but the colors upon them are imbued with "happy magic," so that all who wear them walk through the rain with a mood as bright as a sunny day.
2472: Imbued with elemental energy, this ring fills its bearer with vigor.
24720: These crystal-clear, ice-blue heels are carved from solid ice drawn from deep within the Sacred Soil. With their pure, noble appearance, who would guess they are actually instruments of torture used to interrogate criminals? Those forced to wear the Frostbound Heels will dance uncontrollably. As sweat soaks through their clothes, the biting cold rising from the soles gradually numbs their legs. Even when they collapse from exhaustion, the Frostbound Heels do not stop.
24721: A mysterious tribe of skilled dragon riders lives beyond the Kingdoms of Athena. This book, passed down through countless generations of the tribe, contains information on the appearances and habits of various dragon species. It helps the warriors work together with their dragon companions to unleash powerful attacks in battle.
24722: There exists a group of courageous and skilled seafaring warriors who live on a solitary island beyond the Kingdoms of Athena. They wear long-horned helmets on their heads, with the grandeur of the horns representing the number of battles they have fought and their position within the tribe.
24723: The loud bellow emitted by this horn shatters the tranquility of dusk and echoes throughout the entire island to warn of incoming invaders. This vibrant sound also boosts morale and increases the vitality of dragon riders.
24724: Do you often feel like happiness is out of reach? Do you wish to avoid spending sleepless nights sad and alone? Come and try the Happily Ever After Potion, brewed by the Wandering Alchemist. Just drink up, and you'll bid farewell to all your worries in life! What about the warning label, you ask? Don't worry about it! Anything is worth it for eternal happiness... right?
24725: When Dream Witch first left her hometown and arrived in the land of Men, she appeared aloof and indifferent, causing many to avoid talking to her. Feeling lonely, she cast a spell on a handheld mirror, giving it a voice so that she would have a companion. However, the Dream Witch was never talkative, so the mirror often rambled to itself...
24726: One winter's day, Chronicler visited a pastry shop and noticed a row of gingerbread people on display. Before the owner could deliver them, Chronicler had a brilliant idea to use icing to decorate the figures with festive designs. That evening, everyone who received a gingerbread person witnessed something magical, as the figures came to life, singing and dancing to celebrate the holiday with everyone.
24727: Contrary to popular belief, Don Guapo was not born with his charms. As a child, his family hired a dashing swordsman, who wore a cavalier's hat, to teach him the art of swordplay. Don Guapo was captivated by his teacher's flawless techniques and the romantic tales he shared. However, one day, the teacher ran away with the most beautiful woman in town, leaving Don Guapo with only his hat and a note that read, "All tricks pale in comparison to true love, for only sincerity can capture another's heart."
24728: In the slums of the Kingdoms of Athena, an orphan was constantly bullied due to his frail physique. After even his bread was stolen, he decided to undergo training to grow stronger. Years later, he emerged victorious in the arena and won this championship belt. This belt became a symbol of his struggle, reminding him of his journey from helpless orphan to martial arts master. It continues to inspire him to never give up, reminding him that he can do anything as long as he has determination and perseverance.
24729: 有些領主會將戰爭時俘虜的軍民剃去頭髮，作為炫耀勝利與威嚇敗方的行為。剃下的頭髮在層層清洗與淘選後，被製成一頂頂精美浮誇的假髮，作為貴族於宮廷饗宴娛樂時的美化裝飾，也作為手段用以恫嚇前來參與宴會的外國使節。
2473: A dusty old tablet with runes carved upon its surface.
24730: A royal tailor once traveled to a distant land to learn the art of embroidery and returned with their first creation, a magnificent cape embroidered with pure gold and precious silk threads. It is not an ordinary garment that just anyone can hope to possess, not only due to its high cost, but also for its ability to protect its wearer from bladed weapons.
24731: 曾流行在艾娜希大陸上的一種服裝，可藉由拉緊綁帶顯露出腰身。當時無論種族、性別皆崇尚纖纖細腰，此類服裝因而蔚為流行。直到有個王國的領主因為在用餐時噎到，卻因為緊勒住的束腰讓他喘不過氣、無法順利將食物吐出，所以窒息而死，這款服裝才漸漸不再被大眾所推崇，甚至成為了一種刑求道具。
24732: 艾娜希大陸上曾有位非常愛吃美食的貴族，他可以一整年都不買一件新衣服，卻會將家產都拿去搜刮各地佳餚。他的親友除了煩惱不斷外流的財富，也十分擔心他的身體健康，便四處打聽要如何抑制這名貴族的食慾，於是有人獻上了一條被施加了魔法的腰帶，只要發現配繫上腰帶的人吃超過一日生活所需食物，這條腰帶就會勒緊，提醒他－不可以再吃啦！
24733: 在繁華的年代，衣服就是貴族的門面。除了比拼布料的品質以外，富有的千金名媛們還會想盡辦法使用更多的布料以彰顯雄厚財力。這件禮服上浮誇的泡泡袖與長到曳地的裙襬就是最佳的證明－禮服有多重，穿上它的人就多有氣場！
24734: 曾稱霸艾娜希大陸一方的某位女王，在加冕典禮時所戴的頭飾。原本佩戴頭巾是為了防寒保暖，卻意外發現能襯托出黃金王冠的貴重，因此許多女性君王紛紛效仿，在各種重要場合配戴類似的頭飾。
24735: 帽子越高，越尊貴！據說這種圓錐高帽起源於一位身材嬌小的貴族小姐，她曾因為身高問題而被其他名媛們排擠。在一次的晚宴中，她頭戴浮誇的高帽，帽子尾端連著長長的透明輕紗，在眾人的目光下華麗登場，快要頂到天花板的禮帽順利了攻佔話題中心，也令在場的所有女性們不得不為這驚人的存在感折服。
24736: 「天氣真好，好想出去玩呀！但是戴著帽子就會壓垮我精心打理的頭髮了，真討厭！」身邊愛美的女性朋友們是否也有相同的煩惱呢？別怕，這款可折疊、由輕柔絲綢製成的女帽像是一頂敞篷般擱在髮型上，能讓你免受烈陽的曝曬，又能保持體面，是外出不可或缺的優雅配件！
24737: The proud and aloof Incinerator has a castle and rejects all visitors. The only "door" in the castle is a fireplace connected to the outside world. Aside from the ash, Incinerator is satisfied with her fireplace's balance of practicality and style.
24738: Blood stains the wooden throne, seeping onto the carpet as screams and cries for help echo through the walls. A man leans weakly against the chair, waiting for the Goddess of Darkness to answer his call. The crimson stains turn brown and his body is reduced to dust and bones, but the throne remains, waiting for its owner to return...
24739: 還在擔心洗碗傷了你的纖纖玉手嗎？飽餐一頓後，不想面對堆積如山的碗盤嗎？把清潔碗櫥放在你的廚房吧！只要把使用過的餐具放入清潔碗櫥內，將櫥櫃的門關上後再打開，就能發現原本油膩膩又髒兮兮的餐具全部都亮晶晶啦！
2474: Short but lethal, it's the weapon of choice for hunters.
24740: 經歷了一整天的忙碌後，是不是全身痠痛、只想要躺在軟綿綿的大床上一動也不動呢？這張舒筋活絡床能完美貼合身體的每一寸曲線，讓人感覺像是被溫暖的海洋呵護著，施加了魔法的床墊還會順著肌肉的紋理推揉，如同一波波輕柔的海浪拂過身體，洗去了一身的疲憊，讓人感覺放鬆且紓壓。
24741: "Thank the Goddess for her protection! And praise be to my brave savior!" the painter murmured gratefully as he emerged from the depths of the forest. He had lost his way while searching for a picturesque view, and stumbled upon a pack of ravenous wolves. Just as he was about to give up all hope, a child riding atop a majestic deer burst forth from the foliage, bathed in radiant sunlight. "Leave them to me! Just run in that direction, and you'll find your way out!" the child proclaimed. The painter later immortalized his unknown savior in a stained glass window. Whenever sunlight shines through it, it appears as if the valiant hero has returned once more.
24742: The most dreaded instrument of judgment in the depths of Nox's Castle, this chandelier is more than a mere tool of punishment, but also a merciless mirror of the soul. In its eerie glow, sinners are forced to confront the ugliest truths within themselves. Every sin, every dark thought is magnified. Legends speak of countless souls who have wailed in agony beneath its light, their minds shattered by the weight of their own remorse.
24743: 「吼！看我一下啦！」當光明之子的稱號尚未為世人所熟知時，許多人都把他當成一隻只會張牙舞爪的無害小動物。有一天，梅魯梅克族所居住領地的君王設宴，邀請所有自認為驍勇善戰的戰士參加。為了替梅魯梅克族爭取榮耀而出席的光明之子發現－沒有人在意他！憤怒的光明之子一拳劈開一旁的大理石桌，證明了自己不輸他人的力量，成功地讓宴會上的所有人正視他！
24744: Legend has it that the Goddess Athena had a magic gateway in her palace that could take one anywhere, as long as they know the name of their destination. It was sealed by the Goddess herself when Nox arose, preventing the Dark Army from using it for their nefarious means.
24745: A group of dwarves were once trapped in their minecarts when the path ahead collapsed, leaving them no choice but to reverse their way out through a narrow passage. Tracker pulled out her compact mirror and used the reflections to keep an eye on the road, guiding her companions to safety. Impressed by her ingenuity, the dwarves decided to install mirrors on both sides of their minecarts and designed them in many unique ways.
24746: The dwarves of Perilous Ridge host a minecart racing competition once every decade, a cherished tradition that has endured for 500 years. Competitors devise clever techniques to enhance their minecart's speed and strive to be the fastest of them all. However, this quest for speed comes with inherent danger. One day, a young Drumyr, who had not yet earned the title of Soul Forger, unveiled his first creation: a remarkable helmet. This helmet not only helped Drumyr secure victory but also ensured the safety of all participating dwarves, preventing any casualties.
24747: It is said that this doll was once a ferocious monster that was as tall as the Royal Wonder, with impenetrable scales that no artillery shells could breach. None dared to approach it, for a single thump of its colossal tail would send anyone fleeing. However, one fateful day, a mischievous girl boldly rode up to it on a broomstick. The monster attempted to scare her away with a mighty roar, but she simply chuckled and remarked, "Oh, if only you were a little cuter!" With a snap of her fingers, the world around the monster began to grow larger until it transformed into a soft and cuddly doll, becoming part of Dollyjinks' collection...
24748: During one of his voyages, Wave Crasher stumbled upon an uncharted sea, bathed in the ethereal glow of starlight. As he marveled at its beauty, an immense sea creature suddenly emerged, its fluorescent fins piercing through the tranquil surface. Wave Crasher used an axe to cut off one of its dorsal fins, provoking its wrath. The ensuing seismic waves flung Wave Crasher and his ship away from that mysterious sea, and he would never see that creature ever again.
24749: This orb was one of many weapons forged by the Goddess of Darkness, Nox, who used its destructive power in the war against Athena, leaving the land desolate for centuries. After Athena became victorious, she hid the few remaining orbs deep within the sea, as such weapons would only bring about mutual destruction and leave irreparable scars upon the land.
2475: Only those deemed worthy can harness the power of this crystal.
24750: Long ago, the Kingdoms of Athena was filled with powerful and enormous creatures who could speak the language of humans. They were led by a wise king who advocated for peace and refused to be bow down to the Goddess of Darkness, Nox. Unfortunately, when their king passed, an unworthy hunter seized the king's fur, made it into a cape, and sold it for a high price on the black market. This disrespectful act enraged the creatures and they vowed revenge against humanity, becoming the Monsters we know today...
24751: Legends speak of a mysterious dancer from the eastern kingdoms, who possessed a graceful fan adorned with fiery red patterns and gilded edges. However, this fan was no mere ornament, as its razor-sharp edges turned it into a formidable weapon. With elegant and swift movements, the dancer skillfully repelled her adversaries with this weapon. She became a symbol of both beauty and power, and left many enthralled by her enchanting performances.
24752: A martial arts master was famous for his dedication to training. However, his diligence meant that his gloves always ended up battered and torn due to his immense power and intense battles. The Wandering Alchemist recommended him a pair of supposedly indestructible power gloves, which could even harness his fighting spirit and make his fists as hard as steel. These gloves became a testament to his perseverance and bravery, serving as the source of his power to overcome any challenge.
24753: The Valiant Headband is the perfect companion for aspiring marital artists. It not only keeps sweat out of their eyes, but also fills the wearer with an indomitable aura, igniting their courage and resilience. No matter how many times they are knocked down, they will rise again, ready to face any challenge!
24754: The Imperial Seal is an ancient artifact that has been passed down through the rulers of an ancient eastern kingdom. It holds immense historical value, and symbolizes authority and the law. Any decree that bears this prestigious seal cannot be disobeyed, regardless of one's status or influence.
24755: Legend tells of a king in the East who was a wise ruler in his early years and adored by his subjects. However, as time went on, the king began to fear death. A sorcerer offered him this cauldron, claiming that a potion brewed from tears shed within it would grant immortality. To acquire the tears, the king unjustly punished many innocent people, inciting unrest. On the day the potion was ready, as the king raised it to his lips, the rebels ended his pursuit of eternal life with an arrow through his heart.
24756: In the days of old, priests would use this dice during New Year ceremonies to foretell the fate of their kingdom in the upcoming year. This dice has twelve faces, each face bearing a unique symbol that represents significant events like prosperity, peace, disaster, or war. Many believed in its predictions and relied on it to prepare themselves for impending disasters.
24757: An elderly general was concerned that his subordinates would fight for control of his sizable army after his passing. Hence, he crafted this Jade Wyrm tally. Although it was small, it was surprisingly heavy. Only the general's appointed successor would be able to lift it and lead his army.
24758: Legend has it that there is a beautiful rose garden in the middle of the desert that is guarded by a handsome young man. His renowned rose elixir and good looks attracted travelers from afar. However, he soon became overwhelmed by the attention and decided to conceal the garden with magic. A gold bottle cap engraved with a rose was gifted to his most loyal customers, granting them access to the secret garden.
24759: Legends tell of the first temple of the Goddess Athena, where the waters of its fountain would turn into brilliant gold at noon. The temple priests believed this was a blessing from the goddess herself, granting wisdom and inspiration to those who drank from it. Artists and poets would travel there in search of creative inspiration from this sacred spring. To this day, this legend is still shared among artists.
2476: Crafted from leather and bark, these gloves will last through many battles.
24760: This wine chiller is made from ice that never melts. It emits a faint blue light and keeps beverages perfectly chilled. Adorned with snowflake patterns, it adds a touch of elegance to royal picnics and provides a refreshing respite for the weary.
24761: This golden harp is a treasure belonging to the Siren clan and is believed to have been crafted by the God of the Sea for his beloved daughter. Its gentle melody holds the power to calm the ocean. Many sailors have reported hearing the soothing notes of this harp during treacherous storms, which miraculously calmed the turbulent waves.
24762: Wave Crasher had a harrowing experience on his first carriage ride. The coachman kept making abrupt turns, and the unruly steeds made the ride as bumpy as tumultuous waves. Having conquered many storms at sea, Wave Crasher refused to let a mere carriage get the better of him. Determined to take back control, he used a specially forged helm to steer the carriage easily, rendering both the coachman and horses moot.
24763: Even the most skilled knights fear an unruly horse! According to reports from the royal equestrian officers, dozens of knights suffer from dreadful mishaps while riding horses every year, as many lose control due to their inability to communicate with their steed. To mitigate this issue, the Sage of Storms, a skilled rider, made this special pair of riding gloves. When worn, the wearer will be able to communicate with their horse, thus preventing any accidents from happening.
24766: The Pulse Core was forged by the most skilled artisans of Perilous Ridge. It was originally designed as an energy device to extract mysterious crystals from deep within the Sacred Soil. However, an energy leak caused a catastrophic avalanche, prompting the chief of the Dwarves to ban its use.
24767: A high-tech helmet that comes from beyond the Kingdoms of Athena. It allows the wearer to "see" elemental particles, magnetic fields, and even residual energy from parallel dimensions. However, prolonged use can strain the brain, with some users reporting visions of "things that should not exist" or "illusions of the future."
24768: A slow and steady pulse emanates from this enormous egg, covered in searing gold patterns. Inside is a phoenix, gravely wounded in its legendary battle against Nox. Its burning essence brought light to the Kingdoms of Athena when they were shrouded in darkness and lifted the veil to reveal victory. But drained of its strength, the phoenix collapsed into a golden egg. It is said that when it hatches again, the Kingdoms of Athena will once more be plunged into turmoil.
24769: One of the Nox's most cherished "artworks," this exquisitely crafted and ornate birdcage is, in truth, a tomb for souls. Nox extracts the souls of her prey from their bodies and imprisons them within this specially designed cage, where she can observe the flames of their souls writhing and flickering in despair and emptiness from every angle... until, at last, they are extinguished in unwilling oblivion.
2477: Blow this horn to generate a small magical barrier.
24770: This rugged, oversized slingshot, crafted from hardwood and the sinew of some unknown beast, was The Big Guy’s childhood toy. Tough and exceptionally elastic, it’s said that The Big Guy once casually picked up a pebble, infused it with monstrous strength, and shot it skyward, unexpectedly bringing down an unfortunate Frostwing gliding through the clouds.
24771: When Steambot moved to the Perilous Ridge, Soul Forger presented it with this mechanical nightingale as a housewarming gift. Crafted with platinum bones and adamantine gears, it embodies both masterful ingenuity and dwarven artistry. Its intricately layered resonance chambers allow it to produce melodies that surpass the limits of mechanical creation, brimming with heartfelt emotion. This creation represents Soul Forger's understanding and kinship with their steel-forged companion, offering both companionship and harmony.
24772: These ancient scales serve as the sacred instrument of judgment of the Chief Justice. When the sins of the accused are recounted, the Chief Justice transmutes their price into cold weights, placing them one by one upon the scales. The arm rises and falls in silence, trembling like a soul laid bare. Only when the final weight settles, and both sides hang in perfect balance, does the moment of judgment arrive. In that stillness, the verdict is sealed, declaring the end of the trial.
24773: A magical oil lamp, meticulously crafted by the Wandering Alchemist. When fed different offerings, its smoke weaves into vivid scenes from the past, as if time overlaps here. Legend has it that some have been reunited with departed loved ones in its haze, while others have become lost in their memories within the smoke.
24774: Legend has it that on one winter's night, the Sage of Storms visited all the children in the city wearing this red velvet hat. Its brim was adorned with soft white fur, and its surface was embroidered with shimmering golden stars and silver snowflakes, evoking a crisp winter night sky. Tiny crystal bells dangled from the tip, emitting a pleasant jingling sound with the slightest movement. It is said that if the wearer makes a sincere wish, gives the tip of the hat a gentle pull, and then removes it, they will find a gift related to their wish inside.
24775: Every year, in the depths of winter, the Snow Elves hold a mysterious celebration beside the frozen lake under the glow of the aurora. They blend lake water, aurora light, and cedar scent into a dreamlike, gradient-colored beverage. It is served in frosted glass cups adorned with ice crystals that sparkle like stars. With a gentle sip, delicate bubbles burst on the tongue like the soft sound of the season's first snow touching the ground. A crisp coolness lingers, while a shiver of delight sweeps across the skin, effortlessly unwinding the senses.
24776: This purple rose is the only flower growing in the boy's desolate garden. The youth built a glass dome for her, shielding her from the icy cold wind and unsightly caterpillars, and even cast a spell so that the fragile flower, originally able to witness only three sunrises, could bloom eternally. Yet the glass dome was too small to preserve the boy's years. Now, only this rose still maintains a proud and perfect stance, waiting for her beloved master to return.
24777: Crafted by master artisans versed in intricate mechanisms, these goggles are inspired by Night Owl's sharp gaze. Red leather frames are adorned with expensive brass trim, while deep blue crystal lenses emit a mysterious glow of insight. When worn, they allow the wearer to spot distant prey even on the darkest night and detect the slightest changes in the environment. This equipment is a vital tool used by elite scouts patrolling the Vergeway, indispensable for both safeguarding peace and gathering intelligence.
24778: The Forest Lyre is crafted from the finest millennium-old redwood, its body adorned by master artisans with intricate golden vine patterns that shimmer in the sunlight. Legends say its strings are woven from the silver threads of forest elves, and when plucked, soft green echoes of music linger in the air. Its gentle, melodious tones have the power to soothe the heart and speed the healing of wounds. Whether at lively festivals or in the quiet depths of the woods, the Forest Lyre is a bard's trusted companion for sharing songs of peace and joy.
24779: In the Sea of Silence, eternally shrouded in thick fog, the boundary between sky and ocean vanishes, and sailors often lose their way, slipping into madness. Only this hook can cut through despair. The captain plunges it into the abyss and draws the North Star's reflection from the water's surface. The stolen shimmer of starlight, illusory at first, solidifies the moment it is lifted from the sea and becomes a tangible beacon that guides ships out of this cursed ocean forsaken by the goddess.
2478: A crown of leaves and branches woven with utmost care.
2479: This shield used by goblins offers basic defensive capabilities.
2480: Warm and sturdy, these boots make a good companion for adventurers.
2481: Crafted in a mystical swamp, this staff is able to harness magical energy.
2482: A strong aura emanates from the dragon tooth that hangs from this necklace.
2483: A holy relic abandoned by the faithless. Harbors restorative powers.
2484: A Giant's enchantment blesses those strong enough to lift this axe.
2485: A lightweight helmet that shields its wearer from magic. Knights are never seen without one.
2486: A blade that once belonged to an assassin.
2487: Sturdy as stone yet light as a feather. A perfect balance between agility and defense.
2488: A hat that increases the potency of curses.
2489: A ruby ring sought by those who wish to broaden their intellect.
2490: Only those with true courage can unlock the power of this sword.
2491: Enchanted by a shaman, its true power can only be unlocked by blood and sacrifice.
2492: Sprinkled with fairy dust, this wing provides a layer of protection.
2493: Accidentally dredged up from a riverbed, this necklace exudes a soothing and calming aura.
2494: An ornament commonly worn by vampires. Grants the ability to leech life from enemies.
2495: A crystal ball embedded in a dragon's claw. Exudes raw power.
2496: A robust shield used by dwarves.
2497: The toe of a fallen Ogre is often taken as a symbol of a great warrior.
2498: A crude weapon fashioned from a drill bit. Favored by miners.
2499: Part of a Royal Guard Commander's uniform.
2500: Blessed by a priest, this tome increases magical powers.
2501: These dolls made from bird skulls are used by necromancers in ritual sacrifices.
2502: Abandoned in the woods, this puppet lay forgotten until a mysterious force brought it to life.
2503: An accessory favored by the undead, it leeches the life force of enemies.
2504: Legend says that the bearer of this ring will be unstoppable in battle.
2505: Made from an antelope hide, these pants grant the wearer the agility of a prancing buck.
2506: When wielded in combat, this blade emits flashes of light to blind enemies.
2507: Worn by novice mages, this robe protects against magical attacks.
2508: A legendary item, rumored to have been the eye of a deity.
2509: An essential tool when exploring dark places, it ignites courage and vitality.

2520: Crafted by gnomes, this sturdy shield also provides camouflage in the forest.
25201: The stable structure of every building is supported by plaster.
25202: Durable and sturdy, it is a great tool for building houses.
25203: Mosaic tiles that make walls look beautiful and elegant.
25204: The key to a sturdy wall that can withstand an onslaught of arrows.
25205: Forms a formidable wall that can brave the elements.
25206: Thick stone slabs with meticulous decorative carvings.
25207: A tooth from a fierce dragon plucked by a brave hero.
25208: An ancient dragon horn that wizards use to grind medicine.
25209: Sharp dragon claws used by knights to scratch their backs.
2521: Swift and true, this shining blade strikes fear into enemies' hearts.
25210: A strong dragon tail that is now used as a plaything.
25212: Huge dragon wings that were used as shade from the sun.
25213: It takes courage to stand up to a mighty dragon.
25214: Crystal clear quartz that fairies use as a mirror.
25215: Elegant and dignified patterns that look perfect on a dining table.
25216: A beautiful stone that looks like a starry sky.
25217: Durable, resistant, and easy to maintain.
25219: Instead of roses, why not give rose quartz instead?
2522: Made of gold, this bomb is an extravagant way to blast your enemies apart.
25220: Larimar soothes the body and mind.
25221: Don't worry about such trivial matters. Hold this tightly and take a deep breath.

25223: A pedestal that can boost one's height.
25224: Elegant pillars stand tall and watch as the crown is passed on.
25226: Everyone's eyes will be on you.
25227: Only heroes deemed worthy will have their names engraved on such monuments.
25228: Filters water from the roof on a rainy day.
25229: Usually warm and comforting, though some use it to burn secret messages.
2523: Wielded by High Priests, this weapon represents their desire to uphold justice.
25230: The triumphant team passes through the arch and strides towards a bright future.
25231: Houses a small and bustling space, protecting it from the elements.
25232: A dome that gracefully crowns the roof.
25233: Wood cut into logs that can be turned into furniture.
25234: Its rough bark has been removed and polished.
25235: A sturdy beam that can support a house.
25236: Wooden boards with a versatile use.
25238: These wooden joints fit together snugly.
25239: Cats can't help but play with this ball.
2524: Formed over a thousand years in an oyster's care, this pearl possesses a mystical power.
25240: Versatile fabric that can take on any color or design.
25241: This carpet will protect your feet from the chill of cold tiles.
25243: Support your back while sitting with this cushion.
25244: Hang it up to create your own personal space.
25245: A gorgeous tapestry that adorns the wall.
25246: A rug that was once a beast slain by a hero.
25248: Meticulously etched with contrasting hues to make a stunning masterpiece.
2525: Handle these blades with care, for even a single scratch from them will freeze your blood.
25250: Armor impervious to swords and spears.
25253: The dragon symbol keeps bandits and thieves away.
25254: A stool fit for a victorious warrior to rest and drink a pint on.
25255: A comfortable chair. Don't forget to sit up straight!
25256: A table that serves guests gladly with the dishes it holds.
25257: An antique cabinet that will fetch a good price at auction.
25258: A wine rack filled with a vintage collection.
25259: Doors open to a new world, and doors close to a good night's sleep.
2526: Made in a secret emporium, this staff endows strength upon its holder.
25261: A storage chest that contains many antique treasures.
25262: Black and white duel on this chessboard.
25263: A good sturdy desk. Don't slam it, for you'll only hurt yourself.
25264: Filled with books covered in dust.
25265: Tough nails that are undaunted when driven into a wooden board.
25266: Screws need to be fastened, and nuts need to be tightened.
25267: Gears that can spin relentlessly even if they're dizzy.
25268: Reliable hinges that ensure that doors can open and close.
25269: Interlocking rings that can connect in a continuous loop.
2527: The glory of your family is displayed boldly upon this banner.
25270: Eases the burden of lifting heavy objects.
25271: Raise your glass and party all night!
25272: The candlelight flickers, illuminating the terrifying darkness.
25274: The only key to this padlock is safe in your mind.
25275: A lamp in hand will illuminate the path ahead.
25276: A silver jug that purifies water for tea.
25277: A cherished legacy handed down through countless generations.
25278: Black as coal, dark as night.
2528: An angel's most prominent feature, these wings are a symbol of peace and hope.
25280: A bright and beautiful rouge color made from crushing insects.
25281: Scar lightening cream that causes hallucinations and lead poisoning.
25282: Copper ore is ground to make ocean blue dye.
25283: Brilliant as gold, yet lethal as poison.
25284: Natural and affordable, it can also kill bugs.
25285: Made from seashells, this color symbolizes power and nobility.
25286: Strike it with flint to keep the fire going.
25287: A plier with long handles that cuts efficiently.
25288: Its ring signifies that a visitor has arrived.
25289: Latch the door and you'll sleep with peace of mind.
2529: Those who wear this cursed cowl are overtaken by a bloodthirsty persona.
25290: Place a pot of flowers in it and enjoy the fragrance.
25292: A sturdy iron box that won't dent easily.
25293: The cool night breeze blows through the iron bars.
25297: Essential for creating things in basic alchemy.
25298: These mushrooms illuminate paths through the forest at night.
25299: Shines like silver, but is a lethal poison.
2530: Bestowed upon the kingdom's most valiant knights.
25300: A sturdy material made from alchemy that is impervious to many weapons.
25303: Clear water that should never be drunk.
25305: Snowflakes that never melt despite the season.
25306: Soil rich in nutrients. Why don't you dig in?
25307: Produces scorching hot flames perfect for roasting meat.
25308: Pure and natural water that can be drunk without boiling.
25309: A refreshing breeze that brings relief in the summer heat.
2531: These magical gauntlets grant the ability to manipulate fire.
25310: A strand of a dragon's mighty soul.
25311: Fear not winter's chill, for this sunstone can warm us all.
25312: You can live forever as long as you have the Philosopher's Stone.
25313: Wish upon the goddess fire, and you'll have everything you desire.
25314: A speedy minecart that moves through caves like a breeze.
25315: Throw a letter into this hearth and it'll deliver it for you.
25316: Peer into anywhere you desire and see the world.
25317: This millstone never stops grinding grains into powder.
25318: The little elf that lives here helps chase away flies.
2532: A dagger that harbors the power of mental manipulation.
25320: This magical wind vane helps guide lost travelers home.
25321: No wind can extinguish this fearless flame.
25322: A spring that never stops flowing from the levitating stone.
25323: Turn the world upside down and discover new perspectives.
25325: Turn the clock's hands to change the seasons.
25326: A rare acorn with a star pattern. Keep it in your pocket for good luck!
25327: A beautiful star plant that is said to bring good luck.
25328: Every craftsman needs a lucky hammer!
2533: Intimidating boots made from the pelt of an alligator.

25333: Notice Regarding the Removal of Synthesis Isle
25334:
Dear Lords and Ladies,
We regret to inform you that we have made the difficult decision to officially remove Synthesis Isle. We understand that Synthesis Isle is special to many of you, and this was not an easy choice for our team. This change is a necessary step forward as we strive to provide an improved and more focused gaming experience for all. Please be assured that all other game content will remain unaffected.
As a token of our gratitude for your understanding, we have prepared a gift for you. Thank you for your continued support.
2534: Made from enchanted cloth, this hat can absorb mystical energy.
2535: A mask that commands instant respect.
2536: Made from the bones of an ancient dragon, this necklace burns with unbridled power.
2537: Light and smooth soled, these boots leave no tracks behind.
2538: Regarded as a symbol of a warrior's strength, this belt is stained with the blood of a wolf.
2539: Each beat sounds like a tiger's roar.
2540: A weapon used by spectral archers. Exudes an eerie glow.
2541: An indestructible shield. Only those with tremendous strength are able to lift it.

2544: A legendary book guarded by a phoenix. Holds the secret to the flames of rebirth.

<color=#FFEE9E>Pacifist Phase:</color>
Forts cannot be attacked during this phase. Occupy Forts to attack the {0} with stationed troops.
25502:
1.Vanguard Forts will enter Pacifist Phase for <color=#00FFFF>5 minutes</color> once occupied.
2. During Pacifist Phase, players can alter their strategy by <color=#00FFFF>sending or relieving their Reinforcements, initiating the attack on the Wonder, etc..</color> Troops stationed at the Fort will automatically attack the District Wonder after Pacifist Phase ends.
3. Players can start a rally to attack an occupied Vanguard Fort when it is still in Pacifist Phase. However, if the Vanguard Fort is still in Pacifist Phase when the rally reaches it, the rally will withdraw.
25503: Your Guild must occupy a Vanguard Fort to attack this Wonder
25507: Occupy Forts to attack the District Wonder!

25510:
<color=#FFEE9E>Feudal Vanguard:</color>
Only rally attacks can be used to battle for the Vanguard Forts.
District Wonders can only be attacked by the Fort Commander.
<color=#FF6278>※ Tap the [i] for detailed rules</color>
25512:
Lead the vanguard and prove yourself worthy of the title of Baron!
Vanguard Forts have appeared in the Royal Battleground! Occupy them to fight for the District Wonder.

<color=#FFC861>Basic Rules</color>
1. You must use <color=#FFFF00>rally attacks</color> to occupy a <color=#FFFF00>Vanguard Fort</color> before you can attack the District Wonder.
2. Once occupied, a Fort will enter <color=#00FFFF>Pacifist Phase for 5 minutes</color>. Troops stationed at the Fort will automatically attack the District Wonder after Pacifist Phase ends. The Fort Commander can also choose to initiate an attack during Pacifist Phase.
3. Guilds can send Reinforcements to the District Wonder after they occupy it.
4. The Rally Commander who occupies the Wonder for the longest time will be proclaimed the [Baron]!

<color=#FFC861>About Forts</color>
1. <color=#FFFF00>Numerous Vanguard Forts will appear</color> in the Wonder's Province <color=#FFFF00>5 minutes before</color> the Feudal Vanguard starts.
2. Vanguard Forts can be occupied when the round starts.
3. Rally times for Vanguard Forts are shortened to <color=#FFFF00>2, 3, 4, or 5 minutes</color>.
4. Guilds must <color=#FFFF00>successfully defend the Vanguard Fort for 1 minute</color> to occupy it.
5. A Guild can occupy multiple Vanguard Forts, but at any one time can only send 1 Army from each Fort to attack the District Wonder.
6. If the same Guild has occupied the District Wonder before another Army reaches the Wonder, the traveling Army will immediately withdraw when they reach the Wonder.
7. Players can start a rally to attack an occupied Vanguard Fort when it is still in Pacifist Phase. However, if the Vanguard Fort is still in Pacifist Phase when the rally reaches it, the rally will withdraw.
8. Vanguard Forts will no longer be occupied and return to Battle Phase after the Pacifist Phase ends.

<color=#FFC861>About Feudal Points</color>
Earn Feudal Points by killing Tier 4 and above Troops in District Wonder or Vanguard Fort battles.
Kill 1 <color=#00FFFF>Tier 4</color> troop to earn 1 point.
Kill 1 <color=#00FFFF>Tier 5</color> troop to earn 2 points.

27021:
Battle it out with your fellow newbie Lords to prove yourself worthy of being Athena's Ace!

<color=#FFC861>Rules</color>
1. Complete the designated tasks during the event to earn points. Accumulate Points to earn Point Rewards and get on the ranking boards.
2. There are 2 ranking boards - the <color=#FFEE9E>Athena's Ace Rankings</color> and the <color=#FFEE9E>Point Rankings</color>.
3. The following Point ranking boards are available: <color=#FFEE9E>Bold Builder, Research Racer, Stage Expert, Hero Coach, Verge Conqueror, Verge Recruiter, Artifact Hoarder, Monster Hunter, and Troop Trainer</color>.
4. There are three ranking boards, namely <color=#FFEE9E>Artifact Hoarder, Monster Hunter, and Troop Trainer</color>, that can only be unlocked by upgrading your Castle.
5. Each group will consist of <color=#FFEE9E>200</color> players at most. The top 3 players of each ranking board will win Crowns.
6. If two or more players have the same points, the one who obtained the points first will be ranked ahead.
7. After the event ends, players will receive Ranking Rewards based on their rankings. Rewards will be automatically sent via mail to the winners' accounts.
8. Players will lose their event eligibility if they migrate to a Kingdom where this event is unavailable.

<color=#FFC861>Athena's Ace Rankings</color>
1. Win any number of Crowns on the Point Rankings to get on the Athena's Ace Rankings.
2. Players will be ranked by the number of Crowns they've won (<color=#00FF00>Gold＞Silver＞Bronze</color>).
3. If two or more players have the same number of Crowns, the player with the most ranking points will be ranked ahead.
4. The top 3 players on the Athena's Ace Rankings will get a limited-edition event Avatar that cannot be obtained anywhere else in the game! Don't miss it!

Please observe the following rules while making edits:
1. Do not post any rumors that will upset the balance and stability of the game.
2. Do not post anything illegal or instigate others to engage in illegal activity.
3. Do not post any inappropriate or unethical content.
4. Do not post anything related to politics or divisive ideologies.
5. Do not post advertisements of any kind.
6. Do not impersonate a GM, or lead others to believe you are a GM.
7. Do not post anything sexual, offensive, or violates the privacy of others.

27152: *sniffs* I can only smell crime in the air.
27154: If you want to cross the bridge, you've got to pay the... Wait. How does it go again?
27156: Well, if they'd studied a little harder, they wouldn't be bandits!
27157: What did you say? Come on, guys! Attack!
27159: The bandits flee in a hurry, leaving their stolen goods behind.
27160: Nice! Is this our loot then?
27161: We can't keep them! We have to return them to their rightful owner!
27164: *emerges from the ship* Someone's finally here! Help me! They stole all the cargo from my ship!
27165: Wow! We sure found that rightful owner quick.
27166: My cargo! *rummages through the sack* Wait... Where's the [Dragon Wing]?
27167: I spent my entire fortune on that [Dragon Wing]! Could you help me get it back, brave knight?
27168: A whole lot of sweat. Follow me...
27169: A [Dragon Wing], huh? I wonder if it's related to that [Dragon Claw] Soul Forger exchanged for [Dragon Fire] in the Perilous Ridge...
27170: Where did this strong army come from? We've gotta go back and report to our leader!
27171: Here's the [Dragon Wing]. May darkness return to the land...
27172: Do not worry. Nothing will stand in my way!
27173: Ugh... where did these meddling idiots come from? I'm leaving!
27174: You may be strong... but didn't you ever consider that this may be a trap?
27175: What? Where did she hide all these troops?
27176: Does it matter? We just need to take them all down!
27177: Who are you and what did you say? Damn it, they've escaped!
27178: What a barren place. It's no wonder the people here have resorted to thieving.
27179: Oh, I know that look. What are you going to do?
27180: Help them lead a better life. Perhaps then they'll stop stealing from others!
27181: This is hard, especially with these huge rocks in the way!
27182: Well, just blow them all up then!
27183: That's a good idea, but we need to control the blast radius and keep it contained.
27184: Leave it to me! Could you help me collect some ingredients I'm missing?
27185: There are a lot more rocks than I thought! I need more ingredients!
27186: Great! Now the people here can be self-sufficient and won't need to steal from others!
27187: (Bombin' Goblin brings out a worn box.)
27189: (Oath Keeper opens the box excitedly. *boom!* ...And it blows up in his face.)
27191: (Everyone laughs as they see the look on Oath Keeper's face.)
27192: This Castle Wall should really be expanded!
27193: Wow, I didn't expect you to come all this way to offer your services!
27194: I'm not offering anything for free. I heard there was treasure here!
27195: There's treasure outside the Castle Wall?
27196: Your guildmate has submitted content that is currently being reviewed. Please try again later.
27197: Speaking of treasure, what was that [Dragon Claw] you gave that mysterious person all about?
27198: I'm tired of clearing the path! Tell us more about the Dragon King's bones!
27199: Haven't you heard of the legend? It says that the one who gathers all of the Dragon King's bones will be granted "unlimited power"...
27200: Unlimited power? Please. The only power we need right now is something that can help us clear these blasted obstacles!
27201: I've got it! We need a [Gale Cart]!
27202: It was smart of you to think to use a Gale Cart! This is a breeze!
27203: Found it! It's the legendary Labyrinth!
27204: Ooh, there are Monsters here! Finally, the hunt can begin!

27206: I want half as my share!
27208: I'm not opposed to that! But I'd like a place where I can train my troops, let's call it a [Trial By Fire]...
27209: That's all? All you have to do is give me the toughest [Stone Bricks] you can find!
27210: This is the kind of bricks I was looking for!
27211: It's... magnificent! I knew I could count on you, Drumyr!
27212: This Trial By Fire is my gift to you! How to use it, you ask? Just get in there and you'll find out!

27216: This again? I can take them apart without even looking at this point!
27217: Our next meal's here! Eat them!

27220: ...Did you just try to make a joke?

27244: The destruction of the world matters not. All that matters is that the elements are kept in balance.

27247: The Elementals that guarded this place were destroyed, and the Dragon Spine was stolen...

27251: The air Elemental, Tempestite, is too weak. We need some pure Air Elements to heal it!
27252: (After Tempestite recovers, it emanates a white light and purifies the air in the area.)
27253: The fire Elemental, Pyris, is too weak. We need some pure Fire Elements to heal it!

27255: The earth Elemental, Territe, is too weak. We need some pure Earth Elements to heal it!
27256: (After Territe recovers, it emanates a green light and purifies the land.)

27258: Sir Knight! Please do not hold what happened before against me! Could you help me get back what was stolen from me? They fled towards a plot of territory you need, so it's a win-win!

27260: Just so! Who are you looking for?

27365:
They are not evil, but they are never quiet!

<color=#FFC861>Event Time</color>
The event will end at <color=#FF6278>{0}/{1}, {2}:{3}</color>. Don't miss this opportunity!

<color=#FFC861>Basic Rules</color>
1. From Monday to Saturday, Terrors will descend on the Kingdom Map daily.
<color=#FFEE9E>Terror Monstar: Appears every Monday and Thursday
Terror Animare: Appears every Tuesday and Friday
Terror Hollow Husk: Appears every Wednesday and Saturday</color>
2. Dispatch a Hunting Party to hunt the Terrors.
3. Each Terror has its weakness. Deploy the specified Heroes to deal greater damage to them.
4. You'll compete against other players in the rankings with your highest daily score.
5. If two or more players have reached the same amount of damage, the one who obtained that number first will be ranked ahead.

<color=#FFC861>Rewards</color>
1. Complete daily event quests to receive event rewards.
2. Ranking are calculated at the end of the day. You will receive ranking rewards based on your rank.
3. Each Terror has their own achievement rewards that can be claimed at the achievement interface after meeting the conditions.

<color=#FFC861>Notes</color>
1. Hunting Terrors <color=#FF6278>will not grant</color> points towards other events or quests.
2.When hunting Terrors, DMG increases from consecutive Hunts will not apply, and the [Familiar Skill: Hunting Ground] <color=#FF6278>will not be counted</color>.
3. Terrors will not appear in the Royal Battleground, Dragon Arena, Guild Expedition, or Chaos Arena.
27369:
<color=#FFC861>Ranking Rules</color>
1. Your daily highest score will compete for rankings with other players.
2. In case of equal damage, the player who achieved it earlier ranks higher
3. You must hunt at least 1 Terror on the day to be included in the rankings 

<color=#FFC861>Ranking Rewards</color>
Ranking are calculated at the end of the day, and the <color=#FFEE9E>top 1,000 players</color> will win Ranking Rewards. These rewards will be automatically sent to their Bag via a system mail.

28310: The city's granary fell victim to the fire. To obtain food, the party sets off for the fertile lands of Blithia...
28311: To rebuild the Academy and the Workshop, we need to head to the Perilous Ridge to seek help from the dwarves...
28312: At Soul Forger's request, we head to the Encampment to take down the bandits and discover a sinister plot brewing...
28313: Legend has it that the one who gathers all of the Dragon King's bones will be granted "unlimited power", but what Oath Keeper needs right now is the power of food. In search of more resources, the party turns their gaze toward the Bountiful Plains...
28314: Oracle summons the Sage of Storms to help them in their quest. To prevent the resurrection of the Dragon King, the party must head to Mount Gloria, where the Dragon King met his demise...
28315: The last Dragon Bone is hidden in Twilight Spring. On the way there, a large group of hungry Merfolk block the party's path...
28316: Oath Keeper helps Trickster find his stolen treasure in exchange for information on Chaos Dragon. The party heads to the Lava Plateau to stop the Dragon King from being resurrected...
28317: The party takes the initiative to attack the Sacred Soil and engage in their final battle with Death Knight...

28417: Wesley, if it comes down to it, your troops can eat these weeds, right?
28418: Now there are dead trees everywhere?! Are we going to have to eat dirt now?
28419: *reaches out to touch a tree* I can feel... the plantfolks' sorrow...
28420: Look, something is struggling in the vines!
28421: Since the plantfolk are still here, there's still hope for this region yet!
28422: Looks like someone's deliberately blocking our path with all these trees in our way.
28423: Need me to sniff out the rat who did this? I'm all raring to go!
28424: Icarus, the Goddess brought someone to lead us...
28427: There's something strange going on southwest of here. You should go take a look.
28428: Perhaps I'll join you one day, but for now, I'd like to wander and be free. If fate permits, I'll see you all again.
28429: We went through all that trouble to find this chest, and that's all that's in it?
28430: Well, that's the thrill of adventure for you! Perhaps we'll get real treasure next time.
28431: Isn't this Blithia? Why is there such a huge rock here?
28432: Oh, that's not surprising. The earthquake caused the terrain to change slightly.
28433: Perhaps we could use this huge rock for something!
28434: I can sense the darkness... this is just like the withered forests from before!
28435: Watch out! It's an ambush! Prepare for battle!
28436: What's going on? It seemed like those troops were being controlled!
28437: I didn't expect the Sanctuary to still be standing after the fire.
28438: It must have been protected by the Goddess!
28439: By the Holy Light's decree, I stand as your blade—let my steel be the instrument of your will!
28440: This fleeting journey ends—but our legend has only just been forged. I shall eagerly await the promised dawn.
28441: The Artifact Hall hasn't suffered much damage. Maybe we can repair it...
28442: The Artifact Hall has been repaired! [Collect Artifacts] to strengthen our forces!
28443: We need to rebuild the Academy and Workshop if we want to survive the fires of war...
28444: We'll find no better blacksmiths than the dwarves that live in the [Perilous Ridge]. Why don't we head there and ask them for help?
28445: Dwarves are pretty stubborn and have fiery tempers though. We should expand our Castle Wall first to block their gunfire just in case.
28446: Please complete your current quest first
28449: No matter. I was ready for this!
28450: Look, there's a hole there. Looks like someone's taken away what was hidden in here...
28451: Fools! I told them to cause a ruckus, not set fires!
28452: Ugh, I can't stand this heat any longer...
28453: I don't feel the darkness anymore. Perhaps whatever was in there was the source of the darkness...
28454: This fire... Is the Goddess trying to tell me something?
28455: It's Death Knight! This is all his fault!
28456: We've got to put out these fires or pursue him... Let's split up and do both!
28457: Oh, I do love a good hunt~ Leave it to me to track Death Knight down!
28459: Well, no matter what bird it is, I don't think it'll let us through without a fight...
28461: Just surrender already! You're already melting in this heat!
28462: You're wrong! This is just sweat!
28463: The fire bought me time to put my escape plan into motion. We'll meet again!
28464: These flames are getting out of hand! How could Death Knight be this heartless?!
28465: The Goddess has blessed us! Use this [Alchemy Pot] and synthesize a [Water Element] to extinguish the fires!
28466: Aww, our prey escaped. Should we chase after him?
28467: We need more troops and food if we really want to go after him...
28468: ...Let's clear these weeds out first and recultivate the land!
28469: But... what happened to make Blithia become like this?
28470: There's still some food reserves in the south of the city we can use. We need someone to lead us!
28471: There used to be a granary here...
28472: Everything here's been burned to ash. I don't think we'll find any food here.
28473: There's not even a single mouse left behind. Looks like your troops are gonna starve.
28475: To the south is Blithia, a region filled with fertile lands...
28476: We can use this Watchtower to survey Blithia.
28477: Let me see... Well, I see a huge crow flying around...
28478: What terrible eyesight you have! That's not a crow, it's a [Raven]!
28479: We need to build a gate for our Castle Wall, expand our territory, and get scouts to keep watch from here!
28480: Our city is safe for now. Our next goal is to reclaim Blithia!
28484: The fire's getting too big! Synthesize a <color=#0000FF>Water Element</color> and quick!
28485: This is a great place to build a gate for our Castle Wall. Let's expand our city!
28486: The plantfolk are trapped by dark vines! We need to find a way to free them!
28487: Produce materials to repair the Artifact Hall!
28489: What's this? A chest in the rubble? Wow, the Goddess must be smiling down on me!
28490: They must first prove that they have the ability to protect Blithia...
28491: Ah, finally... I have been freed from my burdens.
28492: On your way here, you must have noticed how strange Blithia has become. That's because dark magic has invaded the land.
28493: The Life Sparks are hidden in the mist. They are the key to saving Storm Fox!
28494: This isn't a Life Spark... but it seems to be leading us somewhere...
28495: This is it! Follow Gryphon's lead and we'll be able to find the Life Sparks and save Storm Fox!
28504: The granary in the south of the city has fallen victim to the fire. Let's follow Oracle's instructions and head to the fertile lands of Blithia to find food.
28505: We come across a Watchtower on the way to Blithia and spot a Raven circling the Citadel.
28506: It's time to expand the city and repair the Castle Wall. After this, we can then head for Blithia.
28507: We finally make it to Blithia, but it is overgrown with weeds. We need to clear the land and get it ready for growing crops.
28508: Dead trees fill the once-fertile lands of Blithia, which becomes a real cause for concern. To revive Blithia, we need to free the plantfolk that have been trapped by dark vines.
28509: Large trees lie across the road, blocking our path ahead. We need to clear these trees to meet [Night Raven: Icarus], the general stationed in Blithia.
28510:
Defeat Night Raven in battle to prove your abilities and gain the fertile lands of Blithia as territory.
Once freed of his burden, Night Raven mentions that dark magic has invaded the southwest region below Blithia...
28511: The Sanctuary outside the city was miraculously untouched by the fire! It's almost as if the Goddess protected it...
28512: Many important buildings once stood in the west of the city. While taking stock of the situation after the fire, it is discovered that the [Academy], a building crucial for development, has burned down.
28513: The Artifact Hall was ravaged by the fire. Repair it to collect Artifacts and make your Turf stronger.
28514: To rebuild the Academy and the Workshop, we'll need to head to the Perilous Ridge to find skilled dwarf blacksmiths.
28515: We can use these Summonstones to hire Heroes!
28520: This is what the all-important research institution has been reduced to? You've got to be joking!
28523: Assign 1 Resident to the Lumber Mill
28526: Upgrade Furniture in the Farm to Lv 1
28528: Defeat the skeleton guards and reclaim the city

<color=#FFEE9E>Chaos Stones:</color>
Use to get rewards from the Chaos Shop

<color=#FFEE9E>Get Chaos Stones from:</color>
Chaos Arena Quests
Chaos Journey
※Unused Chaos Stones will not expire and can be used in the next event.
29436: The Chaos Arena is a place where might reigns supreme! Showcase your wisdom and strategies to earn valuable rewards!
29437:
<color=#FFC861>Gameplay</color>
1. Chaos Arena is a battleground event where top players from different Kingdoms compete against each other. The goal is to complete Arena Quests to obtain Chaos Stones, which can be exchanged for various rewards at the Chaos Shop
2. Arena Quests include occupying Wonders, defeating Enemy Troops, healing Wounded Troops, and more. Quests will progress automatically when specific conditions are met.
3. Wonders in the Arena will gradually unlock as time progress. After the Arena opens, Border Stations will be unlocked after 1 hour, Relay Bunkers after 2 hours, and the Chaos Citadel after 4 hours.
4. Arena Quest progress will only count for actions made within the Chaos Arena. Progress will be halted if you leave the Arena.
5. You can re-enter the Chaos Arena during the event, but there will be a <color=#FFFF00>10m cooldown</color> after entering. You cannot re-enter during this cooldown.
6. Troops will not be lost during this event. There is no Infirmary capacity limit while in the Chaos Arena. <color=#FFFF00>Troops will not be automatically healed when you leave the Chaos Arena</color>. If your Infirmary exceeds its capacity when you leave the Chaos Arena, it will stop taking in Wounded Troops and will not give higher tier troops priority to enter the Infirmary.
7. <color=#FFFF00>Infirmary Healing Cost will be reduced by 90% and Healing Duration will be reduced by 90%</color> while in the Chaos Arena. These boosts will be lost once you leave the Chaos Arena, but can be regained once you reenter the Chaos Arena.
29438:
<color=#FFC861>Entry Requirements</color>
1. Must be at Castle <color=#FFFF00>Lv 17</color> and above
2. No Wounded Troops in the Infirmary
3. Battle Fury must be inactive
4. No Troops outside Turf
5. You can only represent the Home Dominion you were in <color=#FFEE9E>when the preparation phase starts</color>. You will not be able to participate if you switch Dominions.
29440:
<color=#FFC861>Leaving the Arena</color>
1. You will retain the points earned for Arena Quests when you leave the Chaos Arena. You can only continue your quest progress by re-entering the Arena.
2. If your Leader is imprisoned when you leave the Chaos Arena midway, they will only be released when their imprisonment ends.
3. All Leaders caught in the Chaos Arena will be released when the captor leaves the Chaos Arena.
4. Wounded Troops that exceed your Infirmary Capacity will not die when you leave the Chaos Arena. However, <color=#FFFF00>the Infirmary will stop taking in Wounded Troops and will not give higher tier Troops priority to enter the Infirmary until the Infirmary is no longer full</color>.
5. You cannot leave the Chaos Arena while Battle Fury is active.
29441:
<color=#FFC861>Quests</color>
1. You must complete quest requirements in the Chaos Arena to progress Arena Quests (actions made in Mirage Realm will not count towards Arena Quests)
2. Each quest is divided into multiple phases. Complete each phase's objectives to earn Chaos Stones and advance to the next phase.
3. If quest progress exceeds the requirements of the current phase, the excess progress will carry over to the next phase.
4. You must have an Army stationed in an occupied Wonder to earn progress for Wonder-related quests. If you have Armies stationed in multiple Wonders of the same type, quest progress <color=#FFFF00>will be calculated cumulatively</color>.
5. You must <color=#FFFF00>clear all remaining resources in a Resource Tile</color> to earn progress for gathering-related quests.
6. Arena Quest completion will be recorded in Event Statistics.
Complete all phases of a quest to clear that quest. This will count towards the Regular/Wonder Quests Cleared statistic.
Progress will only be counted towards the Arena Quest Completions statistic when you successfully clear all quests available (complete all Regular Quest and Wonder Quest phases) in a Chaos Arena event.
29442:
<color=#FFC861>Event Rewards</color>
1. Complete quests to earn Chaos Stones, which can be used to buy various rewards from the Chaos Shop.
2. You will receive Solo Points based on Chaos Stones earned at a 1:1 ratio. Solo Points are used to unlock and claim checkpoint rewards in Chaos Journey.
3. Unused Chaos Stones will not expire and can be used in the next event.
4. Unclaimed rewards will be automatically sent when the event ends.
29445:
<color=#FFEE9E>Total Chaos Stones:</color>
The total amount of Chaos Stones you've earned during this event period is also the number of Solo Points you've earned for Chaos Journey.
29448:
<color=#FFC861>Arena Rules</color>
1. Troops will not be lost in this event, only wounded. <color=#FFFF00>There is no Infirmary capacity limit. Troops will not be automatically healed when you leave the Chaos Arena</color>.
2. Certain Familiar skills cannot be used in the Chaos Arena.
3. Leaders can only be imprisoned for 5 minutes in the Chaos Arena. Items cannot be used to execute prisoners or escape from Prison. Bounty or ransom can't be set for these prisoners. Prisoners will be released when the event ends and players are returned to their Kingdoms.
4. You will not lose resources or <color=#FFFF00>trigger Extra Supplies</color> if your Turf is attacked in the Chaos Arena.
5. Actions in the Chaos Arena will be included in event statistics.
6. Participants will be granted an 8-hour Maintenance Shield after leaving the Chaos Arena when the event ends.

29854:
Decorations can be used to decorate your Turf. Some even provide useful buffs!

<color=#FFC861>Decorations</color>
1. Decorations can be purchased from the Decorations tab in Building mode (hammer icon in the bottom left corner) and obtained from events.
2. Some Decorations have effects <color=#FFEE9E>that stay active even when they are not placed in your Turf</color>.
3. Decorations (with the exception of time limited Decorations) have <color=#FFEE9E>Decoration Points, which only count towards your total Decoration Points when placed in your Turf</color>.
4. Level Decorations up to enhance their effects and Decoration Points. If you have multiple Decorations with the same name, only <color=#FFEE9E>1 of the highest-level Decoration's effect</color> will be active.
5. Check [My Decorations] to see the number of Decorations that are placed in your Turf and in your storage.

<color=#FFC861>Upgrading & Dismantling Decorations</color>
1. You can use <color=#FFEE9E>Lv 1 Decorations of the same name</color> from your storage to upgrade Decorations.
2. You can dismantle Lv 2 or higher Decorations in your storage back into Lv 1 Decorations of the same name, and get back the corresponding number of Decorations spent on the upgrades.

29971:
The Army Colosseum is a grand competition where players can pit their Armies against the world's top commanders to prove their might!

<color=#FFC861>Rules</color>
1. Each season features different Constellations, that restrict deployable Troop Types, ratios, and Army Lineups
2. Adjust your Troops according to the Constellations to challenge opponents and climb the rankings.
3. You can enter the Colosseum 5 times for free daily. Additional entries can be purchased with Gems (price increases with each purchase).
4. Entries purchased with Gems will be reset when rankings are finalized daily.
5. You will <color=#00FFFF>not suffer any actual Troop losses</color> from Army Colosseum battles, so feel free to go all out!
6. The Army Colosseum is divided into multiple tiers. Your rank will determine your tier placement, and each tier has specific challenge rules. Please see the [Colosseum Tiers] tab for details.

<color=#FFC861>Rewards</color>
1. Rewards are distributed based on your final ranking. You must participate in at least one Colosseum battle during the season to be eligible for rewards.
2. Each season's finalization time may vary. Please refer to the countdown timer on the ranking page.
Daily Rewards: Finalized daily from <color=#FFEE9E>Monday to Saturday</color>
Weekly Rewards: Finalized only on <color=#FFEE9E>Sunday</color>

<color=#FFEE9E>Monster Invasion</color>
1. When a Monster Invasion occurs, a large number of enemy forces will invade your Turf.
2. Construct Defense Towers and Castle Walls to fend off the invasion.
3. Successfully repelling the enemies will grant you rewards, which will be sent to your mailbox.
30092:
<color=#FFEE9E>Resident Configuration</color>
1. During Monster Invasions, residents will take up arms to help defend the Turf.
2. In battles, residents are automatically assigned battle roles and will act according to their assigned role (Warrior/Archer).
3. If you use the recommended configuration, newly joined Residents will be automatically assigned battle roles and will participate in battles. If not, you'll need to manually assign roles to new Residents. Unassigned Residents will not participate in battles.
4. This configuration does not affect work assignments in your Turf.

30132:
Ready, set, launch! Aim for a high score in this thrilling pinball game, and get exclusive event rewards!

<color=#FFC861>Event Rules</color>
1. Your first round is free, but you need <color=#FFEE9E>Pinball Tokens</color> to start subsequent rounds.
2. Tap the <color=#FFEE9E>Multiplier</color> button to adjust the amount of points the bumpers will award with each hit. Tap the <color=#FFEE9E>Use</color> button to use Pinball Tokens to start a round.
3. Once you've started a round, pull the plunger to control the amount of force used, and release it to launch the ball.
4. Points will be awarded when the ball hits a bumper. Bumpers will display a number indicating the number of times they've been hit.
5. If you manage to hit the bumpers 11 times in a single round, you'll be awarded <color=#FFEE9E>10x</color> the points for the 11th hit. After that, bumpers will no longer award additional points or push the ball away.
6. If the ball falls into a pocket with a multiplier, you'll earn more points based on that multiplier.
30133:
7. After the ball falls into a pocket, you'll receive Pig Coins based on the points earned in that round at a 1:1 ratio. Pocket multipliers will refresh every round.
8. Points earned in each round will go towards your total. Reach the point requirements to claim <color=#FFEE9E>checkpoint rewards</color>.
9. Pig Coins can be exchanged for rewards in the <color=#FFEE9E>Pinball Shop</color>. Unused Pig Coins will be converted into Speed Up (3 m) items after the Pinball Shop closes.
10. Hold the Use button to <color=#FFEE9E>Auto Use</color> Pinball Tokens. You can still manually pull the plunger in this mode. Hold the button again to activate <color=#FFEE9E>Auto Play</color>, which will automatically launch the ball.

30160:
Synthesis Isle is an island created from the wisdom of the elves and the craftsmanship of the dwarves. Use Energium to complete quests and increase your Synthesis Level to obtain various rewards.

<color=#FFC861>Basic Rules</color>
1. Tap <color=#FFEE9E>Production Tools</color> to consume Energium and produce materials. Energium will recover over time
2. Drag a material onto a similar one to synthesize them into the next-tier material from the same Production Chain.
3. Materials cannot be synthesized once they have reached the highest tier.
4. Tap an item to view its information in the bar below.
5. Upgrade your Castle to unlock more Production Tools.

<color=#FFC861>Special Statuses</color>
<color=#FFEE9E>Vines</color>: Drag a similar material onto the one in the vines to synthesize them into the next-tier material and clear the vines.
<color=#FFEE9E>Vined Crates</color>: Synthesize materials next to vined crates to open them.

<color=#FFC861>Synthesis Level</color>
1. Collect <color=#FFEE9E>Synthesis Materials</color> and complete Synthesis Quests to earn <color=#FFEE9E>Synthesis Badges</color>.
2. Collect Synthesis Badges to increase <color=#FFEE9E>Synthesis Level</color>. Your Synthesis Level affects the quantity and rewards of Synthesis Quests. You'll also get to claim Synthesis Level rewards at each level.
