30309:
All Guild members unite and work together to summon Guild Monsters. After successfully challenging the Guild Monsters, the entire Guild will receive generous rewards! 

<color=#FFC861>Basic Rules</color>
1. Complete event quests to earn points. When the total points of the entire Guild reach the specified score, <color=#00FF00>Monster Crests</color> will be obtained.
2. Monster Crests can only be used by <color=#FFFF00>Guild Rank 4 or higher members</color> to summon Guild Monsters that grant generous rewards
3. After the Guild Monsters are summoned, they will appear on the Kingdom Map, and all members of the Kingdom can go to hunt them.

<color=#FFC861>Rewards</color>
1. Summon Bonus: The Guild that summons the Guild Monster will receive a <color=#00FF00>Summon Bonus</color>.
2. Slayer Loot: The Guild that kills the Guild Monsters will receive <color=#00FF00>Slayer Loot</color>.
Both the Summon Bonus and Slayer Loot can be opened in Guild Gifts.
30310:
<color=#FFC861>Other Special Rules</color>
1. For points obtained from "Darknest" Quests, <color=#00FF00>50%</color> will be received by the <color=#FFFF00>Rally Captain</color>, while the other <color=#00FF00>50%</color> will be distributed based on the <color=#00FF00>Kill Count ratio</color> and <color=#00FF00>Trap destruction ratio</color> of all <color=#FFFF00>Rally participants (including the captain).</color>
2. You will not be able to participate in this event again in another Guild after receiving the Summon Bonus or Slayer Loot.
3. If you migrate to a Kingdom that does not have this event during the event period, you will lose your current event participation eligibility.

30774: I'll arm the villagers and train them to defend themselves
30775: Incredible! We'll be heroes in our own right!
30776:
My Lord,
Our last harvest was poor, and our Food reserves are nearly depleted! Many families are on the verge of starvation. Please think of a solution!
30777: I will open a granary and prioritize providing for the most needy families
30778: You're too kind! You're truly our savior!
30779: Let’s encourage everyone to reclaim wasteland and plant more crops next year
30780: What a wise decision! We will overcome these hardships and soon be well clothed and well fed!
30781:
My Lord,
Merchants from the north are complaining that our taxes are too high. They say they'll start taking a detour, and if this keeps up, our Trading Post will be deserted.
30782: I'll lower the tax rates to attract more merchants
30783: Brilliant! Our Trading Post will be bustling again!
30784: Let's bring in new goods to tempt the merchants to shop here
30785: Fantastic! Our unique products will draw them in for sure!
30786:
My Lord,
There are troubling rumors about you spreading through the city. The villagers are starting to feel uneasy. You need to address this!
30787: I'll hold a public gathering and explain the truth to everyone
30788: How honest and transparent! The rumors will fade away on their own.
30789: I'll ignore the gossip and let my actions speak for themselves
30790: How noble! In time, the truth will come out.
30791:
My Lord,
Some travelers have been saying our city gates aren't very impressive. People are feeling a little embarrassed about it. What do you think we should do?
30792: Let's redesign the gates and make them grander
30793: That's a great idea! Our city will really stand out.
30794: Tell them it's what's inside that counts, not how things look on the outside
30795: That's true! We care about what really matters: strength and substance.
30796:
My Lord,
Lately, everyone in the village has gotten hooked on a new card game. They're so caught up in playing that they're neglecting their work!
30797: I'll ban the game so people can focus on their work
30798: That's tough, but fair! Now the villagers can get back to their duties!
30799: I'll remind them to balance work and play
30800: That sounds perfect! Finding balance is always best.
30801:
My Lord,
The bridge in the southern village is old and falling apart. Crossing the river is getting dangerous, especially when it rains. Could you please look into this?
30802: I'll allocate funds to repair the bridge
30803: You really care about the people! Now we don't have to worry about getting supplies across.
30804: Let's get everyone to pitch in and fix the bridge together
30805: That's a great idea! Working together will unite everyone and make the village stronger.
30806:
My Lord,
The guards are saying their equipment is old and not good enough to defend us. They're hoping you can provide new weapons and armor.
30807: I'll buy the latest equipment for the guards
30808: Fantastic! Our defenses will be stronger than ever!
30809: I'll focus on training them and improving their skills
30810: That makes sense! In the end, personal strength is what matters most.
30811:
My Lord,
Some scholars suggest building a library to help everyone learn more. But our finances are a bit tight right now. What do you think?
30812: I support the idea. Knowledge will help us grow and develop
30813: Such foresight, my Lord! With a library, we'll become a more cultured community.
30814: Let's build the library once we're more stable
30815: Very practical! After all, making sure everyone has enough to eat comes first.
30816:
My Lord,
The miners at the western mine are protesting about long hours and want higher pay. What should we do?
30817: I'll talk to them and promise better conditions
30818: Very thoughtful! The workers will be more motivated.
30819: Set strict work hour limits, but keep pay the same
3082: Sedimentary rock formed from fossilized plants over millions of years. May be found while gathering from <color=#00FF00>Rich Veins</color>.
30821:
My Lord,
The festival is coming up, and everyone wants a big fireworks show. But that will be expensive... What do you think?
30823: So generous! This will be a festival to remember!
30824: Keep it simple to save money
30825: Very wise! Saving now secures our future.
30826:
My Lord,
Someone found a mysterious ancient stone tablet with strange writing. Is this a good omen or a bad one?
30827: Let's have it studied. Maybe it'll lead to treasure.
30828: I see it's piqued your interest! This could lead to a big discovery.
3083: Fine powder milled from various minerals. May be found while gathering from <color=#00FF00>Rich Veins</color>.
30830: It's good to be cautious! Who knows what could happen!
30831:
My Lord,
The children wish for a playground, but there's no available land. What should we do?
30832: Requisition idle land to build a playground
30833: How kind! The children will be grateful!
30834: Encourage parents to use nature for play spaces
30835: Very wise! Nature makes the best playground!
30836:
My Lord,
Several households are arguing over water rights. Please mediate before things get worse!
30837: I'll re-allocate the water sources to ensure fairness
30838: How just! Now everyone will have water.
3084: Shiny, hard, and malleable material. May be found while gathering from <color=#00FF00>Rich Veins</color>.
30840: How wise to trust your people!
30841:
Lords and Ladies: 
A messenger from a distant kingdom is demanding that we pay higher tributes. They’re quite arrogant. How should we respond?
30842: I will firmly refuse to protect our dignity and interests
30843: How bold! We will never yield!
30844: Agree for now to avoid conflict and buy time to grow stronger
30845: A wise choice! Preserving our strength comes first!
30846:
My Lord,
A bard wishes to sing of your bravery, but some worry it might disturb the people. What do you think?
30847: Allow it. The songs will bring joy and culture to the villagers.
30848: That sounds wonderful! Art truly makes life better.
30849: Reject it. We should keep things peaceful and quiet
3085: Common textile woven from flax plant fibers. May be found while gathering from <color=#00FF00>Fields</color>.
30850: That's sensible. People need their rest.
30851:
My Lord,
Lately, a lot of wanderers have been coming into town. They have nowhere to stay and are struggling. How should we help them?
30852: Set up a shelter and ensure that they have food and a place to stay
30853: That's very kind of you. They'll truly feel cared for.
30854: Let's help them find jobs and encourage them to support themselves
30855: That's a smart idea. It's better to teach people how to stand on their own feet.
30856:
My Lord,
An elderly mage wants to open a magic academy in the city, but some people are worried magic could be dangerous. What do you think?
30858: That's very open-minded! Knowledge helps us move forward.
30859: Ban magic to keep the peace

33571: When the duel begins, rivalry between guilds becomes inevitable. It is a true test of unity, determination, and resolve. Only those who endure to the very end will claim ultimate victory.
33572:
<color=#FFC861>Participation Requirements</color>
1. Your guild must have <color=#FFFF00>more than 25 members</color> before the event starts to participate. Players must be at <color=#FFFF00>Castle Lv15 or above</color> during the event to participate.
2. You can only gain points for <color=#FFFF00>the guild you started the event in</color>.
33573:
<color=#FFC861>Gameplay</color>
1. After the event begins, guilds will be grouped with other guilds with similar Cup counts. Guilds in the same group will then face off in 1v1 duels.
2. The Guild Duel lasts for 5 days, with a different quest theme each day. Guild members can earn points by completing the daily themed quest during the event. All guild members' points are added together to form the day's <color=#FFEE9E>Guild Points</color>.
3. Points are rounded down. Any decimal places are dropped when calculating the points for each quest, before adding them to the points from other quests.
4. <color=#FFFF00>Finalization starts daily at 23:55 (GMT-5)</color>, and points can no longer be earned after this time. Once finalization is complete, the guild with the most <color=#FFEE9E>Guild Points</color> that day is declared the winner and receives that day's <color=#FFEE9E>victory point(s)</color>.
5. When points are finalized on the 5th day, each guild's final score will be tallied based on their total points. The guild with the most points will claim the final victory in the Guild Duel.
33574:
<color=#FFC861>Rewards</color>
1. Reward Categories
<color=#FFFF00>Guild Participation Reward</color>: All guild members who achieve both the daily guild point and solo point goals before daily finalization will receive a guild participation reward.
<color=#FFFF00>Guild Victory/Consolation Reward</color>: All guild members who reach the solo point goal will receive a reward, which varies depending on the duel's outcome.
<color=#FFFF00>Solo Point Tier Rewards</color>: Achieve the solo point tier goals before the daily finalization to claim these rewards. There are 9 reward tiers in total, and the tier you can claim depends on your research progress.
<color=#FFFF00>Solo Point Ranking Rewards</color>: Members from both guilds are ranked daily based on their solo points. Rewards are distributed according to your ranking, but you must reach the solo point goal to be eligible for ranking rewards.
2. After the duel ends, both sides will win rewards. The winning guild will get Victory Rewards, while the losing guild will get Consolation Rewards.
3. Rewards will be automatically distributed after the Guild Duel is finalized. You must be in the <color=#FFFF00>guild you started the event in</color> to receive your rewards.
4. Solo Point Tier rewards must be claimed manually. Any unclaimed rewards will be sent automatically at the end of each day. All other rewards are sent directly to your Bag, and you'll get a mail notification when they arrive.

<color=#FFC861>Registration & Matching</color>
1. <color=#FFFF00>Guilds</color> must register to participate in this event. Only guilds with at least <color=#FFFF00>100 Cups</color> in the Dragon Arena and <color=#FFFF00>30 guild members with Castle Lv 17 and above</color> before registration ends are eligible.
2. Rank 4 and above members can register their guild and choose up to 3 preferred time slots for the event. These time slots can be changed at any time during the registration phase.
3. After registration, Rank 4 and above members can set the participant list.
4. If more than 2,048 guilds register, only the top 2,048 guilds, based on their Dragon Arena Cup rankings, will qualify to compete.

3719:
Congratulations! You got a Novice Relocator!

A Novice Relocator moves your turf to any desired location!

This item can only be used within three days of joining this game. Your Castle must also be below Lv 5.

How to use:
1. Reach Castle Lv 4, tap the lower left button to open Kingdom Map, then tap an empty tile and select Relocate.
2. To relocate to another Kingdom, tap the globe icon to open World Map. Select the desired Kingdom and tap an empty tile to move.

Here begins your new legacy!
3721:
It's time to boost your military might!

Unlock Tier 2 Troops when you upgrade Academy to Lv 9!

Maximize your strength with Troops training and Research now!
3723:
Did you know that Leaders can be captured?

Clear [Kingdom Chapter 5: Mount Gloria] to unlock the Prison. You will then be able to capture enemy Leaders from Lv 10+ Castles. But beware: your Leaders also stand a risk of being captured by enemies with Prisons.

Leader boosts that will still be active while your Leader is captured include Talents, Equipment, and Hero's Battle Skills.

The fates of your Leaders lie in the strength of your Troops and Heroes!
3725:
Have you heard the myth of the Sacred Soil?

It's real! A land of never ending Gems!

Progress through [Kingdom Chapter 8: Sacred Soil] to unlock the Treasure Trove.

Tier 3 Troops will also unlock when your Academy reaches Lv 17.
3727:
Want to know more about executions?

When you execute an enemy Leader from a Lv 17 or higher Castle, the Altar will grant a temporary stat boost.

You'll need to clear [Kingdom Chapter 8: Sacred Soil] to unlock the Altar.

An executed leader will be revived after 7 days. Revival Fruits grant instant revival.
3729:
We've been attacked!

Upgrade the Infirmary to heal more wounded soldiers.
Upgrade your Vault to protect more resources.
Use a Shield Turf Boost.
Deploy Allied Troops from the Embassy.
Train more soldiers.
These are some ways to protect your turf.

Good luck.
3731:
Guildmates are invaluable!

Guild benefits:
1. [Help] for reducing Build and Research times.
2. [Guild Gifts] from guildmates completing Monster Hunts or buying Special Bundles from the Mall.
3. [Guild Quests] that grant materials, resources, Player EXP, and Guild Coins.
4. Unique items at the [Guild Shop].
5. [Supplies] from your Trading Post.
6. [Allied Troops] in your Embassy to help defend your turf.
7. [Start a Rally] at your Battle Hall. You can still join Rallies if you don't have a Battle Hall.
8. Protect each other by Garrisoning troops at a guildmate's Turf.

There's strength in numbers!
3733:
Have you joined a guild yet?

If you want to play with your guildmates, make sure that you're all in the same Kingdom! If not, you can use the [Novice Relocator] to move to where they are.

If you aren't in a guild, try to join one ASAP! Guilds will really help you on your journey!

Keep in mind that a [Novice Relocator] can only be used if your Castle is Lv5 and below. You must also use it within three days after creating your account!

4070: Quarries affect Stone production rate and storage. Production stops when storage is full. Build more or upgrade to increase efficiency.
4071: Mines affect Ore production rate and storage. Production stops when storage is full. Build more or upgrade to increase efficiency.
4072: Farms affect Food production rate and storage. Production stops when storage is full. Build more or upgrade to increase efficiency.

4075:
The Infirmary heals your injured troops! All troops injured while defending, gathering, occupying, or reinforcing and <color=#00FFFF> 60% of troops injured in Skirmishes and outside of your Turf (attacking, rallying, War for Wonders) will be sent to the Infirmary. </color>
When the Infirmary is full, newly Wounded troops at a higher tier than those currently in the Infirmary will be given priority.
4076: The Castle is your most important building. Upgrade to unlock new buildings and features, increase the max level of other buildings, and number of Soldiers you can add to your Army.
4077: The Vault protects resources from Raids. Upgrade to increase max resource storage.
4078: The Academy lets you research stat boosting Technologies. Upgrade to reduce research time.
4079: Lets you start or join Rallies. Coalition leader's Battle Hall level determines the max number of Soldiers in the Coalition Army. <color=#00FFFF> (Soldiers added to a Rally inherit the Coalition leader's attributes, including Battle Skills, Technologies, Boosts, Talents, Equipment, Wonder Stat Boosts, and Altar Boosts.)</color>
4080: Castle Walls prevent enemy troops from entering your Turf. Deploy Heroes and Traps on your Castle Walls. Damaged Walls regenerate over time. Upgrade to increase the Wall's HP, recovery rate, and max Trap capacity.
4081: Watchtower notifies you when enemy troops, Scouts, Supplies, or Allied Troops enter your Turf, Camp, or the Turf you have garrisoned troops at. Upgrade to view more details on the incoming troops. (Adventure Quest details are not restricted by Watchtower Lv.)
4082: The Embassy lets you deploy or hold Allied Troops. Upgrade to increase the amount of Allied Troops you can hold.
4083: The Workshop lets you forge Equipment and fuse Materials to strengthen your Heroes.
4084: The Treasure Trove lets you deposit Gems to earn more over time. Deposit more Gems for higher returns. Upgrade to increase the max deposit and interest rate. Payouts will be in Linked Gems, regardless of whether the deposit is canceled or completed.
4085: The Trading Post lets you and your Allies share resources. Upgrade to increase the amount of resources you can send and decrease your supply tax.
4086: The Prison lets you capture enemy Leaders with Lv 10+ Castles. Upgrade to Lv 17 to Execute leaders with Lv 17+ Castles. Execution wait times will vary according to the Castle Level of the prisoners. Upgrade to reduce wait time. <color=#00FFFF>(Upgrade to Lv 25 for a Capture boost).</color>
4087: The Altar grants temporary stat boosts when you Execute captured Leaders. Upgrade to improve boost effects. <color=#00FFFF> (Executing a Leader grants a 24h boost, Execute more leaders to extend boost duration.) </color>

5914:
You're the new Lord, aren't you?
Allow me to introduce myself. I'm the Oath Keeper, at your service!
5915: Always the first on the battlefield, I'm a loyal companion!
5917: Hey! Why is he here? This is MY stage!
5918: Hah! Did you really think I would be content with just a walk-on role?
5919: Hmph! Well, I'm clearly the star of the show...
5920: Coming soon! Check back in a bit!
5921: And I'm your only audience. Why don't you take center stage if you're so great?
5922: Finally! Now I can show everyone what it means to be the lead!
5923: Please, a round of applause if you will!
5925: Huh, so that's how it is...
5926: Well, they say silence is golden, so you must be rich...
5927: Everyone says I'm more handsome in person! What do you think?
5928: I knew it! We were made for each other!
5929: Hah! I'm sure you'll find out how great I am!
5930: I know you love me. It's too much for words.
5931: You know he's all brawn but no brain, right?
5932: STR over INT any day, knight! Knowledge is important, but big arms are more importanter.
5933: But I'm sure you don't want to hear about my exploits? I've won many battles, unlike some Heroes...
5934: You're the first Lord I've met who's willing to listen to me! I'm touched!
5935: Well, the download isn't ready yet, so why don't you listen to my story...
5936: Silence? I'll take that as you're listening.
5937: Ahem! The Wesley family line can be traced back... to a very long, long, long time ago...
5938: A dark age when the Black Dragon reigned. A brave warrior slew the beast with his spear, freeing the world from its terror...
5939: Are you kidding me? Who would want to listen to such a dull story?
5940: Why don't you have a go then?
5941: Me? I'm a stunner. Just a view of my back is enough to outshine you on stage.
5942: That's just an excuse for your stage fright! Do you want a better view of Death Knight?
5943: You should have said so sooner!
5944: I guess you accidentally picked the wrong option! No matter. Be more careful next time.
5945: Is it that hard to decide? Well, let me make the choice instead.
5946: I know you've taken such an interest in me, so I swear I won't disappoint.
5947: After all that, there's no difference between your back and front views! Where is your face?
5948: Well, my character description does say that I'm undead, so...
5949: I think we can all agree that my appearance is much more appealing.
5950: Your looks are nothing compared to my power.
5951: Why don't we hold a vote? Let's see who's more popular!
5953: How about someone who speaks for the Gods - Lady Bellena!
5954: Thanks for waiting! Sorry I'm late. There was a traffic jam.
5955: Well, we're almost done with the installation! Let's wait a little longer.
5957: I'm here to see our new Lord. Hmm, the bar isn't full yet. Allow me to entertain you until we're ready.
5958: Oh, have you fallen asleep? We can start the game soon...
5959: After we enter the game, we'll pretend this prattle never happened. Do keep that in mind...
596:
Leaving battle results in defeat.
Are you sure?
5960: Once the bar is full, we'll return to our normal behavior and join you on an exciting adventure!
5961: So anyway, why have you invited me on stage?
5962: Death Archer, settle this for us! Who's more popular, me or this jamoca Joe?
5963: I won't lose to you, muscle head!
5964: Impossible! You chose that block of ice?
5965: Why did you choose that bean bag? Is there something wrong with your eyes?
5966: Thanks for giving it the old college try...
5967: The result is in. The winner is... me!
5968: How can you be the winner? You weren't even in the poll!
5969: It was too close to call, so neither of you won. I'm sure you'll agree I'm the prettiest here!
5970: I'm sure everyone's just too busy staring at the progress bar and missed the poll!
5971: Well, even if I'm not the winner, surely I'm the first runner-up?
5973: What? No way! I demand a recount!
5974: According to my investigations, most people just go with the first option.
5976: Hahaha! I knew I'd win! Good thing I like to go first!
5977: Hmph. It's not like you actually won anything.
5978: Oh, man. I wish I made you wager your turf!
5979: Ahem! The bar below is almost full. It's time to end this tomfoolery...
5980: Well, we must leave! See you later! Hope you enjoy the game!
5981: You'll experience such an exhilarating adventure. You'll keep coming back for more!
5982: See you in-game! Please forget everything we've said here!
5983: Huh? Why are you still here?
5984: Uhh, Oath Keeper, Death Knight. Maybe our player needs some battle cries for motivation.
5985: ATTEN-TION! Our land has fallen into the hands of the Death Knight. We must defeat him!
5986: Do not deny the inevitable! Embrace your death!
5993: My blade and shield will keep you safe!
5995: ...Even as an Oath Keeper, this is really testing my loyalty and patience.
5996: No! Nothing will stop me! Curse this bar!
5997: Hey hey hey, eyes on me bud.
5998: Haven't I improvised enough? My throat's gone dry from all this nonsense...

7253: Wonders consist of 2 types: Bases and Forts. In each Kingdom, there is one Base and six Forts. Forts grant Wonder Stat Boost to the Guild that owns them.
7254: <color=#FF0000>Battle Phase:</color> During this time, Guilds will be able to seize Forts. The Guild that occupies the Fort until Occupation Time ends will become its owner. Occupation Time may vary when a Wonder is seized by a new owner. This will be determined by the current duration of Battle Phase. After 120 hours (from the time the Base enters Protection Phase), all Wonders will enter Battle Phase. (Troops killed in Wonder battles will not enter the Sanctuary.) <color=#00FFFF>If server maintenance lasts for 30 minutes or longer when Battle Phase has exceeded 2 hours, the Occupation Time countdown after the maintenance will be based on the "Over 1 hour" duration</color>.
7255: <color=#00FF00>Protection Phase:</color> During this time, Forts can't be seized.
7256: <color=#FFFF00>Wonder Stat Boost:</color> Guild members will receive the relevant Wonder Stat Boost from the Forts owned or occupied.<color=#FF206D>(boosts from the similar wonders will not stack)</color>

8365: Frostwing always aims for the Hero with the highest Max HP. Its icy attacks cover a large area, causing any Hero standing behind the target to take huge damage. Use Heroes with skills that allow them to take cover behind Frostwing!
8366: Gargantua's skill can deal massive damage to its target and the Heroes nearby. You may need more Heroes that can recover HP to survive. It also has very high PDEF, so bring your Heroes with skills that deal Magic DMG to tackle this one!
8367: The Snow Beast always targets the Hero furthest from itself. It charges at the target with its enormous body and also causes all Heroes hit to take damage over time. Use Heroes that can deal high damage against this fearsome beast!
8368: The searing flames of the Jade Wyrm will reduce its target's DEF to zero, thus all Heroes hit by it have to battle with no DEF. Use Heroes with high HP to survive the Jade Wyrm’s wrath!
8369: Gryphon's ultimate skill targets all Heroes on the field, so beware. However its attack can be disrupted if it is inflicted with conditions. Disrupt Gryphon’s attack and prevent annihilation!
837: You'll randomly get one of the items below
8372: The Mega Maggot's mighty tail packs a massive sting! Its venom deals continuous damage, so remember to heal your heroes. Use skills that deal Magic DMG to bring it down!
8373: Terrorthorn lashes its vine whips at Heroes, rendering them immobilized. It also blasts nearby Heroes with seeds, knocking them back. Survive its onslaught to kill this fearsome monster!
8374: The ruthless Hell Drider will attack the Hero with the highest max HP, dealing damage to other Heroes near its target as well. It is also protected by an energy shield, so send your strongest Heroes to destroy it!
8375: The Noceros is a tough creature! Its thick hide can withstand Physical DMG and its crystal horns have the ability to lower your Heroes' Healing Focus. Use skills that deal Magic DMG to defeat it!
8376: The Grim Reaper will harvest the soul of the Hero with the highest Max HP. Use your skills to deal Physical DMG to it before it can claim more victims!
8377: Powerful and unpredictable, the Saberfang will wreak havoc among your ranks. Use magic to bring it down before it decimates your troops!
8378: Made entirely of stone, the Hardrox can shrug off all physical blows. The only way to defeat it is with magic. Get close and cast away!
8379: A fearsome phantom that escaped from the depths of the netherworld. Kill it and take its candy!
8380: The Tidal Titan brings the wrath of the sea down on an enemy hero, dealing massive damage. Its thick skin also allows it to shrug off physical attacks. Bring healers and heroes with powerful magic to take it down!
8381: This fallen angel is immune to all ailments, strikes at long range, and possesses the strange power to turn her nearby opponents into food. Take her down with physical attacks before your heroes become her next meal!
8382: If provoked, the Queen Bee will jab the first target she sees! This matriarch also summons a horde of buzzing bees to drive off invaders. Bring her down with Physical Attacks before you get overwhelmed!
8383: The Blackwing constantly hungers for blood, and will always lock on to the healthiest target in sight! Its venomous fangs nullifies all Life Leech effects, and its thick hide protects it from the strongest magical attacks. Take it down with brute physical force!
8384: The Mecha Trojan will not leave your Heroes alone! Watch out for its relentless attacks that also stun its targets. Keep your heroes alive and chop it down with magical power!
8385: The Serpent Gladiator will deal massive damage to its target and nearby Heroes. Keep your party alive with constant healing, and use magic-users to overcome this warrior!
8386: The Necrosis deals massive damage to its targets and summons underlings to attack Heroes. It can easily shrug off physical blows, so use magic to send it back to its grave!
8387: These naughty Goblins are stealing all the gold! Take them down and retrieve the gold!
8388: The Evil Weevil carries a store of food on its back, like a miniature food cart! Defeat it to take its food!
8389: The Totempest is a totem brought to life by a mysterious enchantment. It spits Timber at all who dare approach it.
8390: Bouldurs are skilled masons that build forts wherever they go. They're also territorial and prone to throwing heavy stones at their enemies.
8391: Krabbies have a penchant for collecting shiny things, and are especially fond of ore. Hunt them down to get their stash!
8392: The Voodoo Shaman is full of tricks! This rogue exudes a debilitating aura, and has the ability to petrify foes with its strange magic. Take it down with physical attacks before your heroes succumb to its sinister spells!
8393: This winged creature was once an angel, but succumbed to the sin of gluttony. Defeat her before you end up as her next meal!
8394: The Hootclaw is a horrifying beast that targets the hero with the most HP remaining. Its massive wingspan lets it attack from long range. Bring healers and magic-users to ruffle its feathers!
8395: The Cottageroar will deal massive damage to its target and nearby Heroes. Bring healers and Heroes with high MDEF to help keep your party alive, then use physical attacks to demolish this monster!
8398: The Gemming Gremlin's sack is filled with treasures from the Labyrinth! Strike it down and claim it for your own!
8399: Don't stand pat, Trickstar's arrows are not made of sponge! Take it down and grab its stash of Holy Stars!
84: Your Bag is too full for this!

9178:
Who dares enter the perilous depths of the Labyrinth? Foul beasts lurk in the shadows, and treasure may be found in every corner! Dare you take the plunge?

<color=#FFC861>Holy Stars</color>
Only the light of a Holy Star can hurt the creatures of the Labyrinth. You'll only get one free hit per day, so make sure you have plenty in store.

<color=#FFC861>Difficulty</color>
<color=#FFEE9E>Normal:</color> Each attack requires 100 Holy Stars, You will get a reward after every attack.
<color=#FFEE9E>Elite:</color> Each attack requires 1,000 Holy Stars, You will get a huge reward after every attack!
(Unlock Elite difficulty by purchasing special packages that contain [Labyrinth (Elite) Access].)

<color=#FFC861>Special Events</color>
<color=#FFEE9E>Divine Blessing:</color> The Goddess Athena might bless your heroes with her power! When the blessing is active, you will not need Holy Stars to attack the creatures of the Labyrinth
<color=#FFEE9E>The Guardians:</color> In your journey through the Labyrinth, you might chance upon one of the Guardians. Don't let it get away! Kill it and take its treasure!

<color=#FFC861>Auto Attack</color>
1.You can tap <color=#FFEE9E>Auto Use</color> to auto attack after using your free hit. This will allow you to choose how many Holy Stars you want to use to automatically continue attacking.
2.If you encounter the daily reset while auto attacking, your free hit will automatically be used.
3.If you encounter a new beast while auto attacking, you will continue attacking it. Please note that the prizes that each beast drops may vary.

9355:
A Guild Leader who seizes the Base will either become the Overlord or Conqueror.
Overlord: If the Guild Leader is from the same Kingdom as the Base, they will gain the title of Overlord and all its benefits.
Conqueror: If the Guild Leader is from a different Kingdom from the Base, they'll gain the title of Conqueror and the Kingdom will lose its benefits.
9356: <color=#FF0000>Battle Phase:</color> During this time, Guilds will be able to seize the Base. The Guild that occupies the Base until Occupation Time ends will become its owner. Occupation Time may vary when a Wonder is seized by a new owner. This will be determined by the current duration of Battle Phase. After 120 hours from the Base's Protection Phase, all Wonders will enter Battle Phase. (Troops killed in Wonder battles will not enter the Sanctuary.) <color=#00FFFF>If server maintenance lasts for 30 minutes or longer when Battle Phase has exceeded 2 hours, the Occupation Time countdown after the maintenance will be based on the "Over 1 hour" duration</color>.
9357: <color=#00FF00>Protection Phase: </color>During this time, Base can't be seized.

9367:
The Overlord can assign a vassal title to any player within the Kingdom. When another player becomes the Overlord, the titles will reset.
Titles can be assigned by the following methods.
<color=#F5D94F>Kingdom Map</color>: Tap a player's Castle followed by the [Assign] button.
<color=#F5D94F>Members</color>: Tap Manage followed by the [Assign Title] button.
<color=#F5D94F>Player Interface</color>: Tap the Gear button followed by the [Assign Title] button.
9374: <color=#ffc961>Kingdom vs Kingdom: </color>During KvK, Wonders will not enter the Protection Phase and all invaders can attack the Wonder. After KvK, the leader of the guild that controls the Wonder will become its owner, and the Wonder will enter Protection Phase for 5-6 days. If no one controls the Wonder, it will enter Battle Phase.

9768:
1. You can garrison troops in your guildmate's Turf.
2. You can only accommodate 1 guildmate's troops at a time
3. The strength of the garrisoned troops will be determined by your guildmate's Army Boosts.
4. In an attack, your guildmate's troops will attempt to stop the enemy outside the Turf. The enemy will not enter your Turf unless they fail.
5. Some Familiar skills are able to bypass the garrisoned troops and engage the Turf directly.

9921:
<color=#FFEE9E>Relieving: </color>
You can send an army with a Hero to relieve the troops stationed in a Wonder. The former Commander must withdraw from the Wonder before the Relief Army arrives.<color=#FF6278>(The Royal Wonder cannot be relieved)</color>
<color=#FFEE9E>Reinforcing: </color>
Send reinforcements to the Wonder to bolster its defenses
<color=#FFEE9E>Withdrawing: </color>
Your guild will retain possession of the Wonder if you withdraw the stationed troops. Occupation Time will continue until another guild takes over the Wonder.

9939:
Unlocked at Castle Lv 9. Activate <color=#00FF00>[Battle Fury]</color> through military actions.
These include:<color=#FFFF00>scouting, reinforcing, stationing, attacking, rallies, relieving the commander, etc</color>. Targets can be Turfs, Camps, Resource Tiles, or Wonders.
Status duration increases with Castle Level, and the effects can be boosted through research and Familiar skills. You can't <color=#FFFF00>use Shields or move to other Kingdoms (Migrate or KvK)</color> while this status is active.

9995:
In the [Battle Royal], elite players from every kingdom will come to the Royal Battleground and, together with their guilds, fight to be crowned the [Emperor]!
<color=#FF6278>(The Emperor will enjoy some VERY special privileges during their reign; effective until the next [Battle Royal])</color>
9996: <color=#FF0000>Battle Phase:</color> During this time, players will be able to fight for control of the Royal Wonder. The commander who occupies it for the longest time will be crowned the Emperor. The Wonder will be owned by the rally commander instead of the guild leader. (Troops killed in Wonder battles will not enter the Sanctuary.)
9997: <color=#00FF00>Protection Phase:</color> During this time, the Royal Wonder can't be attacked
